Options: (a) polish the existing 2 generic Team+Event templates; (b) add a small curated set (e.g. per major sport, or 'minimal' vs 'rich' presets); (c) ship as importable presets vs auto-seeded. Decide count, naming, and whether team templates are included given the yk4j de-emphasis direction. Blocks the rest.
design:
PROPOSED DEFAULT TEMPLATE SET (from sethwv gist 6eca997 + existing docs examples), 2026-06-14.

Source: sethwv ships 6 best-practice templates, ALL unscoped (sport=null, league=null) — seeded UNASSIGNED, user assigns. They use <game-thumbs-base-url> placeholders -> under z02s these become RELATIVE art paths (leading slash) + the user's base URL setting (also fixes the broken localhost:3000 in current seeds = tvnk.2).

THE SET (1 team + 5 event):
1. Default Team (team) — universal team fallback. title '{league} {sport}'.
2. Default Event (event) — universal event fallback. chan '{league} | {away_team_abbrev} / {home_team_abbrev} ({exception_keyword})'.
3. Combat Event (event) — MMA/boxing. title '{league} {event_number} - {card_segment_display}'; uses card_segment_display + event_number; home/away swapped.
4. International Event (event) — title '{gracenote_category}'; international naming.
5. No-Abbrev Event (event) — leagues w/o abbreviations; chan uses full '{away_team} / {home_team}'.
6. MiLB Event (event) — MiLB-specific naming 'MiLB | {away_team_abbrev}...'.

RECOMMENDED SCOPING (guidance; templates ship UNASSIGNED per maintainer):
- Default Team -> global team default.
- Default Event -> global event default (works for most US pro leagues w/ abbrevs).
- Combat Event -> assign to MMA/boxing (UFC, PFL, boxing).
- International Event -> assign to soccer + international comps.
- No-Abbrev Event -> assign to leagues lacking abbrevs (niche soccer, etc.).
- MiLB Event -> assign to MiLB.

OPEN DECISIONS for maintainer:
(a) Replace the current 2 generic seeds (Team/Event) with these 6, or add alongside?
(b) Ship art as RELATIVE paths (z02s) — confirm leading-slash + the style/logo/fallback query params from the gist.
(c) Seed on fresh install only (guard: no existing templates), unassigned.
(d) Surface 'recommended scoping' as docs, in-UI hint, or both.

title: Gracenote-modeled best-in-class default templates
description: ELEVATED (2026-06-14, maintainer): not just 'ship seth's set' — produce best-in-class default templates MODELED ON AUTHORITATIVE GRACENOTE EPG DATA, tested + hardened + compared to real Gracenote output across multiple sports. Methodology: Gracenote = target (maintainer will PROVIDE real Gracenote samples); Jesmann EPG probed from Dispatcharr = current-best real-world approximation ('close but not quite'); the DELTA between them defines what best-in-class needs. Inputs analyzed: user's 2 DB templates (gracenote_category titles + descriptions — already more Gracenote-aligned than seth's {league} {sport}), seth's gist 6 (Default Team/Event + Combat/International/No-Abbrev/MiLB variants, richer channel naming), Teamarr's 207 vars. CONFIRMED GAP: no article-aware team naming + no national-team distinction (Team model = name/short_name/abbreviation only) -> 'The Detroit Pistons' vs 'Netherlands' (national teams omit the article) is impossible today; needs new vars. Subscriptions span: NBA/NFL/MLB/NHL, NCAA (FB/BB/hockey/baseball), MiLB (all), UFC/boxing, WNBA, + hundreds of soccer leagues (domestic + international national teams). Ship seeded UNASSIGNED w/ recommended scoping; relative art paths (z02s).

title: Authoring workflow redesign (guided flow + preview placement)
description: Maintainer chose 'fold workflow into the sweep' (yk4j.1) — go beyond visual consistency and rethink the tab-by-tab authoring FLOW itself. Scope: guided/logical step order, where the live preview lives relative to the form (inline vs sidebar), reducing friction in the create→fill→preview→save loop, and how the 5 tabs (Basics/Defaults/Conditions/Fillers/EPG Options) sequence for a first-time author. Design-led; produces the flow the other children implement against. Depends on .1 (decisions) and .2 (decompose).

title: De-emphasize team template creation in the builder
description: DECISION (yk4j.1, locked 2026-06-14): 'Demote to secondary link'. Remove the two equal Event/Team cards in the create gate (TemplateForm.tsx:331-377). Create flow leads straight into an Event template form (template_type already defaults to 'event', line 78). Team creation becomes a small secondary link ('Need a team template instead? ›') rather than a co-equal choice. Team capability fully retained. Per vhbo Teams-demotion direction.
close_reason: Team de-emphasis shipped per locked decision. Removed the 2-card 'Step 1: Choose Template Type' gate + typeConfirmed state; create flow now opens directly on an Event template (template_type defaults to event). Team is a secondary switch link ('Need a team template instead? ->' / 'Switch to event template ->'), styled noticeable-but-secondary (bordered, primary text, arrow) per maintainer feedback. Switching guards activeTab off the team-only Conditions tab. Also removed redundant banner subtitles + de-blued type badge/banner (semantic tokens). Verified live via Playwright: create opens Event; switch toggles to Team (User icon, Conditions tab appears) and back. tsc+build green, no new lint.

title: Audit template variable scope gating (ALL/TEAM_ONLY/EVENT_ONLY)
description: Every variable in teamarr/templates/variables/*.py is tagged scope=TemplateScope.ALL/TEAM_ONLY/EVENT_ONLY, which gates the picker via GET /variables?template_type=. Audit all 207 variables for correct tagging: confirm event-only vars (game/event/odds context) aren't offered in team templates and vice-versa, and that genuinely shared vars are ALL. Wrong gating either hides usable vars or offers ones that never resolve. Output: corrected scope tags + a test asserting the per-type variable sets. Touches templates/variables/ docstrings if any var's availability changes (see CLAUDE.md docs table).
close_reason: Scope gating audited end-to-end and found ESSENTIALLY CORRECT — no retags warranted. The subagent audit flagged ~16 'team-perspective vars offered to events that resolve empty', but its core PREMISE was wrong: event templates build context in HOME-TEAM PERSPECTIVE (event_epg.py:195 team_id=event.home_team.id; context_builder.build_for_event fetches team_stats for that team + opponent_stats for the away team). So home_record/away_record/home_win_pct/away_win_pct (read ctx.team_stats = home team's splits) and the positional home_team_*/away_team_* families DO resolve in event templates. Verified in code; reverted an initial (wrong) TEAM_ONLY retag after maintainer correctly pointed out home/away team data resolves in event context. End-to-end gating IS wired (GET /variables?template_type= -> filter_by_template_type; TemplateForm passes type). Dead VariablePicker.tsx (zero importers) is ungated but harmless. One real non-scope bug spun out: 'matchup' name collision (combat MMA variant shadowed by identity). Net: leave scopes as-is.

title: Live gamethumb/image render in preview (verify the generated link works)
description: createResolver currently does pure string substitution — image variables (gamethumb, logos, artwork) render only as a URL string, so users can't tell if the generated link actually resolves. Add live image rendering to the preview: when the resolved template yields an image URL (gamethumb especially), actually fetch & render the so the user sees the real artwork, with an explicit broken/404/empty state when the link doesn't resolve. This is the highest-signal preview validation — it proves the template produces a working thumbnail end-to-end.
close_reason: Added live image rendering to the preview. New ImagePreview in TemplateField fetches+renders the resolved URL as an with loading (spinner) + broken-link ('Image didn't load — check the URL resolves') states. Wired via isImageField on the 5 art/image fields: program_art_url, event_channel_logo_url, pregame/postgame/idle art_url. Only renders for absolute http(s) URLs (relative paths await the art base URL feature z02s). Verified live via Playwright: valid URLs load (naturalWidth>0), an invalid URL shows the broken-link message. This is the highest-signal preview validation — proves a template produces a working thumbnail end-to-end. tsc+build+lint green.

title: Refresh & validate preview sample data
description: The live preview resolves variables from fetchSamples(previewSport) with a hardcoded DEFAULT_SAMPLE_DATA fallback (TemplateForm.tsx:103-116). Audit the backend samples source and DEFAULT_SAMPLE_DATA: ensure every current template variable has a realistic, up-to-date sample value (no stale teams/dates/missing new vars), across all preview sports. Goal: the preview reflects what users will actually get, including newer variables.
close_reason: Audited SAMPLE_DATA vs registry: 202 base vars, 10 had NO sample (feed_team/feed_team_short/feed_team_abbrev/_lower/feed_team_logo/is_home_feed/is_away_feed/feed_home_away/broadcast_feed/broadcast_feed_team — the event-only feed-separation family) so the preview showed raw {name}. Added realistic per-sport samples (models channel carrying the HOME team's feed; mirrors home_team values, is_home_feed=true). All 202 now covered; get_all_sample_data resolves for every AVAILABLE_SPORT (fallback verified). Added tests/test_sample_data_coverage.py (2 tests) as a regression guard. ruff + tests green. Unblocks .6 (live gamethumb render).

title: Variable picker UX modernization
description: VariablePicker (298 lines) predates the IA work. Improve discoverability and ergonomics: searchable, scope-filtered (TEAM_ONLY/EVENT_ONLY/ALL via template_type — already supported by GET /variables?template_type=), grouped by category, with clear insert affordance and (ideally) inline descriptions/examples per variable. Align styling with the rest of the app.
notes: ALSO fold in (from .12 eval): VariableSidebar 2x <details>/<summary> (Recently Used + Categories) -> CollapsibleSection primitive (has persistKey/icon/count/actions). These live on the sidebar = .4's surface.

title: Visual consistency + apply shared primitives
description: Bring the builder in line with the post-vhbo app: text-2xl → text-xl headings, emoji → Lucide icons, consistent spacing rhythm, and adopt the mf7v shared primitives (FormField for label+control+help+error, CollapsibleSection, EmptyState). Consumes mf7v work. Per the locked IA decisions from the anchor bead.
close_reason: Visual consistency pass complete + verified live. Converted ALL emoji to Lucide across template-form (tab icons: ClipboardList/Pencil/Target/Calendar/Settings; type badges+banners+cards: Tv/User; card titles: FolderOpen/Clapperboard/Tag/Tv/Clock/Moon; conditional labels: Check/Hourglass; sidebar: FileText/Clock). Headings text-2xl->text-xl. Tabs relabeled 'Basics'/'EPG Options'; single 'Create Template' title. Existing primitives (CollapsibleSection) available; FormField/EmptyState don't exist yet (mf7v.4 / 1kt3.9) so deferred — see follow-up note. Verified via Playwright on real Team+Event templates (Basics+Fillers tabs render all Lucide, no emoji). tsc exit 0, build green.

title: Decompose TemplateForm god component (2478 lines)
description: TemplateForm.tsx is 2478 lines. Extract each tab into its own component (BasicTab, DefaultsTab, ConditionsTab, FillersTab, XmltvTab) and pull shared state/helpers (createResolver, sample fetching) into hooks, so the page becomes a thin shell. Pure refactor — no behavior change — to make the visual/primitive pass tractable. Overlaps 5hq code-health but scoped here as the structural prerequisite for the sweep.
close_reason: Decomposed TemplateForm.tsx 2478 -> 431-line shell + template-form/ module: types.ts, constants.ts, VariableSidebar.tsx (330), TemplateField.tsx (93), CategoryEditor.tsx (97), tabs/{Basic94,Defaults288,Conditions454,Fillers476,Xmltv146}. Pure move-and-rewire, no behavior/visual change (emoji+copy preserved for .3). Verified independently: tsc --noEmit exit 0, npm run build OK, zero duplicate defs, shell imports all 5 tabs+sidebar. Unblocks .3 and .10.

title: Audit builder & lock IA decisions (anchor)
description: Walk the template builder end-to-end (Templates list → create/edit → 5 tabs basic/defaults/conditions/fillers/xmltv → VariablePicker → live preview → save/assign) and lock IA/terminology decisions with the maintainer, the way vhbo did per-tab: heading hierarchy (standardize on text-xl like the rest of the app — builder currently uses text-2xl), emoji 📺/👤 → Lucide icons, tab naming & order, what content belongs on which tab, and any consolidation. Output: a decisions list that the implementation children consume. Blocks the rest of the epic.
design:
LOCKED IA DECISIONS (maintainer, 2026-06-14) — implementation children consume these:

VISUAL/CONSISTENCY (apply in .3):
- Emoji -> Lucide icons everywhere: tab icons (📋✏️🎯📅⚙️), type indicators (📺/👤), card titles (📂/🎬), type badges. Pick semantic Lucide equivalents.
- Headings text-2xl -> text-xl (match post-vhbo app standard).
- Consolidate inconsistent titles: 'Create New Template' + 'Create Template' -> single 'Create Template'.
- Adopt mf7v primitives (FormField, CollapsibleSection, EmptyState).

TAB NAMING (broader relabel):
- Final set: 'Basics · Defaults · Conditions · Fillers · EPG Options'
  (Basic Info->Basics; Other EPG Options->EPG Options; Defaults/Conditions/Fillers unchanged.)
- Conditions stays team-only (hidden for event templates) — existing behavior, keep.

TEAM DE-EMPHASIS (drives .9 — DECISION: 'Demote to secondary link'):
- Remove the two-card 'Step 1: Choose Template Type' picker.
- Create flow leads STRAIGHT into an Event template form (template_type defaults to event).
- Team creation becomes a small secondary affordance: 'Need a team template instead? ›' link.
- Team capability fully retained, just not the default path.

SCOPE (maintainer chose 'Fold workflow into the sweep'):
- Not just visual — also rethink the tab-by-tab authoring FLOW holistically: guided order, inline/preview placement, reduce friction. Tracked as new child .10.
close_reason: Audit complete + IA decisions locked with maintainer 2026-06-14 (recorded in design field). Key calls: emoji->Lucide + text-xl + mf7v primitives; tabs relabeled to 'Basics/Defaults/Conditions/Fillers/EPG Options'; team templates demoted to a secondary link (no more 2-card picker); scope expanded to a holistic authoring-flow redesign (new child .10). Unblocks .2-.10.

title: Template builder IA/UI/UX sweep
description: The v2.7.0 IA overhaul (vhbo) swept the main app tab-by-tab but never reached the Template builder. It's now inconsistent and overgrown: TemplateForm.tsx is a 2478-line god component (5 tabs: basic/defaults/conditions/fillers/xmltv), uses emoji (📺/👤) instead of the app's Lucide iconography, headings are text-2xl while the rest of the app standardized on text-xl post-vhbo, and it never received the mf7v shared primitives (FormField/CollapsibleSection/EmptyState) or the 1kt3 polish. The core authoring interactions — VariablePicker (298 lines), live preview, and templateValidation surfacing — also predate the IA work. This epic does for the builder what vhbo did for the rest of the app: lock IA decisions with the maintainer, decompose the god component, apply shared primitives + visual consistency, and modernize the variable-picker/preview/validation UX. Surfaces: pages/TemplateForm.tsx, pages/Templates.tsx, components/VariablePicker.tsx, components/TemplateAssignmentModal/, utils/templateValidation.ts. Related: vhbo (IA pattern), mf7v (primitives), 1kt3 (polish).

title: Surface CDVR refresh outcome in the run summary / UI
description: Users reporting 'CDVR broken' had zero in-app signal — channelsdvr_refresh / channelsdvr_epg_refresh results live only in logs. Surface the outcome (success / failed / skipped + reason) somewhere visible: generation run summary, Dashboard, and/or the Settings → Channels DVR test panel. Reuse the result fields already on the generation result object (generation.py:63-64).

title: Verify guide actually re-indexed after the PUT (fire-and-forget gap)
description: The guide-refresh PUT to /dvr/lineups/<id> returns 200 even when CDVR re-indexes nothing — so a 'success' result can still mean a stale guide. Add a post-PUT verification: poll CDVR /log (or /dvr guide stats) to confirm a Fetched+Indexed pair for the lineup, and distinguish 'refreshed & indexed' from 'refreshed, content unchanged (dedup, expected)' from 'no fetch happened (real failure)'. Promote the failure case to an explicit warning/result field rather than a silent 200=success.

title: CDVR integration — end-to-end test & hardening
description: Follow-up to the closed 1y7r CDVR epic. The widely-reported 'CDVR EPG isn't updating' bug was root-caused (generation.py step 5d silently skipped the guide PUT when no XMLTV lineup was set) and fixed on dev 2026-06-13 (client derives lineup_id='XMLTV-<source>'). This epic validates that fix end-to-end against a real CDVR and closes the remaining gaps that let the breakage go unnoticed: the guide-refresh PUT is fire-and-forget (returns 200 even when nothing re-indexes), and refresh outcomes are only logged — users had no UI signal. Real CDVR available at 192.168.0.10:8089 (v2026.05.18). See memory cdvr-integration-diagnosis. Unit coverage already solid (25 tests in tests/test_channelsdvr_client.py); the gaps here are e2e, verification, surfacing, and docs.

title: Migration path for existing username/password users
description:
Existing installs store username+password and must keep working — NO forced break.

Layers:
1. Non-breaking fallback: password auth keeps working indefinitely; if dispatcharr_api_key is empty, use the legacy flow. (Don't drop the password column — DB safety.)
2. One-click auto-mint (preferred, if Dispatcharr exposes a key-creation endpoint per kd5a.1): a button authenticates with the stored username/password, mints an API key via Dispatcharr, stores it, switches auth_method to api_key. Offer to clear the stored password after a successful test.
3. NUDGE = Dashboard banner using the Alert primitive (variant=info): declares the change ('Dispatcharr now supports API keys — more secure than storing your password') + a brief numbered step-by-step how-to (e.g. 1. In Dispatcharr, Settings > API Keys > generate; 2. Paste it here / or click Migrate to do it automatically; 3. Test connection). Dismissible + persisted (usePersistentCollapse / a dismissed flag) so it disappears once migrated or dismissed. Only show when still on password auth (dispatcharr_api_key empty AND username/password set).

Fallback if no programmatic endpoint (kd5a.1): manual migration only (paste a key from Dispatcharr's UI); the banner steps cover the manual path.

title: Settings UI: Dispatcharr API key field + test connection
description: Add an API key field to the Dispatcharr settings (Media Servers), mark it the recommended method, wire the existing connection test to validate via key, and de-emphasize/keep username+password as the legacy fallback. Mask the key like other secrets.

title: Backend: dispatcharr_api_key setting + client/TokenManager api-key path
description: Add dispatcharr_api_key to settings (schema + read/update/types). In the client/TokenManager: when api_key is set, inject the ApiKey header on every request and skip the JWT login/refresh; else keep the username/password flow. Connection test + ProviderRegistry.reinitialize handle the key. Backward compatible.

title: Verify Dispatcharr API-key mechanism (header, key generation, min version)
description: Confirm against Dispatcharr source/docs: (1) exact auth header (Authorization: ApiKey <key> vs X-API-Key); (2) how a user generates a key in the Dispatcharr UI; (3) whether there's a PROGRAMMATIC endpoint to create a key (needed for the one-click auto-mint migration); (4) which Dispatcharr version added ApiKeyAuthentication; (5) that the endpoints Teamarr uses (channels, m3u groups, epg) accept it. Gates the rest.

title: Dispatcharr API-key auth (replace stored password)
description:
Today Teamarr authenticates to Dispatcharr with username+password (TokenManager does a JWT login + refresh; the password is stored in settings, schema even notes 'consider encrypting'). Move to Dispatcharr API-key auth so Teamarr never stores the user's password — matching how the other integrations already work (TSDB/CricAPI/ChannelsDVR use keys), and keys are revocable in Dispatcharr.

Feasibility: Dispatcharr supports ApiKey auth (Authorization: ApiKey <key> or X-API-Key header) alongside JWT (per Dispatcharr docs) — VERIFY exact header + min version first (kd5a.1).

Design: keep backward compatibility — if dispatcharr_api_key is set, use it (send the ApiKey header on every request, skip JWT login/refresh); else fall back to the existing username/password flow. API key is the recommended method in the UI.

MIGRATION (kd5a.5) is a first-class requirement, not an afterthought: existing username/password installs MUST keep working untouched, with an optional one-click auto-mint (Teamarr uses the stored creds to mint a key via Dispatcharr) so users move over without manual steps.

title: Backend: rename user-facing 'event group' strings (progress/log/API messages)
description: Display/log strings shown to users: generation.py progress messages ('Loading event groups...', step labels), any API response messages, log lines that surface in the UI/logs. Keep function names (process_all_event_groups), table (event_epg_groups), docstrings can stay or update in the P3 sweep. Grep teamarr/ for quoted 'event group' in f-strings/messages.

title: Frontend: rename user-facing 'Event Group' strings to Stream Source/Source
description: Display strings only (NOT EventGroup type / event_group fields / routes). Known spots: GenerationContext.tsx:77 'Processing Event Groups' toast; RunHistoryTable.tsx 'Teams / Event Groups'; Settings.tsx labels + 'Event Groups Tab'; ChannelLifecycle.tsx help text; Templates.tsx delete warning; StreamOrderingManager.tsx 'Event Group' option label + 'select event group' + description; ChannelStreamPriority.tsx comment. Grep frontend/src for 'event group' (case-insensitive), skip identifiers.

title: Terminology: standardize Event Groups -> Stream Sources
description:
The v2.7.0 IA renamed 'Event Groups' to 'Stream Sources' (nav step 'Sources'), but user-facing copy across the app still says 'Event Group(s)' — e.g. the generation SSE toast 'Processing Event Groups', the Run History 'Teams / Event Groups' line, Settings labels, Channel Lifecycle help text, Stream Priority's 'Event Group' option, Templates delete warning, and many backend progress/log messages.

Standardize all USER-FACING terminology to 'Stream Source(s)' / 'Source(s)'.

IN SCOPE: display strings only — toasts, progress messages, UI labels, button/option labels, help/error text, and (lower priority) comments/docstrings.

OUT OF SCOPE (would require a migration / API break — leave as-is, possible separate epic): internal identifiers — the event_epg_groups table, EventGroup TS type, /groups API route, process_all_event_groups and other function/var names. Renaming those is risky and not user-visible.

Survey: ~14 frontend display strings; ~69 backend 'Event Group' occurrences (mostly comments/docstrings + a handful of progress messages).

title: [PR #236 + remote reqs] Standard regex syntax, Apply-to-Form saves, accent-inclusive team regex
description: Merged PR #236 (@lpukatch): standard JS named-group syntax in Custom Regex form (converts to/from Python via regex-utils). Dropped its stray docker-compose build:. change. Plus two remote-user requests: (1) Pattern Tester 'Apply to Form' now also saves the group (handleSubmit takes overrides to save with just-applied patterns); (2) interactive team-regex builder is accent-inclusive (Latin-1 + Latin Extended-A); pattern generator emits JS (?<name>) syntax to match #236. All on dev.

title: Dashboard -> command center (KPI cards + trend)
description: Elevate the Dashboard beyond the status strip: big-number KPI cards (matched %, channels, last run, programmes) with trend/sparkline from recent runs. The signature surface of the redesign. Depends on design tokens.

title: Depth + motion conventions (elevation, hover lift, transitions)
description: Apply elevation tiers to surfaces; add subtle hover lift on interactive cards/rows; smooth expand/collapse on CollapsibleSection; standard transition tokens. Depends on design tokens.

title: EmptyState primitive + apply to empty lists/tables
description: components/ui/empty-state.tsx (icon + headline + hint + optional CTA); replace plain 'no X' text across tables/lists (channels, templates, teams, assignments, sources).

title: Skeleton primitive + apply to table/card loading states
description: components/ui/skeleton.tsx; replace bare Loader2 spinners on tables/cards (RunHistoryTable, ManagedChannelsTable, settings cards) with skeleton rows for perceived speed.

title: Design tokens: accent + neutrals, radius, elevation tiers, type scale
description: Foundation of the 2026 redesign. Define a real palette (brand/accent color + neutral ramp), border-radius scale, elevation tiers (surface levels/shadows), and a type scale (sizes + weights, hero-number style) as Tailwind theme tokens + CSS vars in index.css. Everything else builds on this. Verify dark+light.

title: [PR #235] Mobile navigation (hamburger + fly-in) — merged
description: Merged PR #235 by @lpukatch: responsive mobile nav. Reconciled with the step-badge/Settings-step-0 nav rework; bumped breakpoint md→lg; added a11y (aria-expanded/label), close-on-Generate, tap-outside backdrop. First slice of mobile implementation (1kt3.3). Remaining: per-page responsiveness (Matching page flagged by author, Settings/Channels grid-cols walls, tables, pickers, modals) under 1kt3.2/1kt3.3.

title: ToggleCard primitive (master toggle gating card body) + adopt in Update Notifications
description: New components/ui/toggle-card.tsx: Card with the master toggle in the header upper-right that gates the body (always/children/footer slots). First adopter: Update Notifications card in Settings.

title: Dashboard progress checklist (0-5 steps, live state, deep links)
description: Replace the stale Getting Started block with a progress checklist against the 0-5 flow (Connect/Settings -> Sources -> Subscriptions -> Matching -> EPG -> Channels -> Generate). Each step shows done/not-done from REAL state and deep-links to its page. Fades/dismisses once complete.

title: First-run detection + state (trigger, dismiss, re-surface)
description: Detect a genuinely-new install: no sources AND no teams AND never generated (fix the old teams==0 mis-trigger). Persist a dismissed flag; allow re-surfacing. Provide a useFirstRun()/state signal the guide UI consumes.

title: Nav: emphasize Settings as step 0 (label + 0 badge)
description: Settings is only a gear icon and feels de-emphasized though it's effectively step 0 (Connect/Dispatcharr). Give it a 'Settings' label + '0' step badge matching steps 1-5, and include it in the first-run drop-off. MainLayout.tsx.

title: Nav: first-run step numbers (0-5) that drop off after visiting
description: Show the step-number badges as first-run affordances; once a step is visited, persist it (localStorage) and drop its number. First launch shows all; after clicking each, its number disappears. MainLayout.tsx.

title: TemplateForm: default new templates to Event type
description: Event templates are the main focus now. On create, pre-select Event in the type chooser (DEFAULT_FORM.template_type='event') and put the Event tile first. TemplateForm.tsx.

title: Subscriptions: hide Custom Leagues tile unless premium key set
description: Remote user found the locked Custom Leagues tile confusing (didn't hover to see why). Hide the tile entirely unless a TheSportsDB premium key is configured (premiumEnabled), instead of showing a disabled/locked tile. Subscriptions.tsx.

title: Remote UI feedback round 2 (v2.7.0)
description: Second batch of remote-user feedback on the v2.7.0 IA: hide Custom Leagues unless premium, default new templates to Event, first-run step-number affordances that drop off after visiting, and emphasize Settings as step 0.

title: Matching: move Custom Rules off the first/default tab to 3rd
description: Custom Rules being first+default implies the user must act. Reorder SubNav to EPG Matching, Event Lookahead, Custom Rules; default activeView to epg_matching. DetectionLibrary.tsx.

title: Dashboard: separate history from the accordions below
description: Add visual separation (top border + spacing) between the EPG Generation History block and the collapsible accordions (Managed Channels, EPG output) beneath it on the Dashboard.

title: Remote-user UI feedback (v2.7.0 polish)
description: Batch of UI feedback from a remote user on the new v2.7.0 IA. Small polish items across Dashboard, the SubNav primitive, the CollapsibleSection primitive, and the Matching tab.

title: [PR #233] Take-and-fix: international country name resolution
description: Community PR #233 by @sethwv (branch feat/country-local-layer). Adds CountryNameResolver (pycountry+babel, ~250 countries x 20 locales) as tier-3 in _resolve_alias, resolving localized country names (Brasil→brazil) without manual aliases. Review: normalization consistent with existing tiers (returns _normalize'd canonical, matches user-alias lowercasing + 'canonical in tp.pattern' check); graceful ImportError fallback. Caveats: adds 2 deps (pycountry, babel); no tests. Fix before merge: add tests (resolve brasil/marruecos/escocia + FIFA override turkey→turkiye + ImportError fallback path), confirm deps install in venv. Credit @sethwv (#233).

title: [PR #234] Take-and-fix: feed pattern after team (false matches)
description: Community PR #234 by @lpukatch (branch feed-fixes). Reworks _detect_team_in_stream_name in event_group_processor.py to require the opposing team NOT appear after a feed/home-away keyword match, preventing 'FEED TeamName' / '4K FEED A B' false positives. Review: logic correct. Fix before merge: (1) strip trailing whitespace (ruff), (2) add a test in tests/test_feed_separation.py. Credit @lpukatch (#234).

title: StatusStrip: live spinners during active generation
description: On the Dashboard StatusStrip, while a generation run is active (useGenerationProgress().isGenerating), show a spinner in place of the values that are mid-recompute — Last generated, managed channels, and matched % — so the strip visibly reflects that those numbers are being updated. Labels stay; values swap to a Loader2 spinner until the run completes, then the fresh values render. Dispatcharr and EPG URL chips are unaffected.

title: Settings strip + redistribute settings/* pages
description: docs/guide/settings/*: Settings is now system/integration only (media-server connections, timezone, advanced). Redistribute moved content: settings/channels→channels sub-pages, settings/epg→EPG/Matching, settings/event-groups→Sources, settings/teams→EPG/Teams. Retire/redirect stale settings pages; keep general/advanced/dispatcharr.

title: Channels section (5 sub-pages)
description: docs/guide/channels.md split into Lifecycle, Consolidation, Numbering, Stream Priority, and Dispatcharr Output sub-pages. Pull in settings/channels.md content that moved into these contextual pages.

title: EPG section (Templates/Teams/Output sub-nav)
description: docs/guide/epg.md restructured into the EPG sub-nav: Templates, Teams, EPG Output. Update docs/guide/templates/assignments.md (assignment now lives on the Templates page header). Document EPG Output page.

title: Matching (was Detection Library)
description: docs/guide/detection-library.md → matching.md: now its own nav step covering detection, regex, program/EPG matching, and exception keywords (consolidated from the old x2 locations). Fold relevant epg-matching.md content where appropriate.

title: Subscriptions (new page; Custom Leagues folded in; Teams demoted)
description: New docs/guide/subscriptions.md: unified sports/leagues/teams subscription home, Custom Leagues folded in, GlobalDefaults lifted here. Reframe docs/guide/teams.md as the demoted Teams-as-mode under EPG. Redirect/retire docs/guide/custom-leagues content.

title: Sources (was Event Groups)
description: docs/guide/event-groups/* → Sources. Reframe Event Groups as stream Sources, document per-group EPG-match toggle living here, and that GlobalDefaults moved out to Subscriptions. Rename files/dir and fix internal links.

title: Dashboard page (StatusStrip rewrite)
description: docs/guide/dashboard.md: Dashboard was rewritten around the StatusStrip (Dispatcharr status, last generated, managed channel count, matched %, EPG URL). Replace old dashboard description.

title: Nav & terminology overhaul (anchor)
description: docs/index.md + docs/guide/index.md: document the new 6-step nav (Dashboard, Sources, Subscriptions, Matching, EPG, Channels) and stripped Settings. Establish the rename map used by all other beads: Event Groups→Sources, Detection Library→Matching, Custom Leagues→Subscriptions, Teams→EPG/Teams. This bead settles terminology — do it first.

title: Docs resync for IA overhaul (v2.7.0)
description:
The v2.7.0 Information-Architecture overhaul (epic teamarrv2-vhbo) restructured the entire UI: new 6-step nav (Dashboard → Sources → Subscriptions → Matching → EPG → Channels) plus a stripped Settings. Pages were renamed (Event Groups → Sources, Detection Library → Matching), Custom Leagues folded into Subscriptions, Teams demoted under EPG, the EPG page split into Templates/Teams/Output sub-nav, and Channels split into Lifecycle/Consolidation/Numbering/Stream-Priority/Output sub-pages. Settings/* content was redistributed to contextual pages.

As a result the entire docs/guide/ tree (and parts of docs/reference/) now describe the OLD UI and are ~100% out of sync. This epic resyncs all user-facing docs to the new IA, page by page, matching the live frontend.

Supersedes the single coarse bead teamarrv2-vhbo.9 (Docs rewrite for new IA). No screenshots in docs (text-only). New route map: see frontend/src/App.tsx. Old→new redirects exist, so old URLs still resolve — docs should use the new canonical paths/names.

title: Build SaveButton primitive (async pending + icon)
description: Create src/components/ui/save-button.tsx (or AsyncButton): wraps Button, takes onClick + pending, renders Loader2 spinner when pending else a leading icon (default Save), label children. Replaces 35 copies of the isPending?Loader2:Save idiom across 17 files.

title: Build Alert primitive (info/success/warning/destructive)
description: Create src/components/ui/alert.tsx: <Alert variant='info|success|warning|destructive'> with optional icon + title + body. Tinted bg/border per tone (matching existing blue/yellow/green/destructive boxes). Replaces 57 hand-rolled colored boxes incl. the blue 'What is X' info tiles.

title: UI primitives consolidation
description:
Eliminate hand-rolled UI in favor of shared primitives (found via the v2.7.0 IA sweep). Build missing primitives first, then migrate call sites incrementally — each migration its own small verifiable bead.

New primitives to BUILD:
- Alert (info/success/warning/destructive) — absorbs 57 hand-rolled colored boxes + the blue 'What is X' info tiles
- SaveButton (async button: pending spinner + Save icon) — 35 copies across 17 files
- RadioCards (selectable bordered option-card group) — Auto/Manual, Consolidate/Separate
- FormField (Label + control + help text + error) — app's most repeated micro-structure

MIGRATE (from sweep):
- GlobalDefaults x3 card stragglers -> Card (overlaps 1kt3.5)
- colored boxes -> Alert
- raw <table> -> Table primitive (ExceptionKeywordsCard, PerLeagueChannelConfig, ChannelNumbering, CustomLeagues, TemplateAssignmentModal, EventGroupImport)
- TemplateForm <details> + hand-rolled collapsibles -> CollapsibleSection

title: EPG: clean up Default Durations UI
EPG: EPG Output view (Output Settings + Durations + XMLTV metadata from Advanced)
EPG: Template Assignments modal -> static view
EPG: 4-view SubNav + routes; Templates view = table only
EPG tab review & reorg
description:
Tab-by-tab IA review — tab ④ EPG. Decisions from maintainer 2026-06-13: replace the 2-view SubNav (Templates | Team EPG) with a 4-view SubNav: Templates | Template Assignments | Team EPG | EPG Output.
- Templates: just the templates table + New Template/Import. Remove the Manage-Template-Assignments button, the Output Settings card, and the Default Durations card (they move).
- Template Assignments: promote the event-template-by-sport/league assignment from a MODAL (TemplateAssignmentModal) to its own full view.
- Team EPG: unchanged (legacy demoted flow).
- EPG Output: new view = Output Settings (path/days/start-hours/include-final) + Default Durations (CLEANED UP — it's a mess) + XMLTV Generator Metadata (pulled from Settings > Advanced, since metadata is EPG output).
- Default Durations UI is a mess — make it cleaner.
notes:
FINAL (maintainer, after iterating): 4-view SubNav: Templates | Template Assignments | Team EPG | EPG Output.
- Templates: just the templates table + New Template/Import. Remove the Manage-Template-Assignments button AND the EpgOutputSettings (Output Settings + Default Durations) — both move.
- Template Assignments: promote TemplateAssignmentModal content to its OWN static SubNav view (no longer a modal/button).
- Team EPG: unchanged.
- EPG Output: Output Settings + Default Durations (CLEANED UP) + XMLTV Generator Metadata (moved from Settings>Advanced, lines ~2143). EpgOutputSettings.tsx currently holds Output Settings + Default Durations (rendered on Templates at line 387) — move it to EPG Output and add the metadata.
- Default Durations is a mess — redesign cleaner.

title: EPG 'use DP as source' group picker: exclude M3U groups
description: EventMatchingSettings 'Dispatcharr groups to include' picker fetches /api/v1/dispatcharr/channel-groups?exclude_m3u=false. Change to exclude_m3u=true so only curated (non-M3U) channel groups appear. One-line frontend change.

title: Matching reorg: subtitle, regex callout, EPG collapsible, detection SubNav + Add next to table
description: Reorg /matching (DetectionLibrary.tsx + EventMatchingSettings.tsx) top-to-bottom: (1) drop subtitle; (2) custom-regex callout banner linking to Sources (most powerful lever, per-source); (3) Event Matching (lookahead) visible w/ one-line explainer; (4) EPG Program Matching as a collapsed CollapsibleSection; (5) Detection Library 5 categories via the SubNav segmented control, with the Add/Import/Export toolbar moved from the page header to DIRECTLY ABOVE THE TABLE (fixes the can't-find-Add bug). Verify create submits for aliases + keywords.

title: Matching tab review & reorg
description:
Tab-by-tab IA review — tab ③ Matching (/matching = DetectionLibrary.tsx + EventMatchingSettings.tsx). Decisions/brief from maintainer 2026-06-13.

PURPOSE OF TAB (maintainer's words): where users dial in 'I've got sources, I know which sports/leagues/teams I want — now give Teamarr rules to make successful matches that feed the downstream steps.'

LOGICAL REORG (top to bottom), built from the ground up:
1. Trash the subtitle.
2. CUSTOM REGEX CALLOUT (top): the single most powerful matching lever is custom regex, and it is PER STREAM SOURCE. Call it out prominently with a link to Sources — each source can need its own patterns. (Regex is NOT moved here; it stays per-source. This is a signpost.)
3. Event Matching: Event Lookahead = how many days of upcoming events to consider when matching streams (feeds providers' days_ahead window). Small, visible. Add a one-line explainer.
4. EPG Program Matching (collapse it — it's big): attach/detach buffers; use Dispatcharr channels as EPG source (+ which groups — SEE BUG below); fall back to Xtream (XC) EPG (+ cache hours).
5. Detection Library (the hints/aliases). Five categories — use the standard SubNav segmented control (ONE level, peer categories — not deep burying): Team Aliases (alt name -> official), Event Type Detection (what kind of event), League Hints (what league), Sport Hints (what sport), Separators (add separators not natively supported). Move the Add/Import/Export toolbar OUT of the far page header to DIRECTLY ABOVE THE TABLE so Add is findable.

BUGS to fix in this reorg:
A. 'Can't add / button not visible': the Add button is unconditionally rendered but only in the top-right page header, scrolled far away below the huge EPG-group wall. Fix by relocating Add next to the table (#5 above). Verify create actually submits for both aliases and keywords.
B. EPG 'use DP as source' group picker must show ONLY curated channel groups that ACTUALLY CONTAIN CHANNELS — exclude M3U-provided groups AND empty groups. Today it fetches /dispatcharr/channel-groups?exclude_m3u=false (all incl M3U) with no channel count. Fix: capture channel_count from Dispatcharr's /api/channels/groups/ (DispatcharrChannelGroup.from_api + route response), and in EventMatchingSettings fetch exclude_m3u=true and filter to channel_count>0.

SCOPE NOTES: Exception keywords stay on Channels (post-match consolidation, not matching). Nothing else obviously needs to move here. A test-pattern tool reachable from Matching is desirable (relates to custom-regex epic 0tgt) — note but don't block.
notes: exclude_m3u=true chosen to avoid backend work (maintainer).

title: Adopt SubNav: Subscriptions, Settings, EPG; remove StepTabs
Build SubNav primitive (segmented, state+route modes)
Unified sub-nav primitive (app-wide)
description:
Secondary (within-tab) navigation is inconsistent: Settings uses an underline tab-strip; EPG (Templates/Teams) uses StepTabs pills; Subscriptions now uses bordered tiles. Mirror the collapsible-primitive effort (wk7t): ONE shared <SubNav> primitive, used everywhere.

DESIGN: a refined segmented control (the 'tile' feel the maintainer likes, dialed back so it stays visually subordinate to the main top nav). Muted track + active segment raised/filled. Supports an optional leading icon and a disabled state (e.g. Custom Leagues lock until premium).

DUAL MODE: must support (a) state-driven (value + onChange, for in-page tiles — Subscriptions, Settings) AND (b) route-driven (NavLink items with , for separate sub-pages — EPG Templates|Team EPG).

ADOPT: Subscriptions (replace inline tiles), Settings (replace tab-strip), EPG Templates.tsx + Teams.tsx (replace StepTabs). Remove the old StepTabs component once unused.

title: Custom regex: investigate & fix matching failures/dysfunction
description: Reported custom-regex failures: patterns that should match don't / extraction misbehaves. Trace custom_regex_* fields from the form through storage into the matching/extraction pipeline (teamarr/consumers/matching). Gather concrete failing stream-name examples, find root cause(s), fix, add tests.

title: Custom regex: enable Pattern Tester in create mode
description: Allow 'Open Pattern Tester' (TestPatternsModal) during Stream Source CREATION, not just edit. Removes the create->save->edit->test friction.

title: Custom regex — friction & correctness
description:
Custom regex (per-Stream-Source pattern extraction in EventGroupForm's Custom Regex section, used by the matching pipeline) has both UX friction and correctness problems reported by the maintainer.

SCOPE:
1. Friction: the Pattern Tester ('Open Pattern Tester' / TestPatternsModal) is EDIT-MODE ONLY — you can't test patterns while creating a Stream Source, forcing create -> save -> edit -> test. Make it available during creation too.
2. Correctness: custom regex failures / dysfunction reported — patterns that should match don't, or extraction misbehaves. Investigate end-to-end (pattern fields -> stored -> applied in matching: teamarr/consumers/matching) against real stream names, identify the break(s), and fix. Capture concrete failing examples.

Surface area: frontend EventGroupForm Custom Regex section + TestPatternsModal; backend matching/extraction using the custom_regex_* fields.

title: Move event-template assignment from Subscriptions to EPG > Templates
description: Relocate the event-template assignment entry point (TemplateAssignmentModal, opened by 'Template Assignments / Manage (N)') off the Subscriptions page and onto the EPG > Templates page, co-located with template creation. Fixes the backward flow (assign on tab 2 before templates exist on tab 4). Modal is self-contained/reusable; just move the trigger + section.

title: Subscriptions: Custom Leagues as 4th tile — premium-only subtitle + remove Free TSDB tier option
description:
Custom Leagues = 4th Subscriptions tile, premium-gated:
- The Custom Leagues TILE in the sub-nav is greyed out / non-interactable until a TheSportsDB premium key is configured in Settings (with the premium subtitle explaining why). Today there's a PremiumGate message inside the page; extend that to disable the tile itself.
- Prominent 'Premium TheSportsDB only' subtitle.
- REMOVE the 'TSDB Tier' select field ENTIRELY from the add/edit dialog (not just the Free option) + drop the tsdb_tier form field/state. Custom leagues are premium by nature, so per-league tier is unneeded; prewarm gating (46y3) can treat all custom leagues as premium. Verify backend doesn't require tsdb_tier (send null/omit).

title: Subscriptions: tile nav (Sport/League, Soccer, Teams) + drop Global Defaults naming/subtitle
description: Replace the single GlobalDefaults card with tile-based sub-nav: Sport/League (non-soccer LeaguePicker), Soccer (SoccerModeSelector), Teams (include/exclude matching filter). Drop the 'Global Defaults' framing and the page subtitle 'The sports and leagues you follow'. Remove the Template Assignments section from here (moves to EPG — see sibling bead). Custom Leagues is the 4th tile (sibling bead).

title: Subscriptions tab review & reorg
description:
Tab-by-tab IA review — tab ② Subscriptions. Decisions locked with maintainer 2026-06-13.

TERMINOLOGY: 'Teams' is overloaded. In Subscriptions, Teams = the include/exclude MATCHING FILTER for the event flow (the current 'Default Team Filter'). The legacy 'Team EPG' (generate EPG for static team channels the user already has; Teamarr does NOT manage those channels) is niche/demoted and stays on the EPG tab — NOT in Subscriptions.

STRUCTURE: Replace the single 'Global Defaults' card with TILE-BASED sub-nav. Drop the 'Global Defaults' name (it's implicit — on this tab the defaults ARE the subscriptions; per-source overrides keep their 'Use global subscription' framing on the Sources tab). Tiles:
  1. Sport/League — non-soccer league subscriptions (LeaguePicker)
  2. Soccer — soccer leagues + mode + followed teams (SoccerModeSelector); own tile because it's genuinely different (mode, followed-teams, ~250 leagues)
  3. Teams — include/exclude matching filter (TeamPicker + mode + playoff bypass)
  4. Custom Leagues — premium-only; prominent 'Premium TheSportsDB only' subtitle; REMOVE the 'Free' option from the TSDB Tier select (feature is premium-gated; free is contradictory)

KEYSTONE: event-template assignment (the 'Template Assignments / Manage' modal) leaves Subscriptions and moves to the EPG > Templates page, co-located with template creation (flow fix: subscribe on tab 2, then create+assign templates on tab 4). Team-template assignment already lives on the EPG-tab Teams page.

The 4 scattered subscription locations are really 2 concepts (league sub, team filter) x (global default in Subscriptions + per-source override on Sources) — a valid pattern; keep it, just ensure the global<->override relationship reads clearly. Relates to existing epics eqz (Custom Leagues via UI) and 46y3 (TSDB premium gating); supersedes vhbo.2.

title: Sources: collapse stat tiles to a single Matched % (detail -> logs)
description: Replace the 4-tile grid (Streams/Filtered/Excluded/Matched) on /sources with a single Matched-percentage indicator (color-coded). Remove the Streams/Filtered/Excluded tiles + their hover breakdowns. The filtering/excluding breakdown detail is acceptable to live in generation logs only (verify it's logged; add if missing).

title: Sources form: standardize collapsibles + reorder sections
