(b) reorder: Basic Settings -> Subscription Override -> Team Filtering -> Custom Regex -> Stream Timezone.

title: Sources list header: drop Matching link, Import -> Add Stream Source, drop subtitle
description: EventGroups.tsx header: remove the 'Matching' button (navigates /matching). Relabel 'Import' primary action to 'Add Stream Source'. Remove the subtitle 'M3U stream groups that feed event-based EPG'.

title: Sources: rename Event Group -> Stream Source (user-facing)
description: Sweep user-facing 'Event Group'/'event group' strings to 'Stream Source' across EventGroups.tsx, EventGroupForm.tsx, EventGroupImport.tsx: form titles (Configure/Edit Stream Source), Delete dialog, empty states ('No stream sources configured'), bulk dialog titles/counts, descriptions, toasts. Page H1 stays 'Sources'. Leave routes, state vars, API fields, comments.

title: Sources tab review & reorg
description:
Tab-by-tab IA review — tab ① Sources (EventGroups list, EventGroupForm, EventGroupImport). Decisions locked with maintainer 2026-06-13:
- Terminology: rename user-facing 'Event Group' -> 'Stream Source' (item level: Add/Edit/Configure/Delete Stream Source, empty states, dialog titles, descriptions, toasts). Tab/page H1 stays 'Sources'. Do NOT touch routes/vars/API fields/comments.
- List header (/sources): remove the 'Matching' cross-link (reachable from nav); primary action 'Import' relabeled 'Add Stream Source'. Drop the explanatory subtitle.
- Form (EventGroupForm): (a) standardize the 5 sections on CollapsibleSection (pure consistency, consumes wk7t.3); (b) reorder sections: Basic Settings -> Subscription Override -> Team Filtering -> Custom Regex -> Stream Timezone (lead with 'what events do I want', push technical Regex/Timezone down).

title: Sweep: adopt CollapsibleSection across remaining sections
description: Migrate remaining section disclosures to CollapsibleSection: ChannelConfigSettings, StreamOrderingManager, TemplateForm sections, DetectionLibrary, EventGroupForm, GlobalDefaults, EventGroupImport. Leave dropdown/picker chevrons (Select, filter-select, LeaguePicker, TeamPicker, VariablePicker, checkbox-list-picker, hierarchical-sortable) alone.
notes: Audit complete (2026-06-13): app-wide grep shows EventGroupForm.tsx is the ONLY file with the genuine clickable-card-header section-disclosure pattern — 5 sections: Basic Settings, Custom Regex, Subscription Override, Team Filtering, Stream Timezone (already left-chevron). All other chevron usages are list-row expanders (ChannelConfigSettings league rows, StreamOrderingManager league expanders, TemplateForm fallback/condition/category) or dropdown/picker affordances (filter-select, VariablePicker, Select) — intentionally NOT converted (different UI element). DECISION: convert EventGroupForm's 5 sections to CollapsibleSection during the Sources tab review (EventGroupForm is a Sources component), in-context, handling the collapsed-state header hints/badges then. Dashboard adoption already done (wk7t.2).

title: All-Time Totals -> de-emphasized bottom footer
description: Extract All-Time Totals out of EpgOutput into a muted footer row at the very bottom of the Dashboard (no card/heading, text-xs muted). Drop the 'Last Run' item (now in the status strip).

title: Collapsible EpgOutput + channel tables, persisted state
description: EpgOutput (URL, coverage, XML preview) and Managed Channels (active + recently-deleted) collapsed by default; persist open/closed per-section in localStorage.

title: Recent Runs: truncate to 4 rows + inline show-more
description: Keep RunHistoryTable but show 4 rows by default with an inline 'Show more' that expands to the 10 already fetched. No new page.

title: Strip Dashboard down: remove quadrants, quick actions, on-page Generate, stale Getting Started
description: Remove the 2x2 quadrant grid + tooltips, the 3 quick-action buttons, the on-page Generate button, and the old Getting Started block (leave a clean slot for epic 297x). Stop calling /stats/dashboard. Fix/remove now-stale route usage. This establishes the new skeleton the other beads build on.

title: Status strip component (read-only health panel)
description: New StatusStrip at top of Dashboard. Read-only chips (no navigation): Dispatcharr connection (●/error from useDispatcharrStatus); 'Last generated' merged chip (✓/✗ + relative time + duration from stats/recent runs; color: green < scheduler interval, amber past it; no scheduler → amber >24h, red >3d); live channel count; copy XMLTV URL button (lone control). New-install placeholders ('Never generated','Not connected'). Compose from existing hooks.

title: Dashboard redesign — health-and-control panel
description:
First tab in the tab-by-tab IA review. Convert the Dashboard from a data/composition overview into a lean health-and-control panel.

LOCKED DECISIONS (2026-06-13, with maintainer):
- REMOVE: 4-quadrant stat grid + all tooltips; the 3 quick-action buttons; the on-page Generate button (top nav already has it); the stale Getting Started block (handled by epic 297x — leave a clean slot).
- ADD: a top STATUS STRIP (read-only, NO chip navigation): Dispatcharr connection · 'Last generated' (merged last-run + EPG-freshness: ✓/✗ + relative time + duration; green < scheduler interval, amber past it; no scheduler → amber >24h, red >3d) · live channel count · copy XMLTV URL button (lone control).
- Compose strip from existing hooks (useDispatcharrStatus, useStats/useRecentRuns, XMLTV URL). Stop calling heavy /stats/dashboard.
- Recent Runs: KEEP, truncate to 4 rows + inline 'show more' (expand to the 10 already fetched; no new page).
- EpgOutput (URL, coverage, XML preview) + Managed Channels (active) + Recently Deleted: collapsible, collapsed by default, persist open/closed in localStorage.
- All-Time Totals: extract from EpgOutput to the VERY BOTTOM as a de-emphasized muted footer row (no card/heading); DROP its 'Last Run' item (now in the strip).
- New-install/empty: strip renders with placeholders ('Never generated','Not connected'); Getting Started slot carries guidance.
- Ship Dashboard standalone FIRST; composition detail migrates to Sources/Subscriptions/Channels as those tabs are reworked (temporary gap accepted).

title: Getting Started / first-run experience — total overhaul
description:
The current Getting Started block on the Dashboard is stale (old IA vocabulary + old routes: Settings/Templates/Teams/Event Groups) and mis-triggered (gated on teams==0, but event-only setups legitimately have zero teams). Replace it entirely.

GOALS:
- Rebuild as a progress checklist against the new 0-5 flow: Connect (Dispatcharr) -> Sources -> Subscriptions -> Matching -> EPG -> Channels -> Generate. Each step shows done/not-done from real state, deep-links to its page.
- Correct the trigger: show for genuinely-new installs (no sources AND no teams AND never generated), not just teams==0. Dismissible once set up; re-surfaceable.
- Decide placement: dedicated first-run screen/onboarding vs. a Dashboard panel that fades as steps complete.
- Optional guided walkthrough (relates to vhbo.8 first-run onboarding — consolidate/supersede that bead).
- Update all CTAs to new routes.

This is separate from the Dashboard redesign (status strip + lean layout), but the Dashboard redesign should leave a clean slot for whatever this produces.

title: UI: surface stale groups + suggest deletion (batch)
description: Notification/badge listing stale groups (Dashboard and/or Channels). Per-group delete + batch 'delete all stale' with confirmation, routed through the safe Dispatcharr delete path. No auto-delete.

title: Define + detect stale groups in reconciliation
description: Decide the precise 'stale' definition and implement detection in reconciliation.py: managed groups no longer backed by any active source/league/event. Add a drift field (e.g. stale_groups) with last-seen metadata. Guard against false positives (off-season/empty).
notes:
DEFINITION OF "STALE" (decided after codebase investigation).

A managed event group is STALE when ALL of:
- it is enabled, AND
- it has a configured M3U source (m3u_group_id IS NOT NULL), AND
- that channel-group no longer exists in Dispatcharr — i.e. m3u_group_id is not in
  the id set returned by client.m3u.list_groups() (GET /api/channels/groups/).

Why this definition (avoids the off-season false positive the epic warns about):
- Dispatcharr channel-groups are PERSISTENT entities (list_groups hits /api/channels/groups/),
  so a group with zero current streams (off-season) STILL appears in the list -> NOT stale.
- A group missing from the list = the group entity was deleted/renamed in Dispatcharr =
  the source is genuinely gone -> stale.
- This is the epic's "source gone" option, NOT the "empty for N runs" option (which
  conflates off-season with removed). last_refresh is useless here (it updates every run
  regardless of streams found).

SCOPE v1 (lylt.1): M3U-source-backed event groups only.
Out of scope (future follow-ups): league-only / dynamic {sport}/{league} group staleness,
and "a league stopped being provided" (leagues now come from the global subscription).
Excluded always: the system channel-source group (is_channel_source = 1).

ROBUSTNESS: if list_groups() fails / Dispatcharr unreachable -> detect nothing (never flag
everything stale on a connection blip). Confirm-only; never auto-delete.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN
lylt.1 (detect):
1. Schema: add `source_last_seen TIMESTAMP` to event_epg_groups (reconciliation.py auto-adds
   the column; no migration block needed). Bump schema_version note if desired.
2. detect_stale_groups(conn, dispatcharr_client) -> list[StaleGroup]:
   - existing = {g.id for g in client.m3u.list_groups()}; if empty due to error -> return [].
   - for each enabled group (exclude is_channel_source) with m3u_group_id:
       in existing -> UPDATE source_last_seen = now (refresh)
       not in existing -> stale; collect {id, name, m3u_group_name, source_last_seen, channel_count}
3. Hook into the end-of-generation reconciliation pass (generation step 7) so source_last_seen
   is maintained and stale groups detected each run; expose via ReconciliationResult as a new
   'stale_group' issue type.
4. Tests: missing m3u_group_id -> flagged; present -> not flagged + source_last_seen set;
   list_groups error -> nothing flagged; is_channel_source excluded.

lylt.2 (UI):
- Badge/notification (Dashboard and/or Channels) listing stale groups + last-seen.
- Per-group dismiss/delete + batch "delete all stale" (with confirmation), via the safe
  Dispatcharr path (lifecycle/_safe_update_channel pattern).

title: Stale group detection & cleanup
description:
When a provider inactivates/removes a group (e.g. an M3U event-group source, a dynamic {sport}/{league} group, or a league that stops being provided) so it's no longer found on the next generation/reconcile, Teamarr should VISUALLY notify the user and suggest deletion. Batch action when multiple stale groups exist.

SCOPE:
- Detect stale groups: groups Teamarr created/manages in Dispatcharr that no longer map to any active source/league/event. Extend reconciliation (teamarr/database/reconciliation.py) drift detection.
- Surface in UI: a notification/badge (Dashboard and/or Channels) listing stale groups with last-seen info.
- Suggest deletion: per-group dismiss/delete + a batch 'delete all stale' action (with confirmation). Deletion must go through the safe Dispatcharr update path (lifecycle/_safe_update_channel pattern).
- Don't auto-delete — user confirms. Avoid false positives for temporarily-empty groups (off-season, no current events).

Open question for planning: define 'stale' precisely (N consecutive generations not found? provider-inactive flag? zero managed channels + source gone?).

title: Implement mobile responsiveness fixes (from 1kt3.2 punch list)
description: Apply the fixes identified by the look & feel audit and mobile responsiveness audit. Likely split into per-area beads once audits are done.
notes:
COMPLETE. Mobile responsiveness shipped to dev:
- Grids: all non-responsive grid-cols-2/3/4 given a mobile single-col base; RadioCards primitive stacks too.
- ResponsiveTable primitive (one column config -> desktop table + mobile cards, no drift; rowClassName/mobileTitle/mobileHidden/renderMobileCard).
- Card-stacked the clean data tables: Run History (Dashboard) + Matching alias/keyword tables (user-reported). Verified 390px + desktop.
- Heavy sort/filter/select tables (Teams, Sources/EventGroups, ManagedChannels): left as horizontal-scroll — the Table PRIMITIVE already wraps in overflow-x-auto, so they scroll-contain and don't break the page. Card-stacking them would strip selection/sort/bulk-edit, so scroll is the correct treatment.
- Modals: fine (Dialog primitive uses w-full inside a p-4 flex wrapper).
- Nav: mobile hamburger (PR #235) + lg breakpoint + a11y.

title: Mobile responsiveness audit — per-page breakpoint inventory
description: Test every page in a mobile viewport. Inventory what breaks: top nav (7 items + Generate), Settings tab bar, multi-column grids (grid-cols-2/lg:grid-cols-4), fixed-width selects (w-64), tables, pickers, modals (Playwright note: modals can overflow viewport). Output a prioritized list of responsive fixes.
notes:
MOBILE RESPONSIVENESS AUDIT (390px) — punch list, prioritized.

High impact:
1. Settings.tsx — 7+ `grid grid-cols-2 gap-4` with no mobile fallback (L336,1031,1061,1131,1466,1636,1730) → `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2`. Plus nested cache stat grids L1942,1985.
2. Teams.tsx — `grid grid-cols-4 gap-3` (L572) no fallback → `grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4`; fixed min-w cards in dropdowns L580/603/633/661.
3. DetectionLibrary.tsx (Matching, user-reported) — fixed-width table cols L489-492, L564-569 → wrap tables in `overflow-x-auto`.
4. Modals overflow on phone: RunHistoryTable L312/447 `max-w-6xl`, EventMatcherModal L236 `max-w-xl h-[80vh]` → add mobile width clamp `w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]`.
5. EpgOutputSettings L58 `grid-cols-3`, TeamEpgSettingsCard L59 `grid-cols-3`, XmltvMetadataCard L43 `grid-cols-2`, EventMatchingSettings L93 `grid-cols-2` — add mobile base.
6. CustomLeagues dialogs L284/307/326 `grid-cols-2` + DialogContent width L278.
7. Channels: ChannelLifecycle L41, ChannelNumbering L155 `grid-cols-2` → mobile base.
8. EventGroupForm L375, EventGroupImport L579, Teams L109, TemplateForm L331/1594 grids.

Clean: Dashboard, Subscriptions, Templates (list), EpgOutput, most Channels pages.

Fix templates:
- grid-cols-2 → grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2
- grid-cols-3 → grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3
- grid-cols-4 → grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4
- tables → wrap in <div className="overflow-x-auto">
- modals → max-w clamp + w-[calc(100vw-2rem)]

Implementation tracked under 1kt3.3.

title: Look & feel audit — critique current design, propose modernization
description: Walk every page (Dashboard, Sources, Subscriptions, Matching, EPG, Channels, Settings tabs) and assess: visual hierarchy, spacing/density, typography scale, color/dark-mode usage, card/tile consistency, empty & loading states, iconography. Output a concrete list of streamline/modernize recommendations (no code yet).
notes:
LOOK & FEEL AUDIT (1kt3.1) + DIRECTION DECISION.

VERDICT: Solid B-tier. Clean, consistent, functional default Tailwind/shadcn dark dashboard (~2022 feel). Fine for the *arr/homelab audience; NOT distinctive or "2026-modern".

GOOD (keep): primitives consistency (Card/ToggleCard/SubNav/Alert/Badge); Dashboard status strip (the one modern touch); semantic color discipline (green/red/amber); fast, uncluttered; dark+light both render correctly (verified).

GAPS:
1. No design language/brand — generic blue-on-gray, looks like any admin template; logo never expressed in UI.
2. Flat uniform surfaces — every card same bg/border, no elevation hierarchy or depth.
3. Wall-to-wall tables / data-tool density; no summary cards/sparklines/trend chips; little breathing room.
4. Utilitarian typography — one system font, weak size/weight contrast, no hero numbers.
5. Static — minimal motion (no hover lift, skeletons, animated counters, smooth expand/collapse).
6. Plain empty/loading states — bare "no X" text + raw spinners.
7. Loose spatial rhythm — sparse pages float in 1440px void; space-y varies page to page.

DECISION (user, 2026-06-14): **Distinctive 2026 redesign** — full design-language overhaul. Not just mobile/polish.

PHASED PLAN (beads under 1kt3):
- Foundation: design tokens (accent+neutrals, radius, elevation tiers, type scale) — blocks the rest.
- Primitives: Skeleton, EmptyState, elevation/hover/motion conventions.
- Signature surfaces: Dashboard -> command center (big-number KPI cards + trend); shared page shell + spacing.
- Motion: animated counters, smooth expand/collapse, transition polish.
- Practical track (already scoped): mobile fixes (1kt3.3 from 1kt3.2 punch list), card consistency (1kt3.5).

title: UI polish & mobile optimization
description:
Separate work stream from the IA reorganization (epic vhbo). Holistic pass on the overall look and feel + mobile browser compatibility.

GOALS:
1. Look & feel audit — is the current design good? Could it be streamlined/modernized (spacing, typography, color usage, density, card/tile consistency, empty states, loading states, iconography)?
2. Mobile browser optimization — responsive layouts across all pages (nav, tabs, multi-column grids, tables, pickers, modals). Currently many Settings/Channels layouts use fixed grid-cols-2/lg:grid-cols-4 and w-64 selects that likely break on small screens.
3. Consistency — shared component usage, button/badge/spacing conventions, dark-mode correctness.

This is design/UX/responsive work, NOT feature or IA reorganization. Implementation beads to be added after a look-and-feel audit + mobile audit.

title: Restructure Settings General tab; remove EPG tab
description: Settings General tab becomes 3 tiles: (1) Time/Localization Settings (blue info box explaining UI vs EPG-output timezone at top, then UI Display Timezone, EPG Output Timezone, Time Formatting subsections), (2) Schedule (Scheduled Generation moved from EPG tab), (3) TheSportsDB API Key (moved from System/Advanced tab). Remove the Settings EPG tab entirely (now empty after Scheduled Generation moves out and Scheduled Channel Reset already moved to Media Servers). Keep centralized epg/display/scheduler state + existing full-object save handlers (PUT /settings/epg is full-replace; Time/Localization and Schedule both share the epg blob).

title: Move Scheduled Channel Reset to Media Servers tab (standalone card)
description: Scheduled Channel Reset (scheduler.channel_reset_enabled/cron) is currently in Settings > EPG. It's a media-server guide-refresh workaround (stale logos), not an EPG-generation setting. Move it to a standalone card in Settings > Media Servers tab. Extract CronPreview to a shared component. Self-contained card via useSchedulerSettings/useUpdateSchedulerSettings; partial save of channel_reset_* only (scheduler update is a merge, won't touch generation schedule).

title: vhbo settings-dissolution final slice: Dispatcharr output cards → Channels
description: Move Default Channel Profiles, Default Stream Profile, Default Channel Group (+M3U toggle + group mode), and Logo Cleanup from Settings Dispatcharr tab to the Channels page (output section). Keep Connection Settings + EPG Source in Settings. SAFETY: extracted component must replicate handleSaveDispatcharr full-blob save exactly (send url/username/epg_id loaded-fresh + edited profiles/group/logos, omit password unless changed) so it can't wipe Dispatcharr creds.

title: ③ Matching: own nav step (detection, regex, program matching, exception keywords)
description: Step 3: Matching — now its OWN top-level nav step (promoted from a sub-zone of the EPG home in the first draft). The technical 'how it matches' side: surface the orphaned Detection Library here; per-group regex; EPG program matching (pull global tuning out of Settings); exception keywords (DEDUP to one home). This is the troubleshooting surface users touch when a match fails. Separated from EPG-output (step 4) so the two distinct mental tasks — how it matches vs how it looks — don't share a page. The per-group on/off toggle itself lives in Sources (step 1); this step is the config/tuning.

title: Docs rewrite for new IA + Playwright e2e verification
description: Rewrite docs/guide navigation to match the new 5-step IA (Sources → Subscriptions → Matching → EPG → Channels; every settings/*.md page whose content moved needs its doc home updated); validate old-URL redirects; Playwright pass over each step flow end-to-end on :5173. Final phase.
notes: Superseded by epic teamarrv2-vt15 (Docs resync for IA overhaul), which breaks this into per-section beads. Use vt15 for the docs work.

title: First-run onboarding: guided 5-step setup walkthrough
description: Optional guided first-run walkthrough so new users aren't lost: step them through Connect → Sources → Subscriptions → Matching → EPG → Channels → Generate. Considered (not committed) per maintainer. Depends on the step homes existing.
notes: Consolidated under Getting Started epic 297x (2026-06-13). This bead = the OPTIONAL guided walkthrough slice; the broader first-run/getting-started overhaul (progress checklist, corrected trigger, placement) lives in sibling beads under 297x. Still depends on the step homes existing.

title: Dedup + naming sweep (team filter scope, residual duplicates, copy)
description: Cross-cutting cleanup: clarify team-filter scope (currently 3 places: GlobalDefaults, EventGroupForm, Stream Ordering — audit smell E), remove any residual duplicate controls left after the moves, resolve remaining naming collisions, normalize copy/labels across the new step homes. Depends on the step homes existing.

title: Settings → system/integration only
description: After the step homes absorb their config (Subscriptions, Matching, EPG, Channels), strip Settings to system/integration concerns ONLY: Dispatcharr CONNECTION (url/creds/test + EPG source link), media servers (Emby/Jellyfin/Channels DVR), scheduled generation + channel reset, data caches, backup/restore, TSDB API key, XMLTV metadata, display/timezone. This is the 'anything that doesn't live cleanly in steps 1–5' bucket. Remove emptied tabs and the Teams/Events tab-name collisions. Depends on steps 2–5.

title: ⑤ Channels home: lifecycle, numbering, consolidation, sort priority, Dispatcharr output
description: Expand the Channels page from read-only view into the 'how/when channels are created' home. Absorb from Settings→Channels: lifecycle timing, numbering mode/range, consolidation, Sort Priority (incl. new Priority Teams), per-league channel config, stream ordering. Absorb the OUTPUT half of Settings→Dispatcharr: default channel profiles, stream profile, channel group + mode, logo cleanup (audit smell J). Keep the read-only managed-channel list as a sub-view.

title: ④ EPG: output presentation (templates, names, logos, feed-separation labels)
description: Step 4: EPG — how the OUTPUT looks. Templates (names, logos, descriptions), assignment by sport/league, feed-separation labeling. The creative, beginner-friendly 'what you want the EPG to look like' side. Now a standalone nav step, cleanly separated from Matching (step 3, the technical detection side) per the revised vision — beginners doing names/logos never trip over regex.

title: ① Sources: reframe Event Groups as stream Sources (+ per-group EPG-match toggle)
description: Step 1: 'Sources' = the M3U event-groups that have streams. Rename/reframe Event Groups → Sources; make the M3U-group-as-stream-source relationship explicit (audit smell P). Mostly relabel + clarify copy + move subscription overrides out to the Subscriptions home (step 2). NEW: the per-group EPG-matching ON/OFF toggle lives HERE (revised vision — you decide per source whether matching runs); the detailed matching CONFIG (regex/detection/keywords) is step 3 Matching, linked from here.

title: ② Subscriptions home: lift GlobalDefaults out of Event Groups; unify the 4 subscription locations; fold in Custom Leagues; Teams-as-mode
description: The highest-value consolidation. Create a top-level Subscriptions page that becomes the single home for 'what sports/leagues you like': move the buried GlobalDefaults composite out of the Event Groups page, absorb the league/soccer-mode controls currently scattered across Settings→Teams, Settings→Event Groups, and Settings→Channels per-league. Fold the orphaned Custom Leagues page in here (adding a league = a subscription). Team-based generation lives here as a secondary 'Followed teams' mode (this is where demoted Teams lands).

title: IA shell: 5-step nav + route structure + old→new redirects
description: Foundation for the overhaul. Replace the 7-item nav with a stepwise flow: Dashboard, ① Sources, ② Subscriptions, ③ Matching, ④ EPG, ⑤ Channels, Settings (system). Remove Teams from top-level nav (de-emphasized → a mode under Subscriptions). Stand up new route paths + redirect every old URL (/teams, /templates, /event-groups, /detection-library, /custom-leagues, /epg) to its new home so bookmarks survive. Page shells can be thin at first; later beads fill them. Connect + Generate are onboarding bookends, not nav items. Blocks all step-home work.

title: UI/UX Information-Architecture Overhaul (v2.7.0)
description: Reorganize the UI around the real user flow (revised with maintainer 2026-06-12). Nav is a stepwise flow: 0 Connect (Dispatcharr) → 1 Sources (M3U event-groups that have streams; per-group EPG-matching on/off lives here) → 2 Subscriptions (sports/leagues/teams you care about; Teams DE-EMPHASIZED — still useful, the genesis of the project, but secondary now that team streams feed event groups; Custom Leagues folded in) → 3 Matching (detection, regex, program matching, exception keywords) → 4 EPG (how the OUTPUT looks: templates, names, logos, feed-separation labels) → 5 Channels (lifecycle, numbering, sort priority incl. Priority Teams, consolidation, Dispatcharr output) → ▶ Generate & see results. Settings = ONLY what doesn't live cleanly above (media-server connections, timezone, integration/system). KEY CHANGE from first draft: EPG split into TWO top-level steps — Matching (3, how it matches) and EPG (4, how it looks) — instead of one home with two zones. Goals: one home per decision, no hunting across Settings + main page, surface orphaned pages (Detection Library, Custom Leagues), kill duplication (exception keywords x2, team filter x3), optional guided first-run walkthrough. Nothing removed — every feature gets a clearer home. Reserved for v2.7.0. Inventory: plans/ux-audit-inventory.md.

title: Spike+build: auto channel-proxy source (emulate self-ref import)
description: Channels mode should emulate the user's self-referential-import workaround: for each curated DP channel, treat the channel's OUTPUT (proxy URL) as one custom stream, so 1 channel = 1 stream and Dispatcharr manages inner failover. Validated feasible: DP streams support is_custom+url, Teamarr has POST, channel uuid available. Spike the proxy URL format, then auto-create/manage custom channel-proxy streams as candidates. Replaces the current 'pick first inner stream' logic.
design: See memory channel-proxy-source-spike.md. Proxy URL = {base}/proxy/ts/stream/{uuid} (confirmed live). Custom streams supported (url+is_custom+m3u_account). Design: auto-create 1 Teamarr-managed custom proxy stream per curated channel, lifecycle-managed, under a dedicated M3U account. Channels-mode (pick-first-stream) removed from dev as wrong abstraction. Also fix list_streams full-catalog perf bug.

title: Migration: preserve EPG-matching off-state when global switch was off (3lp1)
description: Removing the global epg_match_enabled switch (3lp1.1) changed gating: EPG matching + channel-source now activate on per-group/channel-source flags ALONE, not gated by the old global switch. Existing users who had per-group epg_match_enabled or epg_channel_source_enabled set while keeping the global switch OFF would have matching silently activate on upgrade. Migration: if old (vestigial) settings.epg_match_enabled = 0, clear epg_channel_source_enabled and all event_epg_groups.epg_match_enabled. Bump schema_version 73->74.
close_reason: Added v74 migration _migrate_v74_preserve_epg_match_offstate: if old vestigial global epg_match_enabled was OFF, clear epg_channel_source_enabled + per-group epg_match_enabled to preserve off-state across the global-switch removal. Bumped schema_version 73->74. 3 migration tests + fixed 5 version-pin assertions. Also dropped '(current)' from the stream-mode dropdown label per user. Verified on dev DB (global was ON -> correctly no-op). 856 tests pass.

title: Move EPG matching settings to Event Groups tab as its own Card
description: EPG program-data matching settings (header, attach/detach buffers, channel-source + process-as mode + groups picker, Xtream fallback + cache) currently live inside the Output Settings card on the Settings → EPG tab. Move the whole block to the Settings → Event Groups tab as a standalone Card titled 'EPG Program Matching', since EPG matching is a per-group concern. Frontend-only (Settings.tsx).
close_reason: Moved EPG program-data matching settings from Settings→EPG (Output Settings card) to Settings→Event Groups as its own 'EPG Program Matching' Card, between Event Matching and Exception Keywords. Frontend-only move (verified in browser, no duplication, EPG tab clean). Docs relocated: section moved epg.md->event-groups.md with pointer, guide location refs updated.

title: Polish PR #231: default-output live detection + route layer helper
description: Follow-up to merged PR #231 (@bttfw). (1) find_next_live_window missed Teamarr default output: default xmltv_categories=['Sports'] and live flag off, but detection required <live> tag or 'Sports Event' category, so next_live_window was always empty on stock setups. Now recognizes 'sports'/'sport' categories and reports is_live from the actual <live> tag. (2) Moved inline team_epg_xmltv SELECT from the route into database.teams.get_team_xmltv helper (layer separation).
close_reason: Default-output live detection (recognize 'sports' category, report is_live from actual <live> tag) + moved inline xmltv query to database.teams.get_team_xmltv. Fixed date-fragile endpoint tests (now use live-now fixtures). 851 tests pass.

title: Dispatcharr-as-source: process-as-channels mode (global cascading menu)
description: Add global setting (e.g. epg_channel_source_mode: 'streams'|'channels') surfaced as a cascading menu under 'Use Dispatcharr as a source'. 'streams' = current _fetch_channel_source_streams behavior (one candidate per inner stream). 'channels' = group by DP channel, emit ONE representative stream per channel (e.g. primary/first stream id) tagged with the channel's EPG tvg_id, so the channel's internal stream lineup is never fragmented. Scope: GLOBAL. Touches schema.sql settings, settings/epg.py, Settings.tsx, event_group_processor.py:_fetch_channel_source_streams, tests.
close_reason: Added global epg_channel_source_mode ('streams'|'channels'). 'channels' emits one representative stream (lowest id) per DP channel in _fetch_channel_source_streams so its lineup isn't fragmented; 'streams' keeps current per-stream behavior. Cascading menu under 'Use Dispatcharr as a source'. Schema column (reconciled), full settings plumbing, 2 new tests, docs. Verified in browser + API round-trip.

title: Remove global EPG-match switch; per-group control only
description: Remove the global epg_match_enabled toggle (Settings → EPG, 'Match streams using Dispatcharr EPG data'). EPG matching becomes always-available; the sole control is each event-group's epg_match_enabled. Backend reads of the global flag to update (event_group_processor.py: system-group sync ~658, per-group index gate ~1645, dedup ~1271, entry ~1548). Settled: child features (channel-source, buffers, Xtream) un-nest from the removed switch. Keep the column for back-compat or migrate; decide during impl.
close_reason: Removed global epg_match_enabled switch. EPG matching always available; sole control is per-group epg_match_enabled. Removed global gate from consumer (system-group sync, index gate, dedup path), settings plumbing (types/read/update/models/routes), and frontend toggle (un-nested buffers/channel-source/Xtream). DB column left vestigial. Docs updated (epg-matching.md, settings/epg.md, CLAUDE.md). 851 tests pass, frontend builds, verified in browser + PUT round-trip.

title: EPG source/matching UX restructure (per-group control + channel-source modes)
description: Two related changes to the EPG Settings area: (1) remove the global epg_match_enabled kill switch — EPG matching capability is always available, opt-in stays per-event-group; (2) add a global cascading menu under 'Use Dispatcharr as a source' to choose process-as-streams (current: individual inner streams) vs process-as-channels (one representative stream per DP channel, leaving inner streams untouched).
close_reason: EPG source/matching UX restructure complete: 3lp1.1 (removed global EPG switch, per-group control) + 3lp1.2 (process-as-channels mode, cascading menu).

title: Footer easter egg: 'never support curling' line
description: Add an italic line to the app footer (MainLayout.tsx): 'Jesse, Teamarr will never support curling 🥌'. Frontend-only.
close_reason: Added italic footer line in MainLayout.tsx; verified rendering via Playwright

title: Deleting a custom league orphans its team_cache/league_cache rows
description: Lifecycle asymmetry found while verifying eqz.4: create_custom_league_and_refresh scope-caches teams into team_cache + league_cache, but delete_custom_league only removes the leagues row. The cached teams (team_cache WHERE league=code) and the league_cache row (league_slug=code) are left behind until the next FULL cache refresh wipes/rebuilds. Harmless to matching (no active league references them) but a leak. Fix: have the custom-league delete path scope-delete team_cache + league_cache for that code, mirroring _save_league_teams. Verified live 2026-06-11: deleting tst.sup left 16 team_cache rows + 1 league_cache row.
close_reason: Custom-league delete now scope-purges team_cache (WHERE league=code) + league_cache (WHERE league_slug=code) in the same transaction as the leagues-row delete, mirroring _save_league_teams. New purge_league_cache_rows() in database/leagues.py; delete_custom_league() logs purged counts. Test asserts both caches empty for the code + unrelated league untouched. 855 tests pass.

title: Sport guardrails for custom leagues
description:
A custom league's sport drives which matcher + event_type logic runs. Free-text or unsupported sports route through the wrong pipeline and silently produce broken output. Guardrails are mandatory (maintainer-flagged).

THREE GUARDRAILS:

1. SPORT ALLOWLIST (not free text). Restrict custom-league sport to the FUNCTIONALLY-SUPPORTED set, NOT the full sports table. The sports table has 17 rows but only 13 are functional (used by leagues + have matcher support): australian-football, baseball, basketball, boxing, cricket, football, hockey, lacrosse, mma, rugby, soccer, softball, volleyball. EXCLUDE tennis/golf/racing/wrestling — they are placeholder/roadmap sports (epics mf7/1tz/h31) with NO working matcher; a TSDB league under them would generate broken channels. Backend must reject a sport outside the functional allowlist (400), and the UI dropdown only offers functional sports. Source the allowlist from a single canonical place so it auto-grows when tennis/golf/etc. ship.

2. TSDB SPORT CROSS-CHECK. The test-fetch/validation step (eqz.3) must read TSDB's strSport for the entered idLeague (lookupleague.php) and confirm it maps to the user-selected sport. Reject mismatches (e.g. user picks 'soccer' but TSDB says 'Cricket') — prevents mislabeling that routes a league through the wrong matcher/event_type. Derive sport from TSDB rather than fully trusting user input.

3. EVENT_TYPE CONSISTENCY. event_type is constrained to {team_vs_team, event_card} (the only values in use). Combat/individual sports (boxing, mma) default to event_card; team sports to team_vs_team. UI should derive a sensible default by sport and constrain the choice rather than free-form.

Enforced in BOTH the write path (eqz.2) and validation endpoint (eqz.3).

title: Frontend: custom-league UI (premium-gated, test-fetch UX)
description:
UI for managing custom TSDB leagues.

Deliverables:
- Entry point (issue suggests Event Groups tab) showing a list of custom leagues (is_custom=1) with Add Custom League button + edit/delete.
- Add/edit form: league_code, provider_league_id, provider_league_name, display_name, sport, event_type, tsdb_tier. Provider is fixed to TSDB (no dropdown — TSDB-only constraint).
- PREMIUM GATE: when custom_leagues_enabled=false (no premium key), the feature is locked/hidden with a clear explainer ('Custom leagues require a TheSportsDB premium key — add one in Settings'). Non-negotiable.
- TEST-FETCH UX: a 'Test' button that calls the test-fetch endpoint and shows real sample events before the user can save. This is the core UX, not optional polish.
- Help text on how to find TSDB idLeague (provider_league_id) and exact strLeague (provider_league_name) from a thesportsdb.com league URL.
close_reason: Custom Leagues UI: dedicated page (route + Event Groups entry button, mirrors Detection Library). Premium-gated (locked card when capability.enabled=false). List table of is_custom=1 leagues with edit/delete. Add/edit dialog: code/id/name/display + sport dropdown (functional sports from capability) + event_type + tsdb_tier; league_code immutable on edit. Test-Fetch UX shows live TSDB league name/sport/sample events + name_matches + off-season warning before save; allow_empty override checkbox. Added GET /leagues/custom list endpoint + list_custom_leagues DB helper. Playwright-verified end-to-end against real backend + live TSDB: empty state, dropdown, gating, bad-id 400 toast, good-id (EPL 4328) result panel, full create->list->delete cycle (DB left clean). Frontend build clean. NOTE: premium-gate locked state not visually exercised (dev DB has a key) — covered by conditional + backend capability test. User-facing docs deferred to eqz.6.

title: Live TSDB test-fetch / validation endpoint
description:
THE bead that determines whether this feature reduces support load or increases it. The #1 failure mode (#217/#218/#219) is a wrong provider_league_name -> silent 404 / no_event_found.

Deliverables:
- Endpoint (e.g. POST /leagues/test-fetch) taking provider_league_id + provider_league_name (+ date/season), hitting TSDB live via the existing TSDB provider, returning sample events (or a clear 'TSDB returned nothing — check the exact league name' message).
- Used by the UI before save AND re-run server-side on create as a validation guardrail (block save if zero events resolve, with an override-with-warning path for genuinely off-season leagues).
- Premium-gated (403 without key).
- Surface which endpoint resolved (eventsday by name vs eventsnextleague by id) to help users debug name vs id mistakes.
close_reason: Live test-fetch validator: lookupleague(id) resolves identity, validate_tsdb_sport_matches cross-checks sport, eventsnextleague(id) returns sample events. Premium-gated (403). Wired into create_custom_league as the authoritative server-side guardrail — blocks save on zero events with allow_empty override for off-season. name_matches flag (typed name vs canonical strLeague) + resolved_via surface the id-vs-name debugging the bead wanted. SCOPING: did not add a literal eventsday-by-name fallback path; the id-based path is reliable and name_matches covers the debugging need — can be a follow-up if real id/name confusion shows up. Raw TSDB client methods (lookup_league_raw/get_events_by_date_raw/get_next_events_raw) added uncached. Tests cover gate, unresolvable id, sport mismatch, empty-ok, key passthrough, create guardrail + override + cross-check.

title: League write service + CRUD routes (TSDB-only, is_custom flag)
description:
Add the write path the leagues table currently lacks (database/leagues.py is read-only from the frontend today).

Deliverables:
- Add is_custom INTEGER DEFAULT 0 column to leagues table in schema.sql (reconciliation auto-adds on upgrade; no migration block needed per CLAUDE.md). Distinguishes user rows from built-ins.
- Service methods in a service/consumer layer (NOT in the route — CLAUDE.md layer rule): create_custom_league / update_custom_league / delete_custom_league.
- Routes POST /leagues, PUT /leagues/{code}, DELETE /leagues/{code}.
- HARD CONSTRAINTS: reject any provider != 'tsdb' (400). Edits/deletes allowed ONLY on rows where is_custom=1 (404/403 otherwise) so built-ins can never be mutated/deleted via API. All routes 403 when no premium key (from gate bead).
- Required fields: league_code, provider_league_id (TSDB numeric idLeague), provider_league_name (exact strLeague), display_name, sport, event_type; tsdb_tier optional.
- Guard against colliding with a built-in league_code on create.
- Tests for: non-tsdb rejection, built-in mutation rejection, premium gate, restart survival.
close_reason: Write path: is_custom column + DB helpers (insert/update/delete custom rows, all guarded by is_custom=1) + service create/update/delete with full policy (premium gate, TSDB-only, sport/event_type guardrails, code-collision guard, built-in protection 404/403) + POST/PUT/DELETE routes. Restart-survival test confirms novel codes persist across schema re-seed. 55 tests.

title: Premium-gate capability check (backend + shared signal)
description:
Foundational. Single source of truth for 'can this user use custom leagues': settings.tsdb_api_key is set/non-empty (get_tsdb_api_key in teamarr/database/settings/read.py:235). This presence is what makes the TSDB client premium (providers/__init__.py:80 -> client.is_premium).

Deliverables:
- A backend capability flag (e.g. extend an existing settings/capabilities GET response, or add GET /leagues/custom/capability) returning { custom_leagues_enabled: bool } driven solely by premium-key presence.
- ALL custom-league write routes (create/edit/delete/test-fetch) must hard-check this and return 403 when no premium key. Gate lives in the service layer, not just the UI.
- No new column needed for the gate itself.

title: [epic] Custom Leagues via UI (TSDB-only, premium-gated)
description:
Closes #230. Let users add custom leagues through the UI with zero code changes and no redeployment, retiring the manual league-request loop (#220-229 are all TSDB soccer).

NON-NEGOTIABLE CONSTRAINTS (per maintainer):
1. TSDB provider ONLY. Reject any non-tsdb provider at the API layer. ESPN/hockeytech/mlbstats/supabase are out of scope — they have path-style ids, auto-discovery, or provider-specific quirks.
2. Feature is HARD-GATED behind a TSDB premium key. The entire feature (UI + write routes) is locked unless settings.tsdb_api_key is set. Rationale: TSDB free tier is unreliable for arbitrary leagues (eventsnextleague returns ~5 events/day, lookupteam broken, lookuptable limited to featured soccer) — gating on premium avoids turning every custom league into a support ticket.

ARCHITECTURE (already verified feasible):
- League system is fully DB-driven; TSDB provider reads provider_league_id + provider_league_name straight off the leagues row (tsdb/client.py:394-423). No hardcoded league lists.
- Restart-safe: novel league_code rows survive (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS no-op; single INSERT OR REPLACE only touches built-in codes; reconciliation only ADDs columns; the lone DELETE FROM leagues is a one-shot v73 migration on hardcoded MiLB codes).
- Premium gate signal: get_tsdb_api_key(conn) non-empty -> client.is_premium True.

THE REAL RISK: the #1 failure mode across #217/#218/#219 is a wrong provider_league_name -> silent 404 / no_event_found. A custom-league form that just writes a row moves the support burden from 'file an issue' to 'why is my league empty' — worse, because it looks like a Teamarr bug. The MVP's center of gravity MUST be a live test-fetch against TSDB before save.

title: #219: TSDB match fails — no_event_found despite reverse-alias resolving league candidates
description:
Reported in #219 (v2.6.0). TSDB-sourced soccer leagues (swe.3.n / swe.3.s 'Ettan'). Teams classify correctly, REVERSE_ALIAS resolves team1/team2 to the right league candidates ({'swe.3.n'}), but TeamMatcher returns [FAILED] reason=no_event_found and 0/4 matched.

Key clue from reporter: team alias rows are stored with provider='espn' even though the teams only exist via the TSDB provider (consistent across other TSDB leagues incl. uru.2). TSDB /eventsday.php?l=4674 confirms events ARE available for the date with matching idHomeTeam/idAwayTeam. So events exist and aliases resolve, but the event lookup keyed by provider likely mismatches (espn alias provider vs tsdb event provider). Investigate provider-keying in the event index / alias storage path so TSDB league aliases carry provider=tsdb (or make event lookup provider-agnostic within a league).
notes: ROOT CAUSE (verified by code trace): swe.3.n/swe.3.s are hand-inserted custom leagues — NOT in schema.sql (zero Swedish leagues) and no UI/API exists to add custom leagues. Chain: (1) custom league can't be in a group's subscription via UI → is_group_league=False in matcher._prefetch_events. (2) matcher.py:491 forces cache_only=True for TSDB when not is_group_league. (3) nothing warms that cache — prewarm_tsdb_leagues only runs in the TEAM path (team_processor.py:327), never event groups. (4) so swe.3.n events never enter all_events; the events present (no_event_found log at team_matcher.py:1001 fires only when list non-empty) come from other known leagues. (5) reverse-alias candidate filter checks against search_leagues=ALL known leagues (matcher.py:801), so it passes swe.3.n, then filters all_events to swe.3.n → empty → 0 matched. provider='espn' on aliases is a RED HERRING — matcher keys aliases by (alias,league) only, never reads alias.provider. NOT a matcher logic bug. Latent separate gap: non-subscribed TSDB leagues in event groups never get event-cache warmed (by design best-effort), but official TSDB leagues work fine when subscribed (cache_only=False → API fetch).
close_reason: Not a bug — unsupported hand-added custom leagues (swe.3.n/swe.3.s). Verified not in schema.sql/dev DB/docs/code; only Svenska Cupen ships. Custom leagues require code changes in multiple places; directed reporter to submit a league add request. GH #219 closed not-planned.

title: Export/import stream ordering settings

Under the 'Use Dispatcharr channels as an EPG source' toggle (Settings.tsx ~2730), when ON reveal a multi-select picker 'Select Dispatcharr Groups to include' populated by the new available-DP-groups endpoint. Save selection to epg_channel_source_groups. Label: 'Leave empty to include all groups (slower).' Reuse CheckboxListPicker (searchable multi-select) per memory. This same selection drives the sorting dropdown in the sibling bead.
