Reused existing channelGroupsQuery (/dispatcharr/channel-groups) + CheckboxListPicker (ids<->names), saving to epg_channel_source_groups via full-object updateEPG. Helper text explains perf scoping + the sort-rule link. Docs: settings/epg.md row + guide channel-source section. Build OK.

title: Dispatcharr Group stream-ordering rule + dropdown + tooltip
description: Tweak 2 ordering. Add 'dispatcharr_group' to VALID_RULE_TYPES (types.py:176; value-required, NOT in NO_VALUE_RULE_TYPES). Implement _match_dispatcharr_group in stream_ordering.py comparing the stream's persisted dispatcharr_channel_group(_id) against the rule value. Frontend StreamOrderingManager.tsx: add rule type 'Dispatcharr Group'; populate its value <Select> from the saved epg_channel_source_groups selection (resolved to names); add a RichTooltip Info-icon helper mirroring the home/away (team_feed) pattern at lines 470-491. Docs: settings/channels.md stream-ordering section.
close_reason: Dispatcharr Group ordering rule. Backend: persist dispatcharr_channel_group on managed_channel_streams (schema col + add_stream_to_channel allowlist + ManagedChannelStream + 3 call sites thread stream.dp_channel_group / preserved on keyword moves); 'dispatcharr_group' added to VALID_RULE_TYPES (value-required); _match_dispatcharr_group (case-insensitive, NULL never matches) + dispatch. Frontend: rule type in RULE_TYPES + unions, value <Select> populated from selected DP groups resolved to names (getSettings + getDispatcharrChannelGroups), RichTooltip helper mirroring home/away. +3 ordering tests. 771 pass, build OK. Docs: channels.md + epg-matching.md.

title: Scope DP channel-source to selected Dispatcharr groups (backend)
description: Tweak 2 backend. Add epg_channel_source_groups setting (list of DP channel group ids, default empty=all). Add an API endpoint to list available Dispatcharr channel groups for the picker (client.m3u.list_groups -> /api/channels/groups/). In _fetch_channel_source_streams (event_group_processor.py:1214-1315): when selection non-empty, filter candidates to those whose channel_group_id is in the selection BEFORE the expensive EPG-resolution/matching — the perf win (skip undesired groups). Persist each channel-source candidate's dispatcharr_channel_group(+id) onto its managed_channel_streams row (new column, reconciliation handles) so the ordering layer can match it. Empty selection = include all (2.6.0 back-compat).
close_reason: Added epg_channel_source_groups setting (JSON list of DP channel-group ids, default []=all) across schema/types/models/read/update + dedicated & combined /settings routes (update.py now JSON-encodes list/dict values). _fetch_channel_source_streams now scopes candidates to the selected DP channel groups (ch.channel_group_id), checked early to skip EPG-resolution/matching for undesired groups (the perf win); each candidate also stashes dp_channel_group_id/dp_channel_group for the sorting rule (ybt.3). Reused existing GET /dispatcharr/channel-groups for the picker (no new endpoint). +2 tests. 768 pass. UI picker + docs land in ybt.4.

title: Configurable XC EPG cache TTL (default 24h)
description: Tweak 1. Today fetch_xtream_programs uses a hardcoded ttl_seconds=6*3600 (epg_xtream.py:184/202), per-M3U-account disk cache (data/epg_cache/xtream_acct{id}.xml, mtime-based freshness). Make it user-configurable: add epg_xtream_cache_hours int setting (default 24) across EPGSettings types.py/models.py/read.py/update.py + settings/epg.py route, plumb it from event_group_processor (_add_xtream_epg_fallback ~1668) into fetch_xtream_programs as ttl_seconds=hours*3600. Frontend: reveal a 'Cache for (hours)' number input under the 'Fall back to Xtream (XC) provider EPG' toggle (Settings.tsx ~2750), mirroring the attach/detach buffer reveal pattern. Docs: settings/epg.md + guide/epg-matching.md Xtream section.
close_reason: Added epg_xtream_cache_hours (int, default 24) across schema.sql, settings types/models/read/update, /settings/epg route + combined /settings route; threaded through event_group_processor._build_epg_index -> _add_xtream_epg_fallback -> fetch_xtream_programs(ttl_seconds=hours*3600), replacing the hardcoded 6h. Frontend: reveal 'Cache for (hours)' input under the XC fallback toggle. Docs: guide/epg-matching.md + settings/epg.md. 766 tests pass, frontend build OK.

title: EPG matching config refinements (XC cache TTL + DP group scoping)
description: Two user-requested tweaks to the 2.6.0 EPG matching features. (1) Make the XC provider-EPG fallback cache TTL user-configurable (default 24h) with a reveal sub-field under the toggle. (2) Let users SELECT which Dispatcharr channel groups to include when using DP channels as an EPG source — scoping the candidate scan (perf win: skip matching work for undesired groups), and surfacing those selected groups as a new 'Dispatcharr Group' stream-ordering rule type with a hover helper. Design (2026-06-06, mz83 thread): 'Use DP as source' toggle reveals a cascading multi-select of DP groups; selection scopes the fetch AND populates the sorting dropdown. Empty selection = include all (backward-compatible with 2.6.0).
close_reason: Both tweaks shipped to dev. (1) Configurable XC EPG cache TTL (ybt.1). (2) DP channel-source group scoping + Dispatcharr Group sorting rule (ybt.2/3/4): select which DP channel groups to scan (perf win) and sort by them. 771 tests, ruff clean, frontend builds.

title: #218: uru.2 still fetches from ESPN despite TSDB-only routing
description: Follow-up to #203/#217. The #203 fix (3636cd9) configured uru.2 as TSDB-only and excluded it from ESPN dynamic soccer discovery. But @dbelcham's logs (2026-06-06, after a full cache clear + regen) show ESPN still fetching it: 'teamarr.providers.espn.client:170 | [FETCH] soccer/uru.2/scoreboard'. The ESPN-discovery exclusion does not prevent the per-league scoreboard fetch at generation time — provider-selection logic for a TSDB-only league is not being honored on the fetch path. Investigate where the scoreboard fetch resolves its provider; the exclusion likely only filters the discovery/cache-refresh list, not the live fetch. Repro: subscribe uru.2, clear caches, regenerate, grep logs for 'soccer/uru.2/scoreboard'.
close_reason: Root cause: ESPN.supports_league() dotted-soccer discovery heuristic ('.' in league -> True) ran before honoring the DB provider assignment, so ESPN (priority 0) claimed uru.2 at fetch time despite provider=tsdb. Fix: decline a league explicitly mapped to a different provider in the leagues table before the dot fallback; discovered leagues (not in table) still resolve via the heuristic. 6 regression tests.

title: EPG matching i18n: localize program category gate (non-English categories)
description:
Point 3 of i18n hardening. _CLASSIC/_NON_EVENT in epg_matcher.py classify_program_policy() are hardcoded English ('classic sport event', 'sports non-event'). Non-English EPG categories fall through to ATTEMPT — fails SAFE (a non-game won't extract two in-window teams), so the only real cost is non-English REPLAYS (Classic) not being filtered and possibly mis-matching a live event.

BLOCKED: we must not guess category strings. Need real samples of what Dispatcharr/XMLTV actually emit for non-English feeds (Spanish/Portuguese/etc.) before hardcoding an alias map. Reporter gave plausible examples (Deportes, Evento deportivo, Deportivo en directo) but unconfirmed. Resume once we have validated category strings from an actual non-English program guide.
close_reason: Won't-do for now. Reported Spanish categories are all live-event labels that already pass the gate (ATTEMPT); the only behavior-changing fix (skipping non-English replays/non-events) needs real Spanish CLASSIC/NON_EVENT category strings we don't have and likely won't get. Gate fails safe today. Reopen if a replay-labeled non-English category surfaces from an actual guide.

title: Recognize Spanish gender markers (femenino/masculino/(F)) in classifier
description: Point 4 of i18n hardening. _WOMENS_KEYWORDS/_MENS_KEYWORDS and the team-name gender-marker stripper only handle English (W)/(M)/Women/Men. Add Spanish femenino/femenina/(F) -> women's and masculino/masculina -> men's. Extend both the umbrella-league narrowing regexes and the team-name cleaning so 'España Femenino' narrows to women's leagues and the marker is stripped from the team name.
close_reason: Extended gender detection + parenthetical stripping for Spanish/Portuguese femenino/femenina/feminino/(F)/masculino/masculina; English 'feminine' and 'Menorca' guarded against false matches; 10 tests

title: Strip live-broadcast prefixes (DIRECTO/EN DIRECTO/LIVE) before team extraction
description: Point 2 of i18n hardening. Leading live-status tokens are not stripped, so they leak into team1: 'DIRECTO España - Inglaterra' -> team1='DIRECTO Espana'. Not just Spanish — a bare leading 'LIVE ' isn't stripped today either (only the parenthetical (Live) marker is). Fix: strip a small set of leading live-status tokens (live, directo, en directo, en vivo, ao vivo, vivo, direkt, etc.) before team extraction in the classifier. Add classifier tests with Spanish + English strings.
close_reason: Strip non-English live-status prefixes (DIRECTO/EN DIRECTO/EN VIVO/AO VIVO/DIRETTA/DIREKT) in normalize_stream; English 'live' excluded to avoid Live Oak-style collisions; 15 tests; docs updated

title: Expose custom separators in Detection Library UI
description: Point 1 of i18n hardening. Adding hyphen to GAME_SEPARATORS globally is too risky (false splits). Backend already supports user-defined separators end-to-end (detection_keywords table, /api/v1/detection-keywords CRUD, get_separators() merge) — only the UI tab is missing. Detection Library deliberately dropped separators with a stale comment claiming it 'moved to Custom Regex section in Event Group form', which never happened. Fix: add 'separators' to TAB_ORDER in DetectionLibrary.tsx and correct the stale comment. CRUD form already gates target field on has_target=false, so no special-casing needed.
close_reason: Exposed separators tab in Detection Library; whitespace-preserving keyword prep; verified end-to-end with hyphen separator

title: EPG matching i18n hardening (non-English EPG sources)
description: Discord report: non-English (Spanish) EPG sources silently fail to match. Four root causes: (1) hyphen separator + no UI to add custom separators, (2) live-broadcast prefixes (DIRECTO/EN DIRECTO/LIVE) not stripped, (3) EPG program categories English-only, (4) gender markers femenino/masculino not recognized.

title: Import screen: expose 'Team stream source' + 'EPG program matching' toggles at import time
description: User request: add the per-group 'Team stream source' (team_streams_enabled) and 'EPG program matching' (epg_match_enabled) toggles to the initial Event Group IMPORT screen, so users can set them during bulk import instead of importing then editing each group. Backend BulkGroupSettings already carries epg_match_enabled (api/routes/groups.py ~L323); verify/add team_streams_enabled. Frontend: frontend/src/pages/EventGroupImport.tsx (already has bulkEPGMatch -> epg_match_enabled at ~L228); add UI controls for both toggles in the import flow and wire team_streams_enabled through bulk create. Tag for next dev.

title: EPG matching: filter candidates to Dispatcharr-mapped streams when channel-source mode on (backend)
description: When the global channel-source toggle is on, restrict the candidate stream list feeding EPG matching to streams assigned to a Dispatcharr channel with an epg_data_id (tier-1 of resolve_program_tvg_ids). Seam: event_group_processor._build_epg_index / _fetch_streams — intersect group streams with get_stream_channel_map(). Confirm semantics with user first (filter-existing-group vs full channel replacement). Add tests.
notes:
DESIGN LOCKED (2026-06-04, paused mid-build for a regression): implement DP-channel EPG source as a PERSISTED, system-managed event group (NOT in-memory) because XMLTV (_generate_xmltv/_store_group_xmltv), stats (create_run/update_group_stats), and FK constraints (processing_runs.group_id -> event_epg_groups) are all per-group. Plan:
1. settings: epg_channel_source_enabled bool — full stack (schema.sql settings ~L180; settings/types.py EPGSettings; settings/read.py BOTH readers ~L147 & ~L388; settings/update.py field_mapping; api/routes/settings/models.py EPGSettingsModel; epg.py GET+PUT; __init__.py combined GET; frontend api/settings.ts + Settings.tsx toggle next to xtream fallback ~L2730).
2. event_epg_groups: add is_channel_source BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0 (reconciliation auto-adds). dataclass EventEPGGroup + _row_to_group + create_group param/INSERT.
3. groups.py ensure_channel_source_group(conn, enabled): idempotent upsert of system group name='Dispatcharr Channels', is_channel_source=1, epg_match_enabled=1, skip_builtin_filter=1, team_streams_enabled=1, duplicate_event_handling='consolidate', leagues=[] (uses global subscription via _get_subscription_leagues), m3u_group_id NULL. enabled mirrors toggle.
4. process_all_groups: read setting, call ensure_channel_source_group(enabled). It then flows through the loop normally.
5. _fetch_streams: branch if group.is_channel_source -> _fetch_channel_source_streams(): get_stream_channel_map() + get_epg_data_list() (active non-_Teamarr) + EXCLUDE Teamarr-managed dispatcharr_channel_ids (managed_channels) + m3u.list_streams() for detail; emit candidate dict per stream on a qualifying channel with tvg_id = the CHANNEL's epg_data_id tvg_id (drives index + tier-1 resolve). Teamarr channels are OUTPUT not INPUT — exclude.
6. UI groups list route (api/routes/groups.py) EXCLUDE is_channel_source from the list (honor one-tick-box; keep out of Event Groups UI).
7. tests: candidate builder (mock client), settings parity; docs: epg-matching.md + settings/epg.md. Additive — runs alongside M3U-group EPG matching; consolidation attaches to existing event channels or creates via global/per-league/per-event defaults.

title: Settings: add global 'Use Dispatcharr channels as EPG source' toggle (UI)
description: Add a global boolean setting (e.g. epg_channel_source_enabled) + a Switch in Settings.tsx inside the epg_match_enabled block, sibling to the Xtream fallback toggle (~line 2730). Wire into EPGSettings type (frontend/src/api/settings.ts) and the settings GET/PUT. Column add on settings table (reconciliation auto-adds). Helper text: 'Match only streams already assigned to a Dispatcharr channel (use its curated EPG) instead of every stream in the provider group. Applies to all EPG-matched groups.'

title: Dispatcharr-channels as EPG-match source
description: From Discord (mz83/Sarge/seth): today EPG matching pulls EVERY stream in a flagged provider M3U group and resolves each via the cascade (channel epg_data_id > direct tvg_id > name > XC fallback). Users want to restrict candidates to streams they've actually curated onto Dispatcharr channels — 'just the ones I actually have mapped elsewhere'. mz83: 'one tick box option'. UI decision (2026-06-04): GLOBAL toggle in Settings -> EPG under the master switch, sibling to the XC fallback toggle (all EPG-matched groups). Likely semantics: when ON, candidate streams for each EPG-matched group are filtered to those assigned to a Dispatcharr channel (tier-1 channel mapping in resolve_program_tvg_ids), instead of the whole provider group. Needs design confirmation before coding. Seam: event_group_processor._fetch_streams / _build_epg_index; resolver already prefers the channel mapping. Stream dict shape unchanged downstream.
notes: Pipeline anchors (from Explore): _fetch_streams event_group_processor.py:1148-1187; _build_epg_index :1456-1544; resolve_program_tvg_ids epg_resolver.py:75-174 (tier1=channel epg_data_id); get_stream_channel_map channels.py:714-737 (stream_id->channel dict w/ epg_data_id+streams[]); EPGProgramIndex.build epg_index.py:49-124. UI: Settings.tsx ~2730 next to epg_xtream_fallback_enabled.
close_reason: Shipped in v2.6.0 (commit 6d8363d). Both children complete: 183.10 (global 'Use Dispatcharr channels as EPG source' toggle in Settings -> EPG) + 183.11 (backend candidate filter to Dispatcharr-mapped streams, hidden system group, dedupes against existing EPG-match groups). Stale-open epic; closing now that 2/2 children are done and the feature is in the released product.

title: EPG Matched: fold into Stream Type select (UI-only)
description: Remote tester suggestion: roll the epg-matched stream-ordering rule into the existing Stream Type select rather than keeping it as a separate top-level rule type. EPG-matched is a provenance/source classification (came via EPG-guide resolution), a peer of team/event streams on the same 'how was this stream recognized' axis. Ordering is first-match-wins by priority so nothing is lost. Implement as UI translation layer: drop epg_match from top-level rule-type dropdown, add 'EPG matched stream' to Stream Type options; map to/from backend {type:'epg_match'} on save/load. No backend change, no migration.

title: Docs sweep: EPG resolution cascade + XC provider-EPG fallback
description: Follow-up docs pass for the EPG resolution rework shipped in 8932e74 (epic crs). Already done: docs/guide/epg-matching.md (cascade table channel->direct->name->XC, Xtream fallback section, requirements/troubleshooting) and docs/guide/settings/epg.md (toggle row). Remaining to review/update: (1) README — EPG matching isn't in the features list at all; decide whether to add EPG matching + XC fallback; (2) docs/guide/settings/channels.md mentions EPG match — verify still accurate; (3) docs/reference/architecture/consumer-layer.md + dispatcharr-layer.md — document the new epg_xtream.py module + active-source filter + cascade reorder; (4) docs/guide/event-groups/creating-groups.md EPG-match mention; (5) add a short 'regional sports networks / Xtream EPG' example; (6) consider a screenshot of the new toggle. Verify any count/feature claims across docs.

title: Settings toggle: XC provider EPG fallback (on/off)
description: Gate the Xtream/XC EPG fallback (cascade layer 4) behind a global on/off setting. Copy: 'For EPG matching, a valid stream-to-EPG mapping is required in Dispatcharr. As a backup, for XC (Xtream) providers, Teamarr can independently fall back on the provider XC EPG data.' Default OFF (opt-in: it fetches ~100MB provider xmltv per XC account per generation). Wire: settings column epg_xtream_fallback_enabled, settings read/write + API, gate _add_xtream_epg_fallback on it, frontend toggle in Settings -> EPG, docs.

title: Normalize heterogeneous EPG program text in build_match_input
description: EPG sources format the matchup differently. Jesmann (DP): title='MLB Baseball', sub_title='Pirates at Astros'. Infinity/Xtream: title='MLB Baseball : New York Mets at Seattle Mariners LIVE-superscript', sub_title=''. build_match_input pipe-joins title+sub_title so classify_stream strips the leading league hint — but only works when the matchup is in sub_title. For inline-':'/dash-separated titles with empty sub_title the league prefix folds into team1, and unicode superscript decorations (LIVE/NEW) pollute the trailing team. Fix in build_match_input (single chokepoint for ALL EPG sources): strip unicode modifier/superscript decoration chars from both segments; when sub_title is empty, convert the first ' : '/em-dash/en-dash separator in the title into the pipe boundary so the prefix becomes a strippable hint. Must not change DP-format behavior (no ':' / no decorations -> no-op). Add tests; verify existing epg_matcher tests stay green.
close_reason: build_match_input normalization (strip superscript decorations; split inline ':'/dash league prefix when sub_title empty). Validated live on Infinity xtream titles. Tests added (5).

title: Tests + docs for Xtream EPG fallback
description: Unit tests: detection, URL builder, iterparse filtering by channel+window, cache behavior, cascade precedence (layers 1-3 win over xtream). Update docs/guide/epg-matching.md with the new layer + Xtream auto-EPG behavior.

title: Wire Xtream EPG as cascade layer 4 in _build_epg_index
description: After direct/channel/name resolution, collect streams still unresolved whose account is Xtream. Fetch their provider XMLTV programmes (bead c2) and merge into EPGProgramIndex keyed by stream tvg_id, so the matcher + anchor gate path is unchanged. Add resolver stat 'xtream'. Must NOT override layers 1-3 (only fills gaps). Per-account batching (one fetch per Xtream account, not per stream).
close_reason: Wired Xtream EPG fallback into _build_epg_index as cascade layer 4. Reordered resolver to channel→direct→name per user spec; added active-source filter (name/direct only against enabled imported EPG, _Teamarr excluded). Live result: index coverage Inf Ent 21→155, Inf Sports 26→119, MSX Ent 31→74, MSX Sports 32→77 tvgs. 165 EPG matches (was ~20), incl RSNs DP couldn't resolve (Detroit SportsNet, BravesVision, Arizona Family) all live-aligned (Δ=0-15m). Audit clean, no exceptions.

title: Fetch + streaming-parse + cache provider XMLTV
description: Stream-fetch the provider xmltv.php and iterparse (ElementTree.iterparse) filtering programmes to (a) a set of wanted channel ids = unresolved stream tvg_ids and (b) the match window. Avoids holding 99MB/308k programmes in memory. Return dict[tvg_id -> list[program-dict]] shaped like DispatcharrProgram (start/stop/title/sub_title). Cache parsed result per account with hours TTL (service_cache or in-memory). Handle gzip, HTTP errors, timeouts gracefully (return empty -> degrade to no-match).
close_reason: parse_xmltv_programs + fetch_xtream_programs implemented; 14 tests pass; validated on real 99MB Infinity XMLTV (1.0s, correct channel/window filtering, builds DispatcharrProgram). Finding: this provider puts matchup in <title> after ' : ' with empty sub-title + unicode Live/New decorations — relevant to crs.3 matching.

title: Xtream detection + xmltv.php URL builder
description: epg_xtream.py: is_xtream_account(account)->bool (account_type=='XC' and server_url+username+password present). xmltv_url(account)->str building '{server_url}/xmltv.php?username=..&password=..' (normalize trailing slash). Pure, unit-tested. Source for account fields: DispatcharrConnection m3u accounts (server_url/username/password/account_type).
close_reason: epg_xtream.is_xtream_account + xmltv_url implemented, 8 tests pass, verified against real accounts

title: Xtream source-matched EPG fallback layer
description: Add a resolution-cascade layer (level 4, just above future fuzzy) for streams that don't resolve against Dispatcharr's curated EPG. When the stream's M3U account is Xtream Codes (type=XC), Teamarr fetches the provider's own xmltv.php — channel ids are source-matched to the M3U tvg_ids (live-verified: Infinity EPG uses ESPN.us/USANetwork.us/FoxSports1.us, exactly the stream tvg_ids), giving an exact direct mapping. Authoritative for coverage but patchier sports overrides than a dedicated guide, hence ranked below direct/channel/name and above fuzzy. Teamarr-side fetch (not added to DP) keeps the user's guide clean. Live context: Infinity resolves 1/149 against DP guide; its own xmltv has 14777 channels / 308521 programmes incl WNBA/college team titles.
close_reason: All children shipped (8932e74): Xtream XC provider-EPG fallback live + toggle + docs + tests

title: EPG resolver: disambiguate multi-feed names by program-count probe
description: After prefix-stripping (yke), the dominant remaining resolution gap is ambiguous_name: a normalized channel name (e.g. 'espn') maps to several distinct EPGData tvg_ids — genuinely different feed variants (base / HD / 4K / +/ Alternate / Pacific) across EPG sources — so the resolver refuses to guess. Live-observed: Infinity US Sports has 58 ambiguous names (ESPN/FS1/TNT/TBS/MLB Network...). Empirically, exactly ONE candidate per name carries real guide data; the rest are empty phantom catalog rows (espn: ESPN HD=32 programs, base/Alt/+/4K=0-5; fs1: FS1 HD=28, rest 0; tbs: TBS HD=71, rest 0). Fix: when a name is ambiguous, probe program counts in the match window for each candidate and resolve to the strict-max (count>0). Self-correcting (never binds an empty guide), no source-priority guessing.
design: Add EPG manager count_programs(tvg_id,start_before,end_after)->int (page_size=1, read DRF 'count'). Resolver: add optional disambiguate callback (name,candidates)->tvg|None; on ambiguous names call it, resolve via new stat 'name_probed' if it returns a winner, else keep skipping. Keeps resolver pure/testable (inject fake). _build_epg_index supplies a window-bound, memoized disambiguator. Strict-max; tie or all-zero -> skip. Bound probe count + log cost.
close_reason: Rejected by user: heuristic 'winner by program count' adds per-gen probe calls and guesses. Superseded by Xtream source-matched EPG fallback (new epic) which gives exact tvg_id alignment instead.

title: EPG resolver: strip country/region grouping prefixes for name matching
description: Lift EPG-match resolution coverage. Providers like Infinity prefix every stream with a country grouping label (e.g. 'US: ESPN FHD', 'UK | Sky Sports'). normalize_channel_name keeps the 'us'/'uk' token, so 'US: ESPN' -> 'us espn' never matches the EPGData catalog name 'espn'. Result: name-cascade resolution = 0 for such providers (live-observed: Infinity US Sports resolved 1/149 vs MSX 32/101). Fix: strip a leading <country-code> + delimiter (':' or '|') prefix before normalization. Must NOT strip identity words: 'USA Network' (no delimiter) keeps 'usa'. Delimiter-anchored allowlist keeps it safe. NOTE: big-name channels (ESPN/FS1/TNT) will still hit the ambiguous-name guard (multiple EPG sources) even after prefix strip — that disambiguation is a separate downstream bead.
design: Add delimiter-anchored country-code prefix strip as the FIRST step in normalize_channel_name (operate on lowercased string while ':'/'|' still present, before punctuation->space). Allowlist of region codes (us/usa/uk/ca/au/nz/ie/ire/fr/de/es/it/nl/pt/be/ch/no/se/dk/fi/br/mx/ar/in/gr/al/tr/bg/cz/pl/eu/intl/latam/ex-yu/exyu). Applies symmetrically to stream + EPGData names (no-op on undelimited names). Add tests.
close_reason: Shipped in 8932e74: country/region prefix strip in normalize_channel_name; Infinity name resolution 0->25

title: EPG window anchored to wrong program occurrence for series games
description: When a linear channel's EPG guide has the same title on multiple nights (playoff series: 'Knicks at Spurs' Game1/Game2, or replays), _match_via_epg emits a result per program and the window can anchor to a program ~2 days from the event start. Observed: event 2026-06-04 00:30 UTC, attach_at 2026-06-05 08:00 (program slot 06-06 08:00-10:00). Stream is out-of-window when it should be live. Need to disambiguate: pick the program slot nearest the event start_time.
notes: Approach (user-chosen): absolute-nearest-time. Thread program.start_dt as an anchor_dt into the team matcher; rank same-team candidate events by abs(event.start - anchor), tolerance-bounded; bypass match cache for anchored (EPG) matches to avoid same-title collisions. Fold in encore handling: when multiple programs match one event, keep the result whose program start is nearest the event start (live broadcast), drop encore duplicates.

title: STREAM_AUDIT false-positive on windowed EPG streams
description: generation.py _run_stream_audit builds expected set from get_channel_streams (NOT window-gated) and compares to Dispatcharr actual streams (window-gated push). Out-of-window EPG time-shared streams always appear as mismatches -> misleading WARNING logs. Reconciliation already uses get_ordered_stream_ids correctly. Fix: audit should use get_ordered_stream_ids as expected set, mirroring reconciliation.
close_reason: Already fixed in 59cb885 (t5e): _run_stream_audit now uses get_ordered_stream_ids (window-gated set), mirroring reconciliation. Commit is in main.

title: Remove back-to-back buffer clipping from EPG stream windows
description: User decision: drop clipping entirely. Attach/detach buffers should drive the window unclipped, even if a linear stream is attached to multiple overlapping event channels at once. Remove clip_before/clip_after from compute_stream_window, the epg_clip_before/after fields on match outcomes/results, their computation in matcher._match_via_epg, propagation in event_group_processor, the call site in lifecycle/service, and clip-specific tests. Update epg-matching.md (remove clipping language).
close_reason: Removed clipping entirely: compute_stream_window no longer takes clip args, epg_clip_before/after fields dropped from outcome+result, matcher no longer computes neighbour bounds, event_group_processor/service call sites updated, docs updated. Buffers now apply unclipped; overlap accepted.

title: Recompute attach/detach window every generation for already-attached EPG streams
description: EPG time-shared stream attach_at/detach_at are computed once at attach time (service.py:589) and never recomputed. Changing epg_stream_pre/post_buffer_minutes does not take effect on already-attached streams until they detach+reattach. User wants the window recomputed every generation run. Fix: in the consolidate existing-stream path, refresh the stored window from the fresh EPG program slot + current buffers. Reconciliation already enforces membership via get_ordered_stream_ids, so no manual re-push needed.
close_reason: Added update_stream_window(); consolidate existing-stream path now recomputes attach/detach from fresh EPG slot + current buffers every generation run

title: EPG matching: preview path drops tvg_id so EPG matches never appear in UI preview
description: event_group_processor.preview_group builds stream dicts as {'id','name'} only (line ~542), omitting tvg_id. _build_epg_index requires s.get('tvg_id'); with none present tvg_ids is empty -> index None -> EPG matching silently skipped. The production process path (line ~1163) includes tvg_id/tvg_name/channel_group/etc. Result: a user who enables EPG matching and clicks 'Preview stream matches' sees zero EPG matches — preview is misleading. Fix: include tvg_id (and tvg_name) in preview's stream dicts to mirror production. Blocks UI-based verification of the feature.
close_reason: Fixed in EPG matching overhaul: cascade resolver (tvg_id namespace bridge, no channel requirement), naive-datetime fix, pipe-join build_match_input, preview tvg_id. Verified live (19 matches). 685 tests pass.

title: Stream ordering predicate for time-shared linear (EPG) streams
description: Add a stream-ordering rule predicate (e.g. stream_origin: epg|name, or a 'linear' rule type) so users can sort time-shared linear/EPG-matched streams relative to dedicated event streams. Discriminator: managed_channel_streams.attach_at IS NOT NULL (time-windowed) or match_method=EPG. Builds on PR #216 stream sorting (sethwv) and epic 183 EPG matching. Today linear streams order only via name/m3u/group/catch_all rules (generic name 'ESPN' gives no distinct handle). Depends on 183.5 (attach_at column) + 183.4 (EPG match).
close_reason: Closed as REDUNDANT — already covered by the existing epg_match ordering rule. Goal was to let users sort time-shared linear streams distinctly from dedicated streams. Verified at matcher.py:668-670 that every MatchMethod.EPG assignment ALSO sets epg_program_start/end -> attach_at/detach_at, so match_method='epg' <=> time-windowed linear, always (name matches are the full-life ones). The epg_match rule (stream_ordering.py:251-257) keys on match_method=='epg', which therefore already selects exactly the time-shared linear streams — surfaced in the UI as the 'EPG matched stream' Stream Type option (djl). The proposed attach_at-based discriminator would split EPG matches into windowed vs non-windowed, but no non-windowed EPG matches exist in practice, so it adds no real capability. Reopen only if a future path produces full-life EPG matches.

title: Tests + docs for EPG program-data matching
description: Tests: search_programs client (mocked HTTP + 404 feature-detect); bulk index + overlap lookup; matcher emitting one-stream→many-events with per-program windows; time-windowed membership reconciliation (attach in-window, remove out-of-window, RE-ATTACH later same day); back-to-back buffer overlap clipping; dedicated-stream full-life path unchanged. Docs per CLAUDE.md: new matching/EPG + linear-stream-time-sharing guide page (how it works, opt-in, buffers, 'channel exists for EPG but stream only swaps in near game time', cadence granularity caveat), settings page, CLAUDE.md architecture note.
notes: DOCS MUST INCLUDE (do not neglect): (1) minimum Dispatcharr version dependency — endpoint confirmed on 0.24.0, feature-detected, graceful degradation on older builds; (2) per CLAUDE.md Documentation Updates table: new guide page for linear-stream EPG matching/time-sharing, settings page for the toggle+buffers, CLAUDE.md architecture note, docs/reference/architecture/dispatcharr-layer.md (already updated in 183.2 with DispatcharrProgram + search_programs + version note). Cross-check any count/version claims.
close_reason: Tests + docs complete. Coverage audit: search_programs success + 404 feature-detect, bulk index + half-open overlap lookup, one-stream->many-events fan-out with per-program windows, dedicated full-life path unchanged — all covered. Closed the one PARTIAL gap (checklist #5) with test_same_stream_rotates_across_channels_one_day: a single physical stream rotating across 3 event channels in a day (attach->detach->re-attach, incl. a midnight-spanning window). Docs comprehensive (guide/epg-matching.md: opt-in default, buffers, Dispatcharr 0.24.0 + feature-detect, cadence-granularity caveat, ordering rule; settings/epg.md + settings/channels.md cross-linked; CLAUDE.md architecture note present); bumped guide docs_version 2.5.7->2.6.0. Note: bead's 'back-to-back buffer clipping' test item is obsolete — removed in 2.6.0 (6qx/e89c966); a test asserts the attribute no longer exists. 763 tests pass.

title: Settings/UI: enable toggle + attach/detach buffers + window source
description: Add epg_match_enabled (default OFF, opt-in) gated on feature-detection (b2). Add buffer settings for time-windowed membership: pre_attach and post_detach (minutes/hours), and window-source choice (use EPG program start/end vs Teamarr event start/end). Decide per-sport vs global with the user. Hide/disable toggle with tooltip when connected Dispatcharr lacks the endpoint. Schema: add columns to settings (reconciliation handles). Update DispatcharrSettings type + read.py + settings UI with a short explainer of linear-stream time-sharing.
notes: BEHAVIORAL GOTCHA (caught via user challenge): epg_match_enabled must set skip_builtin (like team_streams_enabled) at event_group_processor filter setup — static-named linear streams (ESPN, NBA1) have no vs/@ separator and get dropped by the placeholder/require_event_pattern filter BEFORE matching. Without skip_builtin the EPG matcher never sees them and matches nothing. Fixed: skip_builtin = skip_builtin_filter OR team_streams_enabled OR epg_match_enabled. NOTE: route-plumbing mirroring of the two flags in groups.py IS correct (both per-group bools); but matcher-param wiring is NOT mirrored (epg uses epg_index, gated on global+endpoint). Lesson: a new per-group flag needs BOTH plumbing review AND filter/matcher behavioral review — they differ.
close_reason: Shipped in v2.6.0. Global master toggle epg_match_enabled (default OFF, opt-in) + attach/detach buffers epg_stream_pre_buffer_minutes/epg_stream_post_buffer_minutes (default 60) live across backend (settings types/read/update/routes/models) and surfaced in Settings -> EPG; per-group epg_match_enabled toggle in EventGroupForm + bulk EventGroupImport. Feature-detected on Dispatcharr 0.24.0 (graceful off on older builds). Design decisions resolved: GLOBAL toggle + global buffers + per-group enable (not per-sport); window source resolved to always use the EPG program start/end window (no separate setting — that's the purpose of EPG matching). Covered by test_settings_epg_route_parity; documented in docs/guide/settings/epg.md + guide/epg-matching.md.

title: Time-windowed stream membership (attach X before / remove X after; shared across events)
description:
THE headline mechanism. Make a matched stream's membership in a channel TIME-WINDOWED instead of static-for-life, so one shared linear stream rotates across many event channels.

Per (channel, stream) compute an attach window = [event_or_program_start - pre_buffer, event_or_program_end + post_buffer]. On EACH generation run, reconcile: if now in window -> ensure stream attached (correct priority); if now outside -> ensure stream removed. The same stream_id is concurrently attached to whichever event channel is currently in-window, and absent from the others.

DB/lifecycle work: (1) store stream-attach/stream-detach times per managed_channel_streams row, distinct from channel create/delete; (2) rework the terminal soft-delete (removed_at) into a re-evaluatable membership state so a stream can be attached -> removed -> re-attached across the day; (3) extend reconciliation/drift so time-window-driven add/remove isn't seen as external drift; (4) clip overlapping buffers for back-to-back events on the same linear channel (avoid wrong-game during overlap); (5) keep DEDICATED single-event streams on full-life attachment (only shared/multi-event streams time-window). Window source (program time vs event time) per product decision. Depends on matcher emitting per-program windows (b4).
notes: STREAM ORDERING (user Q 2026-06-01): EPG/linear streams already flow through compute_stream_priority_from_rules at attach (service.py:917) keyed on name/m3u/group+catch_all, and get_ordered_stream_ids still ORDER BY priority,added_at — window gate only filters active set, doesn't change ordering. GAP: no rule predicate can target a stream BECAUSE it's a time-shared linear/EPG stream (rules key on generic name 'ESPN'). Clean discriminator now exists: attach_at IS NOT NULL (or match_method=EPG). Follow-up bead created for a 'stream_origin: epg|name' ordering predicate so users can place linear feeds relative to dedicated streams. NOT blocking core 183.5.
close_reason: Time-windowed stream membership complete. Window columns + gate model (user-confirmed): attach_at/detach_at on managed_channel_streams (NULL=full-life). compute_stream_window() = EPG program slot +/- global buffer, CLIPPED to neighbour programs (back-to-back wrong-game avoidance). get_ordered_stream_ids() window-aware (active=removed_at NULL AND (attach_at NULL OR attach_at<=now<detach_at)); reconciliation drift uses window-gated 'expected'. epg_program_start/end + clip bounds threaded match->lifecycle. epg_stream_pre/post_buffer settings. removed_at stays terminal. 20 windowing/clip tests, full suite 669 green, docs updated. Default-off until 183.6 wires EPG matching. Ordering predicate split to 183.8.

title: Matcher: EPG-title matching for linear channels (one stream → many events)
description: In consumers/matching, use Dispatcharr EPG program TITLE as a matching signal so traditional LINEAR channels become matchable. For each stream carrying a tvg_id, look up overlapping programs (from b3 index) and run each program title through the EXISTING classify_stream -> TeamMatcher pipeline (EPG title is just a better input string; reuse all extraction/league logic). CRITICAL: a single linear stream can legitimately match MULTIPLE events in a day (ESPN -> 3-5 events) — do NOT collapse to one match; emit one (stream,event,program_window) match per overlapping program. Add MatchMethod.EPG; thread through MatchOutcome/MatchedStreamResult; carry the program start/end times on the match so the lifecycle layer (b5) can build attach/detach windows. Reconcile EPG vs name match by confidence; record which signal won.
notes: WHEN A STREAM HAS BOTH name-match AND EPG-match (user Q 2026-06-01): need a reconciliation policy. Cases: (a) both agree on same event -> match, record both signals fired (highest confidence). (b) disagree -> pick by confidence/method precedence; record which signal won + the loser for debuggability (epic says 'Reconcile EPG vs name match by confidence; record which signal won'). (c) name matches but EPG doesn't (stream named for a game but guide is generic/filler) -> keep name match (current behavior preserved). (d) EPG matches but name doesn't (linear 'ESPN' name unmatchable, but program title 'Cubs at Cardinals' matches) -> THE headline win; EPG unlocks the match. KEY: stream-name match = stream matched to ONE event for its whole life; EPG match = one stream -> MANY events across the day (per overlapping program). These are different membership models — a stream that name-matches a single dedicated event stays dedicated (full-life attach, 183.5); a linear stream that only EPG-matches gets time-windowed. When both: if name match is a dedicated single-event stream, prefer dedicated semantics; if the tvg_id shows program multiplicity (linear), prefer EPG time-windowing even if name also matched. Decision must be explicit in matcher + carried into lifecycle (183.5).
close_reason: EPG-title matching capability complete. StreamMatcher consumes injected EPGProgramIndex: title+sub_title through shared classify_stream->TeamMatcher (extracted _route_to_outcomes, reused by name+EPG paths), category gating (classic>non-event>event precedence, absent=attempt+fallback), one-stream-many-events fan-out, MatchMethod.EPG + program windows threaded through MatchOutcome/MatchedStreamResult, _reconcile_epg (linear=EPG wins, dedicated=name wins). Reuses TeamMatcher cache (free per-program memoization) — dropped redundant compute_program_fingerprint. 25 EPG tests, full suite 653 green, ruff clean, docs updated. Live wiring (build+inject index for opted-in groups) is 183.6.

title: Bulk program pre-fetch + local tvg_id→programs index
description: Once per generation run (not per-stream — avoid N+1), bulk-fetch programs overlapping the target date window via search_programs(start_before=<window_end>, end_after=<window_start>, page_size=500) paginated. Build an in-memory index: tvg_id -> sorted list of (title, sub_title, start_time, end_time). Provide lookup(tvg_id, event_start, event_end) returning programs whose [start,end] overlaps the event window. Scope the fetch to only tvg_ids present in the candidate stream set when possible. Cache for the run.
notes: SCOPING (HARD REQUIREMENT, per user 2026-06-01): fetch EPG ONLY for tvg_ids carried by candidate streams in IMPORTED EVENT GROUPS — NOT every Dispatcharr channel. Flow: collect candidate streams from imported event groups -> keep those carrying a tvg_id (Teamarr already carries it on stream dicts, event_group_processor.py:1163) -> distinct tvg_id set -> fetch programs for just that set in the date window -> index. API CONSTRAINT (probed live): /api/epg/programs/search/ takes ONE tvg_id per call (comma list returns 0; repeated param = last-wins, NOT OR). So scope via N calls, one per distinct tvg_id (N = small set of linear channels in event groups, NOT per-stream) — no unscoped window sweep in production. channel_id filter also single-value.
close_reason: Added EPGProgramIndex (consumers/matching/epg_index.py): scoped per-run fetch (one call per distinct event-group tvg_id, NOT whole instance), _Teamarr exclusion, tvg_id index + half-open overlap lookup. Added categories to DispatcharrProgram (custom_properties.categories). 10 index tests + dataclass test; full suite 628 green; ruff clean. Architecture docs updated.

title: Add EPGManager.search_programs() client method + feature-detection
description: Add search_programs() to teamarr/dispatcharr/managers/epg.py wrapping GET /api/epg/programs/search/. Support params: tvg_id, start_before, end_after, title, channel_id, epg_source, page_size, fields, page. Use existing paginated_get pattern. FEATURE-DETECT: probe once (e.g. HEAD/GET with page_size=1) and cache a 'programs_search_supported' flag; on 404 mark unsupported so callers degrade gracefully (older Dispatcharr versions lack this endpoint). Return typed results (new DispatcharrProgram dataclass in dispatcharr/types.py).
close_reason: Added EPGManager.search_programs() + supports_program_search() feature-detection + DispatcharrProgram dataclass. Live-verified on Dispatcharr 0.24.0. 11 unit tests, full suite green (618), ruff clean. Architecture docs + version dependency documented.

title: Spike: live-validate /api/epg/programs/search/ + stream→tvg_id→program linkage
description: Authenticated probe of /api/epg/programs/search/ on a real instance before design lock. Confirm: (1) a Dispatcharr STREAM exposes a tvg_id that joins to programs; (2) for a real LINEAR channel (e.g. ESPN), pull a full day of programs and verify we get clean per-program start/end times + descriptive sports titles (team names / 'A vs/at B') — these times become the attach/detach windows; (3) quantify how many distinct events a busy linear channel carries per day (validates the time-windowing use case); (4) response fields returned; (5) behavior for streams with no EPG. Deliverable: data-shape doc + confirm window source (program time vs event time) is viable + go/no-go. BLOCKS the rest.
design: GO. Endpoint /api/epg/programs/search/ live-confirmed: DRF-paginated (count/next/previous/results), page_size+page+start_before+end_after+tvg_id+title params work. Program object embeds channels[] AND streams[] each with id/name/tvg_id/m3u_account (join key=tvg_id); clean ISO8601-Z back-to-back times usable as attach/detach windows; epg_source field lets us exclude our own _Teamarr programs. Busy linear channels 30-66 programs/day; multiplicity LINEAR/DEDICATED gate viable; no-EPG=HTTP200 count0 (graceful). CRITICAL: naive at/vs title regex is ~100% false positives (news/movies) - matcher MUST route titles through existing classify_stream->TeamMatcher pipeline per 183.4. Full findings: plans/spike-183.1-epg-programs-findings.md
notes: Spike must also: confirm we can reliably classify a tvg_id as LINEAR (multiple distinct programs/day) vs DEDICATED (single program), since that gate decides who gets time-windowed. Capture a real ESPN-style day to validate program multiplicity + clean per-program times.
close_reason: GO. Endpoint live-validated; data-shape doc + go/no-go delivered. Unblocks 183.2.

title: EPG Program-Data Matching
description:
Match streams to sports events using Dispatcharr EPG program data (GET /api/epg/programs/search/), AND time-window shared linear streams across multiple event channels.

CORE GOAL (clarified): A traditional linear channel (ESPN, ESPN2) airs 3-5+ events in a day. We want ONE such stream to serve MANY event channels, but only be ATTACHED to each event's channel for a window (X before start .. X after end), then removed — so it's free to serve the next event and so tuning event A's channel never shows event B's game.

WHY THIS FITS (not the #209 inversion): Channels stay 1:1 with events — stable identity, stable tvg_id, own generated EPG/filler. ONLY stream membership becomes time-windowed. Teamarr already reconciles channel stream lists every generation run (_safe_update_channel); we add a time-window predicate to the membership decision. This is far lighter than #209 (which inverts channel identity: one channel, many events sequentially).

EPG DATA IS THE ENABLER: EPG search tells us ESPN airs 'Alabama at Auburn' 1-4pm and 'Lakers at Celtics' 8-11pm. We (a) match each PROGRAM to an event (linear channels are unmatchable by stream name today), and (b) use the program's start/end as the precise attach/detach window per event.

KEY CHALLENGES: (1) stream attach/detach window is SEPARATE from channel create/delete window — new per-(channel,stream) scheduled times; (2) membership must become re-addable/re-removable over time (today soft-delete removed_at is terminal) — a stream is attached, removed, re-attached as it rotates; (3) attach/detach granularity is bounded by generation cadence (~runs every N min); (4) back-to-back events on the same linear channel can have overlapping buffers — clip to avoid wrong-game during the overlap; (5) classify shared-linear (multi-event EPG) vs dedicated single-event streams (keep current full-life attach). 

Today Teamarr matches stream NAME only; tvg_id/tvg_name carried on every stream dict (event_group_processor.py:1163) but ignored. Membership is static per run (managed_channel_streams), filler is Teamarr's own XMLTV. Requires a NEW Dispatcharr endpoint — feature-detect + opt-in setting. Relates to closed #145 and repeated 'match EPG not stream name' requests.
notes: VERSION DEPENDENCY (confirmed 2026-06-01): /api/epg/programs/search/ requires a newer Dispatcharr build — confirmed working on Dispatcharr 0.24.0 (via GET /api/core/version/). Never hard-required: all callers gate on EPGManager.supports_program_search() (404->disabled) and degrade gracefully. Documented in docs/reference/architecture/dispatcharr-layer.md; user-facing version note + settings tooltip land in 183.6/183.7.
close_reason: Epic complete — EPG Program-Data Matching shipped in v2.6.0. All 13 children closed: client+feature-detect (183.2), bulk index+overlap (183.3), matcher one-stream->many-events (183.4), time-windowed membership (183.5), settings/UI toggle+buffers (183.6), tests+docs (183.7), ordering predicate redundant/closed (183.8), Dispatcharr-channels EPG source sub-epic (183.9), plus spikes/bugfixes (183.1/183.12/4c9/6z1/npz).

title: PR #204: Fix Supabase doubleheader score-merge collision
description: In SupabaseLeagueClient.build_score_map, scores are keyed on (game_date, home_city). Baseball doubleheaders (two games, same date, same home team) collide — second game overwrites the first, so _merge_scores attaches the wrong score (or same score to both schedule entries). Fix: extend key to (game_date, home_city, away_city), or use game.id if schedule_game_overrides and games share IDs. Verify with Seth whether CBL schedules doubleheaders.
close_reason: Fixed in 5cc0e34: build_score_map/_merge_scores now join on game_number (exact int, matches schedule_game_overrides.game_number) instead of (date, home_firstword). Fixes doubleheaders AND a latent Chatham-Kent silent-miss. Live CBL probe: 100% current-season coverage. 6 tests added.

title: Audit docs for teams ↔ Dispatcharr channel conflation
description: docs/guide/teams.md (already partially fixed in teamarrv2-liq) was built on the wrong premise that Teamarr creates a Dispatcharr channel per team. The actual model: teams produce XMLTV <channel> + programmes; Dispatcharr channels are only created from matched event streams (managed_channels, keyed on event_id, never team_id). Audit other docs pages — dispatcharr-integration, templates/team-vs-event, index, and anything else that touches teams — for the same drift, and fix.

title: Remove hallucinated 'Team Channels in Dispatcharr' section from teams docs
description: docs/guide/teams.md ends with a section claiming Teamarr creates and manages a Dispatcharr channel per team (with logos, channel groups, channel numbers, and idle persistence). This is fabricated — Teamarr only emits XMLTV programmes for teams; Dispatcharr channels are created from matched event streams (managed_channels is keyed on event_epg_group_id and event_id). Delete the section.

title: Add ChannelsDVR M3U refresh integration
description:
Parallel media-server hook alongside Emby/Jellyfin. Triggers PUT /providers/m3u/sources/<source_name>/refresh on the configured ChannelsDVR server after each EPG generation, so the m3u/EPG that Teamarr writes is picked up immediately by the user's DVR.

Scope:
- New backend client teamarr/channelsdvr/client.py (test_connection, trigger_m3u_refresh)
- New API routes teamarr/api/routes/settings/channelsdvr.py (GET/PUT settings, POST test)
- New schema columns: channelsdvr_enabled, channelsdvr_url, channelsdvr_username, channelsdvr_password, channelsdvr_source_name (reconciliation handles upgrade — no migration block needed)
- ChannelsDVRSettings dataclass + read/update functions
- Hook in teamarr/consumers/generation.py after Jellyfin step, non-blocking, populates result.channelsdvr_refresh
- Frontend: types/api/hooks/Settings.tsx card matching Emby+Jellyfin pattern
- Docs: extend any media-servers reference if present

Design decisions:
- Single source_name text input (not multi-select)
- Optional HTTP Basic Auth (username/password) for users behind a reverse proxy; default is no auth per ChannelsDVR docs
- Test connection hits GET /status (or fallback)

title: Add Jellyfin integration (#200)
description:
@YoshiKaiVR requests Jellyfin Live TV auto-refresh after EPG generation, parallel to existing Emby integration (epic teamarrv2-3ho).

User insight from Seth (JAYS): Emby and Jellyfin share a common API ancestor (Jellyfin forked Emby). The same endpoints often work; the only meaningful difference is auth header:
- Emby: ?api_key=<token> query param
- Jellyfin: X-Emby-Token: <token> header (also still accepts ?api_key for compat)

Implementation plan:
1. Survey existing Emby integration (settings, refresh hook, where it fires post-generation).
2. Decide architecture: parallel Jellyfin client vs. shared client with auth-style switch. Likely cleanest = shared base, two thin subclasses for auth.
3. Settings UI: add Jellyfin section mirroring Emby (URL, API key, enabled toggle).
4. Schema: new settings columns (or reuse pattern if Emby uses generic media-server settings).
5. Wire post-generation refresh hook to call Jellyfin /LiveTv/GuideInfo or whatever endpoint Emby currently hits.
6. Docs: add Jellyfin to Settings guide page; mention shared-API note.
7. Tests: refresh-call success and failure paths.
close_reason: Shipped on dev: Jellyfin integration parallel to Emby. Refactored EmbyClient with PATH_PREFIX (Emby keeps /emby/, Jellyfin uses server root). Added schema columns, settings dataclass, read/update helpers, Pydantic models, /settings/jellyfin + /jellyfin/test routes, refresh hook in generation.py. Frontend: combined Emby and Jellyfin into a single 'Media Servers' tab. Visual verification via Playwright. 4 jellyfin client tests added (518 total pass).

title: Separate event and filler XMLTV categories on templates (#199)
description:
Per #199 (@jesmannstl). Today templates have a single `xmltv_categories` list and a `categories_apply_to` gate that's either 'events' or 'all'. The user wants to add a category like 'Series' to filler programmes only (Emby compat) without affecting event categories.

## Changes
- Schema v72: add `xmltv_filler_categories JSON DEFAULT '[]'` to event_epg_templates and team_epg_templates; migrate `categories_apply_to='all'` templates by copying xmltv_categories → xmltv_filler_categories; drop `categories_apply_to` column
- Backend: dataclasses get xmltv_filler_categories, drop categories_apply_to. Filler generators read xmltv_filler_categories directly.
- Frontend: XMLTV tab gets two category cards (Event Categories, Filler Categories) side by side. Drop Apply Categories To dropdown.
- Flags and Video stay events-only (semantically correct for live broadcasts).
- safe_sql allowlist updated for new column.

## Acceptance
- Existing templates with apply_to='all' have filler categories matching event categories after migration
- Existing templates with apply_to='events' (default) have empty filler categories
- Setting filler categories independently emits correct `<category>` tags on filler programmes
- Removing all event categories doesn't affect filler

title: Add {broadcast_feed} and {broadcast_feed_team} EPG template variables (#195)
description:
Per #195 (@scoobwrx). Two new EVENT_ONLY template variables for EPG content when feed separation is active:

- `{broadcast_feed}` → 'Home Team Feed' / 'Away Team Feed' / '' (empty when no feed separation)
- `{broadcast_feed_team}` → 'Seattle Mariners Feed' / '' (empty when no feed separation)

## Why this isn't redundant
`{feed_home_away} Team Feed` and `{feed_team} Feed` look equivalent but render incorrectly when feed separation is off — the variable resolves to '' but the literal text orphans, leaving 'Team Feed' or 'Feed' alone. The new vars return '' as a unit so the whole phrase disappears.

## Design decisions
- Singular `broadcast_feed_team` (not `_teams`) for consistency with existing `feed_team` family
- No 'Network Feed' fallback — empty string when feed separation is off, so users can wrap in optional context if they want
- TemplateScope.EVENT_ONLY (won't appear in team template variable picker)

## Files to update
- `teamarr/templates/variables/home_away.py` — add 2 `@register_variable` blocks
- `tests/test_feed_separation.py` — add tests for both vars
- `docs/guide/templates/variables.md` — document new vars + update count
- Variable-count claims: `CLAUDE.md`, `docs/index.md`, `docs/reference/architecture/template-engine.md`, `docs/guide/templates/variables.md` — 205 → 207

title: Stream ordering priority input: row reorders mid-keystroke, blocking double-digit entry
description:
Issue #198 (@MrDynamo, v2.4.3). In Settings → Channels → Stream Ordering, typing a priority value re-sorts rows on every keystroke. Cursor stays in the input position visually but now belongs to a different rule, making double-digit entry impossible.

## Root causes
1. `StreamOrderingManager.tsx:267` uses `key={index}` where index is the sorted-array index, not a stable rule identity. React reuses DOM nodes positionally — when the priority change re-sorts, focus stays in the DOM at position N but that DOM now wraps a different rule.
2. The priority input commits on every keystroke (line 102-107). Even with stable keys, the row visually jumps mid-typing, which is jarring.

## Fix
- Stable client IDs on rules; key `RuleRow` by ID, not array index
- Defer priority commit until blur or Enter — use local string state in the priority input

## Test plan
- Manual: type "15" into a priority input that's currently 3 — should not reorder until blur
- Verify Enter commits and triggers sort
- Verify Save still works

title: Allow assigning M3U-sourced channel groups (toggle to bypass exclude_m3u filter)
description:
User jstevenscl in chat (3/23): they have a Dispatcharr channel group containing manually-added channels, but the group is also tagged as originating from an M3U provider. Teamarr currently hides any group with m3u_accounts populated, so they can't pick it for event-group channel assignment. mz83 (egyptiangio) confirmed: 'right now you can't, but i have gotten requests for this before, so may add a toggle'.

Backend already supports it: GET /api/v1/dispatcharr/channel-groups accepts exclude_m3u=false (teamarr/api/routes/dispatcharr.py:130). Default true. The hide logic is teamarr/dispatcharr/managers/m3u.py:128-129 (g.m3u_accounts truthy → filtered). No backend work needed.

Frontend work: add a 'Show M3U-sourced channel groups' toggle on the channel group picker(s). When enabled, fetch with exclude_m3u=false. Affects at minimum Settings.tsx:1017 (per-league config). Audit other pickers (event group settings, global defaults) and add the same toggle uniformly so behavior matches across the app.

Default: keep exclude_m3u=true (current behavior is the safer default — most users shouldn't see M3U groups). Toggle is opt-in for users who manually curate M3U-tagged groups.
acceptance_criteria: Channel group pickers in the UI have a 'Show M3U-sourced groups' toggle (default off). When toggled on, M3U-tagged groups appear in the dropdown and can be selected and saved. Persists per-picker or globally — implementer's call. All channel group pickers in the app behave consistently.

title: Wire team_filter_enabled master toggle (currently dead)
description:
The settings column 'team_filter_enabled' (schema.sql:204) is written by settings/update.py:426 and read into TeamFilterSettings at settings/read.py:173, but is never consulted by the consumer pipeline. grep 'team_filter_enabled' in teamarr/consumers/ returns zero hits. Effect: the UI toggle meant to disable team filtering entirely does nothing.

## Fix
- In event_group_processor.py::_filter_by_teams (line 1616), check settings.team_filter_enabled at the start. If False, return (matched_streams, 0) immediately — no filtering, full passthrough.
- Group-level override: consider adding subscription_team_filter_enabled to subscription_league_config if per-group control is desired; otherwise global-only is fine.
- Add tests covering the toggle on/off.
- Found during audit of #197 (2026-04-22). Unrelated to #197 itself but surfaced in the same code path.

title: Fix #159: NCAA Baseball import fails on channel_id collision
description:
## Problem
User imports NCAA Baseball teams → 'Import Failed: Internal Server Error' (#159).

## Root Cause
ESPN's API returns duplicate team names with different provider IDs in the same league. For example, college-baseball has:
- Team ID 322 → 'Boise State Broncos'
- Team ID 1139 → 'Boise State Broncos'
- Team ID 1153 → 'TBD' (placeholder)
- Team ID 1154 → 'TBD' (placeholder)

channel_id is generated from PascalCase(team_name) + '.' + league_id. Both Boise State entries produce BoiseStateBroncos.ncaabb. The teams table has UNIQUE on channel_id, so the second INSERT fails and the entire transaction rolls back → 500 error.

Affects 6 leagues: college-baseball (2 dupes), college-softball (8 dupes), mens/womens-college-volleyball (2 each), world-baseball-classic (2).

## Important: Two Categories of 'Duplicates'
1. **True duplicates** — same school, different ESPN IDs (Boise State 322 vs 1139, Clemson 1140 vs 529). Same name, same abbrev, same logo. ESPN data quality issue.
2. **Distinct teams with same display name** — different schools with same mascot name (softball: 'Tigers' = Benedict vs Campbell vs Stillman, 'Panthers' = Claflin vs Clark Atlanta). Different abbreviations. Both are real teams that need importing.

## Proposed Fix (in team_import.py)
1. **Filter TBD placeholders** — skip teams named 'TBD' or 'TBD TBD' before importing.
2. **Handle channel_id collisions** — when a generated channel_id is already taken (by existing DB row or earlier team in same batch), append the provider_team_id to make it unique (e.g., Tigers.ncaasbw → Tigers.ncaasbw.869). This correctly handles both true duplicates (harmless extra entry, user can delete) and distinct teams (properly separated).
3. Track used channel_ids in-memory during the import batch to catch within-batch collisions, not just existing DB rows.

## Files
- teamarr/services/team_import.py — _generate_channel_id and bulk_import_teams

title: Multi-sport hint support — sport hints can map to multiple sports
description:
Sport hints currently map one pattern → one sport. Ambiguous terms like 'football' (soccer vs American football) and 'main card' (MMA vs boxing) need to map to multiple sports.

PROBLEM:
- 'English Football League' triggers sport_hint='Football' (American football)
- Matcher rejects soccer events because 'Football' \!= 'Soccer'
- Built-in SPORT_HINT_PATTERNS has a dead code bug: bare \\bfootball\\b matches first as 'Football', soccer negative-lookahead pattern never reached

SOLUTION:
- Support str | list[str] for sport hint target values
- Store JSON arrays in existing TEXT column (backward compatible)
- Fix built-in patterns: bare 'football' → ['Soccer', 'Football']
- Frontend: tag/chip input for multi-sport, badges in table

ALSO FIXES:
- Combat sport keywords ('main card', 'prelims') can map to ['MMA', 'Boxing']
- Users can define their own multi-sport hints via Detection Library

Closes the dead-code bug in SPORT_HINT_PATTERNS (constants.py:478 vs 490).

title: Unify run history tables into shared RunHistoryTable component
Add generation timeout and network failure resilience
description: When the network goes down during generation, it spins indefinitely. Issues: (1) Dispatcharr client has 30s timeout x 5 retries = 150s per failed call, and EPG wait_for_refresh polling calls get_source() in a loop — a single poll can overshoot the 120s overall timeout. (2) No overall generation timeout or cancellation mechanism. (3) ThreadPoolExecutor for event fetching has no timeout on as_completed(). Fixes: Add timeout to as_completed(), reduce Dispatcharr retry count during polling, and add overall generation timeout with clean abort.

title: Remove per-group EPG refresh (redundant with end-of-generation refresh)
description: event_group_processor.py line 1099 calls _trigger_epg_refresh() inside _process_group_internal() for every group. generation.py already does a unified EPG refresh + channel association at lines 357-379 after all groups complete. The per-group refresh is redundant and multiplies latency (N groups x 120s timeout). Fix: remove the per-group _trigger_epg_refresh call. The _trigger_epg_refresh and _associate_epg_with_channels methods on the processor can also be removed since they're only called from there.

title: API — add sport/league primary filters, update ordering
description:
Update the channels API to reflect that sport/league are the primary organizational axes, not group.

Channels API (api/routes/channels.py):
1. Add sport query parameter to GET /channels/managed
2. Add league query parameter to GET /channels/managed (currently only client-side)
3. Change default ORDER BY from event_epg_group_id,channel_number to sport,league,channel_number
4. Keep group_id filter parameter (renamed conceptually to source_group_id in docs, same param name for backwards compat)
5. Update ManagedChannelModel: add field alias or comment clarifying event_epg_group_id is source provenance

New query flow:
  GET /channels/managed                         → all channels, ordered by sport/league/number
  GET /channels/managed?sport=football           → football channels only
  GET /channels/managed?league=nfl               → NFL channels only
  GET /channels/managed?group_id=5               → channels whose first stream came from group 5
  GET /channels/managed?sport=football&league=nfl → combined filters

CRUD additions:
1. get_managed_channels_for_sport(conn, sport) → list[ManagedChannel]
2. get_managed_channels_for_league(conn, league) → list[ManagedChannel]
3. Or: add sport/league params to get_all_managed_channels() for flexibility

Frontend API client (api/channels.ts):
1. Add sport and league optional params to listManagedChannels()
2. Update ManagedChannel interface comment for event_epg_group_id

Test: pytest API tests + verify new query params work correctly

title: Simplify cross-group enforcement + reconciliation
description:
With event-scoped uniqueness, cross-group enforcement is largely unnecessary. Channels are event-unique from creation — no post-hoc merging needed.

Cross-group enforcement (cross_group.py):
1. In consolidate mode: becomes a no-op. Channels are already event-unique, so there are no
   cross-group duplicates to merge. The module docstring already describes the exact scenario
   that can no longer happen: two groups creating channels for the same event.
2. In separate mode: each stream gets its own channel (primary_stream_id differs). Cross-group
   enforcement is still irrelevant — separate channels are expected.
3. Conclusion: the entire enforce() method can return early with empty result.
   Keep the module/class skeleton for now (remove in a future cleanup pass).
4. Update module docstring to explain why it is a no-op.

Reconciliation (reconciliation.py):
1. Remove group_ids filter from reconciliation endpoints — channels are event-owned, not group-owned.
   The filter was useful when channels "belonged" to groups; now it is misleading.
2. Keep reconciliation logic (orphan detection, drift detection) but remove group-centric framing.
3. Update detect() to iterate all channels, not per-group.
4. Update API parameter: remove group_ids query param from GET /reconciliation/status.

Event group processor (event_group_processor.py):
1. get_managed_channels_for_group() call at line ~1720 is for EPG output. This is the one place
   where group-scoping still makes semantic sense: "which channels have streams from this group?"
   However, with event-scoped channels, a channel may have streams from multiple groups.
2. Consider: should EPG output include ALL channels that have at least one stream from this group?
   Or all subscribed channels regardless of stream source? Likely the latter — EPG is subscription-scoped.
3. Refactor: replace get_managed_channels_for_group with a query that gets all active channels
   for subscribed leagues (sport/league filter, not group filter).

Test: pytest + verify reconciliation still detects orphans and drift correctly

title: Data dedup migration + event-scoped unique index
description:
Replace the group-scoped unique index with an event-scoped one. Requires migrating existing data that has duplicate channels for the same event across groups.

Current unique index:
  (event_epg_group_id, event_id, event_provider, COALESCE(exception_keyword, ""), primary_stream_id)

New unique index:
  (event_id, event_provider, COALESCE(exception_keyword, ""), primary_stream_id)

Data migration strategy (v63):
1. Find channels where (event_id, event_provider, exception_keyword, primary_stream_id) appears in multiple groups
2. For each duplicate set:
   a. Pick winner: channel with lowest event_epg_group_id sort_order (or earliest created_at as tiebreak)
   b. Move streams from losers to winner (INSERT into managed_channel_streams with source_group_id preserved)
   c. Soft-delete loser channels (mark deleted_at, delete_reason = "migration_dedup")
3. Verify zero remaining duplicates
4. DROP INDEX idx_mc_unique_event
5. CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_mc_unique_event ON managed_channels(event_id, event_provider, COALESCE(exception_keyword, ""), primary_stream_id) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
6. The old idx_managed_channels_group index stays (still useful as a filter index)

Edge cases:
- Channels with different exception_keywords for same event are NOT duplicates (separate channels)
- Channels with different primary_stream_ids for same event are NOT duplicates (separate mode)
- Deleted channels (deleted_at IS NOT NULL) are excluded from dedup
- Channels with no Dispatcharr ID: merge DB records only, no Dispatcharr sync needed
- Channels WITH Dispatcharr IDs: log but do NOT delete from Dispatcharr in migration (reconciliation handles that on next run)

Safety:
- Run in a transaction with rollback on any error
- Log every merge/delete for audit trail
- Verify no unique constraint violations before creating new index

Test: migration test with synthetic duplicates + pytest + verify new index prevents cross-group duplicates

title: CRUD + lifecycle — event-scoped channel lookups
description:
Change the primary lookup pattern from group-scoped to event-scoped. This is the behavioral core of the refactor.

Channel identity key: (event_id, event_provider, exception_keyword, primary_stream_id)
The group_id is NOT part of channel identity.

CRUD changes:
1. find_existing_channel() — remove group_id as required param, make it optional
   - Default: search by (event_id, event_provider, keyword, stream_id, mode) across ALL groups
   - Optional group_id filter retained for backwards compat during transition
2. Add find_channel_by_identity(conn, event_id, event_provider, keyword=None, stream_id=None, mode=consolidate) — the new primary lookup
3. get_all_managed_channels() — change ORDER BY from event_epg_group_id,channel_number to sport,league,channel_number
4. find_any_channel_for_event() — remove exclude_group_id param (no longer needed since we want ANY match)

Lifecycle service changes:
1. process_matched_streams: replace find_existing_channel(group_id=...) with event-scoped call
   - First call: find_channel_by_identity(event_id, event_provider, keyword, stream_id, mode)
   - If found: add stream (regardless of which group created the channel)
   - If not found: create new channel
2. REMOVE _handle_cross_group_overlap() entirely — the main lookup already finds channels from any group
   - Its functionality is subsumed by the event-scoped find_existing_channel
   - Cross-group overlap check was only needed because find_existing_channel was group-scoped
3. _create_channel: still passes group_id as source provenance (stored in event_epg_group_id)

Exception keyword handling:
- find_channel_by_identity respects exception_keyword in the lookup
- Streams with different keywords still get separate channels (correct behavior)
- Streams with no keyword can fall back to keyword channels (existing fallback logic preserved)

Separate mode:
- find_channel_by_identity in separate mode checks primary_stream_id
- Each stream still gets its own channel (correct behavior)
- The only change: no longer scoped to a specific group

IMPORTANT: This bead changes behavior BEFORE the unique index is updated. The application-level
lookups prevent duplicates. The old group-scoped unique index is still in place as a safety net.
No data migration needed yet.

Test: pytest with updated expectations + verify no duplicate channels created across groups

title: Schema migration — make event_epg_group_id nullable, change FK to SET NULL
description:
Make the column nullable and change ON DELETE CASCADE to ON DELETE SET NULL. A channel should survive the deletion of the group that initially supplied its first stream.

Schema changes (v62):
1. ALTER TABLE managed_channels — allow NULL for event_epg_group_id
2. Recreate FK as ON DELETE SET NULL (SQLite requires table rebuild for FK changes)
3. Update ManagedChannel dataclass: event_epg_group_id: int -> int | None
4. Update ManagedChannelModel pydantic: event_epg_group_id: int -> int | None
5. Update frontend ManagedChannel interface: event_epg_group_id: number -> number | null
6. Grep all code paths that assume non-null event_epg_group_id and add null guards

Key code paths needing null guards:
- crud.py: create_managed_channel still accepts int (the creating group passes its ID)
- crud.py: get_managed_channels_for_group still filters by group (valid filter)
- service.py: _create_channel still passes group_id (stream provenance)
- cross_group.py: iterates channels by group — needs null check
- channels.py API: groupLookup.get() already handles missing with fallback
- Frontend: groupLookup.get(channel.event_epg_group_id) needs null check

Migration strategy:
- Existing rows keep their event_epg_group_id values (no data change)
- New behavior: if a group is deleted, its channels get event_epg_group_id = NULL instead of being cascade-deleted

Test: pytest + frontend build + verify existing channels unaffected

title: Terminology sweep — rename parent group / owned by group language
description:
No behavior changes. Pure documentation/comment/log-message pass.

Changes:
1. Schema comment: Parent Group -> Source Group (which group supplied first stream)
2. Schema comment: Channel Settings (from group config) -> Channel Settings (from subscription)
3. CRUD docstrings: event_epg_group_id: Parent group ID -> Source group ID (stream provenance)
4. Lifecycle log: event_owned_by_other_group -> channel_exists_for_event
5. Lifecycle log: event owned by group X -> channel already exists for event
6. Cross-group docstring: update to reflect groups as stream sources, not owners
7. find_any_channel_for_event docstring: update group ownership language
8. find_existing_channel docstring: clarify group_id is for scoping, not ownership

Files: schema.sql (comments), crud.py (docstrings), service.py (logs), cross_group.py (docstrings)

Test: ruff check + pytest (no behavior change, all tests pass unchanged)

title: Channel Ownership Model — Decouple from Event Groups
description:
## Problem

After the Sports Subscription (ay2) and Profile Consolidation (mzt) epics, event groups are
agnostic stream providers — they no longer own sports, leagues, templates, profiles, or channel
groups. However, the channel layer still treats event_epg_group_id as the **ownership key**.

**The new reality:**
- Subscription owns sports/leagues
- Events (from providers) own channels
- Streams attach to channels from any group via matching + prioritization
- Event groups are just M3U stream sources

**Channel identity (the true ownership key):**
```
(event_id, event_provider, exception_keyword, primary_stream_id)
```
Where:
- event_id includes segment suffix for UFC (e.g., event123-prelims)
- exception_keyword creates separate channels per keyword (e.g., Spanish broadcast)
- primary_stream_id creates separate channels in "separate" mode

The group is NOT part of this key — it's stream provenance, tracked per-stream
in managed_channel_streams.source_group_id.

## Gaps

1. **Schema** — event_epg_group_id is NOT NULL with ON DELETE CASCADE + unique index scoped by group
2. **CRUD** — find_existing_channel() scoped by group_id, primary retrieval is get_managed_channels_for_group()
3. **Lifecycle** — _handle_cross_group_overlap() exists as a separate phase because lookups are group-scoped
4. **Cross-group enforcement** — entire module exists to fix post-hoc what should be prevented by event-scoped uniqueness
5. **API** — exposes event_epg_group_id as primary filter, orders by group
6. **Frontend** — "Group" column prominent in channels table, no Sport column
7. **Terminology** — "Parent Group", "event owned by group" throughout codebase

## Approach

Make lookups event-scoped BEFORE changing the DB index (application prevents duplicates first,
then index catches up). This ensures no moment where index and code disagree.

## Key files
- teamarr/database/schema.sql (managed_channels table + unique index)
- teamarr/database/channels/crud.py (find_existing_channel, get_managed_channels_for_group)
- teamarr/database/channels/types.py (ManagedChannel dataclass)
- teamarr/consumers/lifecycle/service.py (_handle_cross_group_overlap, _create_channel)
- teamarr/consumers/enforcement/cross_group.py (entire module)
- teamarr/consumers/reconciliation.py (group-scoped queries)
- teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py (EPG output per group)
- teamarr/api/routes/channels.py (API model + endpoints)
- frontend/src/pages/Channels.tsx (table columns, filters)
- frontend/src/api/channels.ts (ManagedChannel interface)

**Channel groups:** Remove channel_group_id/channel_group_mode from event_epg_groups. Add per-league channel_group config to subscription. Dynamic modes ({sport}, {league}) become a global or per-league setting.

**Pattern (from team filtering):**
- NULL = inherit global subscription
- Explicit value = override for this group only

**Scope:**
- Schema: add subscription override fields to event_epg_groups
- Database CRUD for group subscription overrides
- Consumer resolution: group override → global subscription fallback
- API: accept/return per-group subscription fields

- teamarr/database/schema.sql (event_epg_groups table)
