title: Investigate Feb 15 issue flood
Tiered Provider Model (BYOK TSDB)
description:
Two-tier provider support model for TSDB leagues.

Tier 1 (Free): ESPN, HockeyTech, MLBStats, and TSDB leagues where free API (key 123) is sufficient. Free tier limits: 5 events/day per league via eventsday.php, 10 teams via search_all_teams.php, 30 req/min.

Tier 2 (Premium BYOK): TSDB leagues with >5 events on busy match days. User provides their own TSDB premium API key in Settings. Premium: 100 req/min, 3000 teams/search, 3000 events/season.

Key design rule: ALL TSDB leagues must be tagged as tsdb_free or tsdb_premium in the schema. If a user provides a premium key, use it for ALL TSDB requests (even free-tier leagues) to get higher rate limits (100 vs 30 req/min). We still use the same endpoint methodology (eventsday.php etc) for simplicity — premium key just removes result caps and gives more headroom.

Cricbuzz deprecation: Cricket leagues currently use cricket_hybrid provider (TSDB teams + Cricbuzz events). With premium TSDB key, eventsday.php returns full cricket schedules. Deprecate cricbuzz provider and migrate cricket leagues to tsdb_premium tier. Users without premium key keep current behavior (free TSDB has limited cricket data).

Already shipped: s9n.1 provider hot-reload, s9n.2 key validation endpoint + UI validate button, premium rate limit fix (100 req/min not unlimited).

Free tier limitations (verified 2026-03-08): eventsday.php max 5 events/query, search_all_teams.php max 10 teams, eventsnext.php HOME only, 30 req/min.

All three numbering modes (strict_block, rational_block, strict_compact) and MANUAL mode become aware of external channels.

title: Compute external occupied channel set
description: Add function to compute set of channel numbers in Dispatcharr not managed by Teamarr. Called once at generation start from the already-warm channel cache. Returns set[int] of occupied numbers to pass through the numbering pipeline.

title: Channel number collision awareness (#146)
description: Overhaul channel numbering to be aware of existing Dispatcharr channels. Teamarr currently only checks its own managed_channels table when assigning numbers, causing collisions with user's existing IPTV/XC provider channels. Fix: compute external occupied set from Dispatcharr cache, skip those numbers in all assignment modes, add overflow protection and conflict reporting. GitHub #146.

title: Deprecate Gold Zone feature post-Olympics
description:
Remove the Gold Zone feature entirely. Originally created for Olympics 2025, target window was post-Feb 23 2026 (Olympics closing + 1 day). That date has passed.

Scope: ~15 files, ~900 lines to remove.

**Delete entirely (3 files, ~700 lines):**
- teamarr/consumers/gold_zone.py (534 lines)
- teamarr/api/routes/settings/gold_zone.py (52 lines)
- tests/test_gold_zone.py (114 lines)

**Edit (12 files):**
- Backend: generation.py, lifecycle/service.py, settings routes/models/types/read/update, connection.py
- Frontend: Settings.tsx, settings.ts, useSettings.ts
- Schema: 5 columns on settings table (leave dormant, zero out in migration)
notes:
TARGET: After Feb 23, 2026 (Olympics closing ceremony Feb 23, give a day buffer)

Gold Zone footprint — 15 files to touch:

BACKEND (12 files):
- DELETE consumers/gold_zone.py (487 lines — all processing logic)
- DELETE api/routes/settings/gold_zone.py (API endpoints)
- EDIT consumers/generation.py — remove import + call-site (~15 lines, 302-331)
- EDIT api/routes/settings/__init__.py — remove gold_zone route registration
- EDIT api/routes/settings/models.py — remove GoldZone pydantic models
- EDIT database/settings/types.py — remove GoldZoneSettings dataclass
- EDIT database/settings/read.py — remove get_gold_zone_settings()
- EDIT database/settings/update.py — remove update_gold_zone_settings()
- EDIT database/settings/__init__.py — remove re-export
- EDIT database/schema.sql — remove 5 gold_zone columns from settings (migration)
- EDIT database/connection.py — remove migration reference
- EDIT consumers/lifecycle/service.py:2547 — remove hardcoded GoldZone.us TVG ID check

FRONTEND (3 files):
- EDIT pages/Settings.tsx — remove Gold Zone settings card (~90 lines)
- EDIT api/settings.ts — remove API client functions
- EDIT hooks/useSettings.ts — remove hook/query

History: teamarrv2-4v4 (original epic, 10 beads), teamarrv2-6vk (extraction + Day ##)

title: Extract Gold Zone code from generation.py into dedicated gold_zone.py
notes: Move lines 761-1203 from generation.py → consumers/gold_zone.py. All Gold Zone work tracked under 4v4 epic (12 commits). generation.py keeps only the call-site (~30 lines). Post-Olympics: delete gold_zone.py + remove call-site.

title: Add Olympic day number to Gold Zone channel name
Gold Zone Day ## disambiguation in channel name
notes: Milano-Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics: Feb 7-21. Gold Zone airs 1300-2100 UTC daily. Add 'Day ##' (1-15) to channel name based on active Olympic day. Hardcoded date mapping — no settings needed.

title: Add tests for single-mode vs multi-mode league scoping
Bifurcate search_leagues in event_group_processor based on group_mode
notes: Single mode = exactly 1 league (enforced by UI). search_leagues = [group.leagues[0]] for single, all_known_leagues for multi. Single path hits match_single_league (len==1), multi path unchanged.

title: Fix single-mode groups matching outside configured leagues
notes: GitHub #149. Bifurcated matching: single-mode groups search ONLY configured league, multi-mode groups search all then filter. group_mode field (single/multi) finally used in matching pipeline.

title: Add matching tests for tournament stream patterns
description: Add test coverage for: (1) _clean_team_name with gender-prefixed round indicators, (2) _score_teams_against_event with abbreviation matching, (3) regression: Miami (OH) not stripped, existing team names still match.

title: Add abbreviation/short_name to fuzzy team scoring
description: In _score_teams_against_event (team_matcher.py lines 797-863), fuzzy scoring compares stream team names only against event.home_team.name / event.away_team.name. The Team dataclass also carries short_name and abbreviation fields (populated by ESPN). For 3-letter codes like SWE, token_set_ratio('swe','sweden')=67% but token_set_ratio('swe','swe')=100%. Fix: score against all team representations (name, short_name, abbreviation) and take the max.

title: Fix round pattern for gender prefixes in _clean_team_name
description: The round_pattern regex in classifier.py (lines 713-732) fails to strip tournament round indicators with gender prefixes: (M Group B), (W Semi-Final), (Men's Qualifying), (Women's Group A). The regex expects content to start directly with Group/Round/etc, but 'M ' or 'W ' at the start prevents matching. Fix: add optional gender prefix before the alternatives block.

title: Improve stream matching for tournament/international streams (Olympics, World Cup)
description: Tournament-style streams like 'Winter Olympics 25: SWE vs ITA (M Group B) @ 3:10 PM ET' use 3-letter country codes (IOC) instead of full team names. Current matching works but is fragile — 67% fuzzy score just 7 points above the 60% threshold. Two root causes: (1) round pattern doesn't handle gender prefixes like '(M Group B)', (2) fuzzy scoring compares only against team.name, ignoring team.abbreviation which would give 100% exact match.

title: Frontend: Special tab with Gold Zone toggle
description:
New 'Special' tab in Settings page.

**Tab:**
- Add 'Special' tab to SettingsPage tab list (with a sparkle or star icon)
- Tab contains one card for now

**Gold Zone Card:**
- Header: ❄️🥇 Winter Olympics Gold Zone
- Subtext/description: 'Auto-match Gold Zone Olympics coverage'
- Checkbox/switch toggle to enable/disable
- When enabled, show channel number input:
  - Label: 'Channel Number'
  - Number input
  - Helper text: 'Dispatcharr channel number for Gold Zone'
- Save button (or auto-save on toggle like other settings)

**API integration:**
- Read gold_zone_enabled + gold_zone_channel_number from settings API
- Write back on change
- Toast on save success/failure

**Styling:**
- Match existing Settings tab patterns
- The card should feel festive/special — the emojis do the heavy lifting
- Keep it minimal — one card, one toggle, one input. That's it.

**Future-proofing:**
- The Special tab is a container for temporary fun features
- After Olympics: remove the Gold Zone card content (or hide behind a date check)
- The tab structure stays for future one-offs

title: Backend: Gold Zone settings, stream interception, EPG injection
description:
All backend work for the Gold Zone feature:

**Settings:**
- Two new settings in the settings table: `gold_zone_enabled` (boolean, default false), `gold_zone_channel_number` (integer, nullable)
- API: GET/PUT via existing settings endpoints (add to settings schema)

**Stream Interception (event_group_processor.py):**
- When gold_zone_enabled=true, before normal stream classification:
  - Check each stream name against match terms: Gold Zone, GoldZone, Goldzone, Gold-Zone (case-insensitive)
  - Matched streams are pulled out of the normal pipeline
  - After processing, route matched streams to the Gold Zone Dispatcharr channel
- Match logic: case-insensitive check for any of the terms in stream name

**Channel Lifecycle:**
- On first match (or when enabled): create Dispatcharr channel
  - Name: 'Gold Zone'
  - Channel number: from gold_zone_channel_number setting
  - Logo: https://emby.tmsimg.com/assets/p32146358_b_h9_ab.jpg
  - tvg-id: 'teamarr-external-gold-zone'
- Add all matched streams to this channel
- Don't delete the channel on generation cycles (it's static while enabled)
- When disabled: optionally clean up the Dispatcharr channel

**EPG Injection (generation.py):**
- When gold_zone_enabled=true, during run_full_generation:
  - Fetch https://epg.jesmann.com/TeamSports/goldzone.xml (with caching — don't re-fetch if <6hr old)
  - Rewrite channel ID from 'GoldZone.us' to 'teamarr-external-gold-zone'
  - Append to xmltv_contents before merge_xmltv_content()
- Cache the fetched XML in a setting or file to avoid hitting the URL every generation cycle
- Handle fetch failures gracefully (log warning, skip injection, don't block generation)

title: Settings UI for external EPG channels
description:
New section in the Settings page for managing external EPG channels.

**Location:** New tab or section in Settings page (frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx)

**Table view:**
- List of configured external EPG channels
- Columns: Name, Match Terms, XMLTV URL (truncated), Status (enabled/disabled), Last Refreshed, Actions
- Row actions: Edit, Delete, Refresh Now, Enable/Disable toggle

**Add/Edit dialog:**
- Name (text input) — channel display name in Dispatcharr
- Match Terms (text input) — comma-separated keywords, case-insensitive
- XMLTV URL (text input) — with 'Test URL' button that validates and shows preview
- XMLTV Channel ID (dropdown or text) — populated from Test URL results, or manual entry
- Logo URL (optional text input) — override logo, preview shown
- Channel Group (selector matching EventGroupForm pattern — static dropdown + dynamic options)
- Channel Profiles (ChannelProfileSelector component)
- Refresh Interval (number input, hours, default 6)
- Enabled toggle

**Test URL flow:**
1. User pastes URL, clicks 'Test'
2. Backend fetches + parses XMLTV
3. Returns: list of channels (id + display name), programme count per channel, date range
4. User selects which channel ID to use from the results
5. Preview shows sample programme titles

**Reuse existing components:**
- ChannelProfileSelector for profile selection
- Channel group selector pattern from EventGroupForm (static + dynamic)
- Standard Dialog/Form patterns from existing Settings sections

title: Scheduler integration for external EPG refresh
description:
Integrate external EPG refresh into the CronScheduler.

**Trigger points:**
1. During generation cycle (run_full_generation) — refresh stale sources before merging
2. On manual refresh via API endpoint (POST /external-epg/{id}/refresh)
3. Optionally: dedicated cron job for periodic refresh independent of generation

**Implementation:**
- In generation.py, before the XMLTV merge step, call external_epg_service.refresh_stale()
  which refreshes any sources past their refresh_interval_hours
- Log refresh activity to generation logs
- Handle fetch failures gracefully (use stale cache, log warning, don't block generation)

**Monitoring:**
- Include external EPG status in generation results (how many sources, last refresh times)
- Warn if any source hasn't been refreshed in 2x its interval

title: EPG fetch, cache, and merge injection
description:
Fetch external XMLTV sources and inject into the generation pipeline.

**Fetching:**
- HTTP GET to xmltv_url with timeout + error handling
- Parse XML to validate it's valid XMLTV
- If xmltv_channel_id specified, filter to only that channel's programmes
- Cache the filtered XMLTV in the external_epg_channels.cached_xmltv column
- Update last_fetched timestamp
- Respect refresh_interval_hours (don't re-fetch if recently cached)

**Merge Injection (generation.py):**
In run_full_generation(), after fetching team_xmltv and group_xmltv:
- Query all enabled external EPG channels that have cached XMLTV
- Remap the channel ID in the cached XMLTV to match the Dispatcharr channel's tvg-id format
- Append to xmltv_contents list before merge_xmltv_content()

**tvg-id mapping:**
The external XMLTV uses channel ID 'GoldZone.us' but the Dispatcharr channel will have a different ID. Need to rewrite the channel/programme IDs in the cached XMLTV to match the Dispatcharr channel ID (or use a consistent format like 'teamarr-external-{id}').

**Service layer:**
New teamarr/services/external_epg_service.py:
- fetch_and_cache(channel_id) — fetch + filter + cache
- get_cached_xmltv_for_merge() — return all cached XMLTV for generation
- refresh_all() — refresh all enabled channels past their interval

title: Stream interception + channel lifecycle for external EPG
description:
Two pieces:

**1. Stream Interception (event_group_processor.py)**
Before normal stream classification/matching, check each stream name against enabled external EPG channels' match_terms. If matched:
- Remove stream from the normal matching pipeline
- Add stream to a 'external_epg_streams' collection keyed by external EPG channel ID
- After normal processing, route these streams to their unified channels

Key: interception happens BEFORE classifier.classify() since Gold Zone would be classified as PLACEHOLDER and discarded.

**2. Channel Lifecycle**
For each external EPG channel that has matched streams:
- Check if Dispatcharr channel exists (dispatcharr_channel_id in DB)
- If not: create channel in Dispatcharr with configured name, logo, group, profiles
- Add all matched streams to the Dispatcharr channel
- Store the Dispatcharr channel ID back in the external_epg_channels table
- Handle channel group resolution (static or dynamic patterns via DynamicResolver)

Edge cases:
- Multiple M3U sources may have Gold Zone streams — all route to same channel
- If no streams match in a cycle, keep the channel (don't delete — it has external EPG)
- Channel reconciliation: external EPG channels are NOT subject to event-based deletion timing

title: API endpoints for external EPG channels
description:
REST API in teamarr/api/routes/external_epg.py:

Endpoints:
- GET /api/v1/external-epg — List all external EPG channels
- GET /api/v1/external-epg/{id} — Get single channel details
- POST /api/v1/external-epg — Create new external EPG channel
- PUT /api/v1/external-epg/{id} — Update existing
- DELETE /api/v1/external-epg/{id} — Delete channel
- POST /api/v1/external-epg/{id}/refresh — Force refresh cached XMLTV
- POST /api/v1/external-epg/test-url — Validate a XMLTV URL (fetch, parse, return channel list + programme count)

Register router in app.py.

The test-url endpoint is important for the UI — lets users paste a URL and see what channels/programmes it contains before saving.

title: Schema + data layer for external EPG channels
description:
New `external_epg_channels` table in schema.sql with migration (v55):

Fields:
- id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY
- name TEXT NOT NULL (channel display name, e.g. 'Gold Zone')
- match_terms TEXT NOT NULL (comma-separated keywords: 'Gold Zone,GoldZone,Goldzone,Gold-Zone')
- xmltv_url TEXT NOT NULL (external XMLTV URL)
- xmltv_channel_id TEXT (channel ID within the XMLTV, e.g. 'GoldZone.us' — if null, use first channel)
- channel_group_id INTEGER (optional Dispatcharr group)
- channel_group_mode TEXT DEFAULT 'static' (support dynamic patterns)
- channel_profile_ids TEXT (JSON array, supports wildcards)
- logo_url TEXT (optional logo override)
- enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1
- refresh_interval_hours INTEGER DEFAULT 6
- last_fetched TIMESTAMP
- cached_xmltv TEXT (cached XML content)
- dispatcharr_channel_id INTEGER (created Dispatcharr channel ID, managed by lifecycle)
- created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
- updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

Python layer:
- Dataclass: ExternalEPGChannel in teamarr/database/external_epg.py
- CRUD operations: get_all, get_by_id, create, update, delete, get_enabled
- Keyword matching function: check_external_epg_match(stream_name) -> ExternalEPGChannel | None
  Case-insensitive substring matching against all enabled channels' match_terms

title: External EPG Channels — keyword-matched channels with external XMLTV
description:
## ❄️🥇 Winter Olympics Gold Zone

**Simplified scope**: Single toggle in Settings → Special tab. NOT a general-purpose system — a fun one-off feature for the 2026 Milano Cortina Olympics. Hide the tab after the Olympics; bring it back for future one-off events.

### UX
- Settings page gets a new **Special** tab
- Single checkbox: ❄️🥇 **Winter Olympics Gold Zone**
- Subtext: *Auto-match Gold Zone Olympics coverage*
- If checked, show channel number input
- That's it. No CRUD, no add/edit forms, no general config.

### Backend
- Two settings: `gold_zone_enabled` (bool) and `gold_zone_channel_number` (int)
- Stream interception: when enabled, match streams containing Gold Zone/GoldZone/Goldzone/Gold-Zone (case-insensitive) across all event groups, skip normal matching, route to unified Dispatcharr channel
- EPG: fetch hardcoded XMLTV URL (https://epg.jesmann.com/TeamSports/goldzone.xml), cache, inject into merge
- Channel lifecycle: create/manage one Dispatcharr channel named 'Gold Zone' at the configured channel number

### External EPG
- URL: https://epg.jesmann.com/TeamSports/goldzone.xml
- Channel ID: GoldZone.us
- Logo: https://emby.tmsimg.com/assets/p32146358_b_h9_ab.jpg
- 15 daily episodes Feb 7-21 2026, 9hr blocks of whip-around Olympic coverage

### Future
- After Olympics: hide the Special tab (feature flag or just remove the tab content)
- Bring back for future one-off events (Super Bowl specials, World Cup, etc.)
- The Special tab is the home for fun temporary features

title: Add custom pattern radio option to bulk import channel group selector
description:
In EventGroupImport.tsx:

1. Change bulkChannelGroupMode state type from 'static' | '{sport}' | '{league}' to string (to allow custom patterns)
2. Add a 4th radio button 'Custom — Define a custom pattern with variables' after the {league} option
3. When selected, show a text input (font-mono) with placeholder 'Sports | {sport} | {league}'
4. Show helper text: 'Available: {sport}, {league}'
5. The custom input value becomes the channel_group_mode sent to the API

Reference implementation: EventGroupForm.tsx lines 1813-1876 (the single group form already has this exact UI pattern).

title: Add custom dynamic channel group to bulk import
description:
Issue #137: The bulk import UI (EventGroupImport.tsx) only offers three channel group modes: static, {sport}, {league}. The single group form (EventGroupForm.tsx) already supports a fourth 'Custom' option with a free-text pattern input (e.g., 'Sports | {sport} | {league}').

The backend already accepts any string for channel_group_mode and the dynamic resolver handles arbitrary patterns. This is a frontend-only gap.

Fix: Add a 'Custom' radio option + text input to the bulk import channel group selector, matching EventGroupForm.tsx behavior.

title: Validate backup path setting
description:
The backup settings update accepts any string as the backup path without validation. Add checks:
1. Path must resolve under allowed directories (data/, /app/data/)
2. Directory must be writable (or creatable)
3. Reject obvious bad paths (/etc, /proc, etc.)
4. Return clear error if validation fails

title: Use sqlite3.backup() and add restore validation
description:
Two related fixes:

1. Replace shutil.copy2() with sqlite3.backup() for creating backups. shutil.copy2 on a live SQLite DB can produce corrupt copies if writes happen during copy. sqlite3.backup() is the safe alternative.

2. Add SQLite validation before restore. Currently the restore endpoint copies any file to the active DB path without checking if it's valid SQLite. Add PRAGMA integrity_check before overwriting.

3. Move pre-restore backup to configured backup directory (currently goes to DB parent dir, won't appear in backup list UI).

4. Move file operations from route handler to BackupService (layer separation).

title: Integrate BackupScheduler into existing CronScheduler
description:
PR #142 creates a separate BackupScheduler class (~230 lines) with its own thread and event loop. This duplicates the existing CronScheduler infrastructure used by EPG, channel reset, and cache refresh.

Fix:
1. Add backup settings to SchedulerSettings dataclass
2. Add _task_backup() method to CronScheduler
3. Wire backup execution into _run_tasks()
4. Remove standalone BackupScheduler class
5. Remove separate startup/shutdown from app.py
6. Match channel_reset task pattern

title: Fix path traversal in backup filename endpoints
description:
All backup endpoints accepting filename parameter only check startswith('teamarr_') and endswith('.db'). Missing validation for path separators (/, \, ..) which allows directory traversal.

Fix: Extract shared filename validator, apply to all 5 endpoints (DELETE, protect, unprotect, restore, download). Reject filenames containing /, \, or .. characters.

title: Fix schema migration — restore v52/v53, add v54 cleanly
description:
PR #142 replaces the existing v52 and v53 migration blocks with its own code. This would break upgrades from pre-v52 versions. Fix:
1. Restore original v52 migration (default_bypass_filter_for_playoffs columns)
2. Restore original v53 migration (api_timeout update)
3. Add new v54 migration block for scheduled_backup_* columns
4. Fix log message (currently says 'upgraded to 52' but sets 54)
5. Update schema.sql DEFAULT version to 54
6. Verify migration path works from v51 → v54
notes:
## Pre-Merge Migration State (ARCHIVED)

### Current v52 block (lines 661-672 of connection.py) — WILL BE DELETED BY PR:
```python
# v52: Bypass team filter for playoffs (issue #70)
# Global default in settings, per-group override in event_epg_groups
if current_version < 52:
    _add_column_if_not_exists(
        conn, "settings", "default_bypass_filter_for_playoffs", "BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0"
    )
    _add_column_if_not_exists(
        conn, "event_epg_groups", "bypass_filter_for_playoffs", "BOOLEAN"
    )
    conn.execute("UPDATE settings SET schema_version = 52 WHERE id = 1")
    logger.info("[MIGRATE] Schema upgraded to version 52 (playoff filter bypass)")
    current_version = 52
```

### Current v53 block (lines 674-683) — WILL BE DELETED BY PR:
```python
# v53: Bump api_timeout default from 10 to 30
# The DispatcharrClient always used 30s effectively, but the DB setting
# (which was never wired up) defaulted to 10. Now that we wire it up,
# bump existing users from 10 -> 30 to avoid a timeout regression.
if current_version < 53:
    conn.execute("UPDATE settings SET api_timeout = 30 WHERE api_timeout = 10 AND id = 1")
    conn.execute("UPDATE settings SET api_retry_count = 5 WHERE api_retry_count = 3 AND id = 1")
    conn.execute("UPDATE settings SET schema_version = 53 WHERE id = 1")
    logger.info("[MIGRATE] Schema upgraded to version 53 (api timeout/retry defaults)")
    current_version = 53
```

### What PR #142 replaces them with (BROKEN):
```python
# v54: Add scheduled backup settings columns
if current_version < 54:
    _add_column_if_not_exists(conn, "settings", "scheduled_backup_enabled", "BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0")
    _add_column_if_not_exists(conn, "settings", "scheduled_backup_cron", "TEXT DEFAULT '0 3 * * *'")
    _add_column_if_not_exists(conn, "settings", "scheduled_backup_max_count", "INTEGER DEFAULT 7")
    _add_column_if_not_exists(conn, "settings", "scheduled_backup_path", "TEXT DEFAULT './data/backups'")
    conn.execute("UPDATE settings SET schema_version = 52 WHERE id = 1")  # BUG: says 52
    logger.info("[MIGRATE] Schema upgraded to version 52 (scheduled backup settings)")  # BUG: says 52
    current_version = 54  # BUG: jumps to 54
```

### Fix plan:
1. After merge, restore v52 block exactly as above
2. Restore v53 block exactly as above
3. Add new v54 block with ONLY the 4 backup columns + correct version set
4. Fix schema.sql DEFAULT to 54
5. Test migration from v51 -> v54 (simulated)

title: Merge PR #142 to dev
description: Merge Sp0rtsFr3ak's backup system PR #142 into dev branch as-is. Post a comment on the PR acknowledging the contribution and noting we'll make some follow-up fixes for integration.

title: Backup System — merge PR #142 and fix issues
description:
Merge PR #142 (Backup System by Sp0rtsFr3ak) into dev and fix all issues identified in review. PR has solid core logic (BackupService, UI, hooks, API types) but needs architectural fixes before release.

**PR #142 — What's good:**
- BackupService class with create/delete/rotate/protect
- React Query hooks with proper cache invalidation
- Settings UI with cron presets and backup file management
- Protection mechanism (prevent rotation deletion)

**Issues to fix post-merge:**
1. Schema migration overwrites v52/v53 blocks — must restore and add v54 cleanly
2. Path traversal in filename endpoints — no validation for .. or /
3. Separate BackupScheduler — should integrate into existing CronScheduler
4. shutil.copy2 on live DB — should use sqlite3.backup() for safe copies
5. Restore without SQLite validation — could brick the app
6. Pre-restore backup goes to wrong directory
7. Layer separation — file ops in route handler instead of service
8. Backup path setting not validated

**Approach:** Merge PR as-is to dev, then fix each issue in follow-up commits. Contributor gets merge credit.

title: Fix Detection Library endpoints returning 500 (Depends(get_db) incompatible with @contextmanager)
description: All 8 detection_keywords routes use Depends(get_db) but get_db is decorated with @contextmanager, which is incompatible with FastAPI's dependency injection. Fix: replace Depends(get_db) with 'with get_db() as conn:' inside each route body. Closes GitHub #136.

title: Fix ESPN _parse_team losing logo_url on summary endpoints
description: Line 491 of espn/provider.py uses team_data.get('logo') which only works for scoreboard endpoints. Summary/schedule endpoints use 'logos' array format. Fix: use self._extract_logo(team_data) which handles both formats. Closes GitHub #139.

title: Code Health Audit — Mar 2026
description:
Next repeatable audit cycle. Claim this bead and run the full audit checklist from the parent epic.

## What to check
- Run all 5 audit categories from parent epic checklist
- Look for new dead code introduced since Feb 2026 audit
- Check if any TODO:PRUNE markers are ready to act on
- Verify progress on remaining open beads (5hq.5 god functions, 5hq.10 layer separation)
- Update lessons learned in parent epic if new patterns found

## Baseline from Feb 2026 audit cycle
- Dead API: 1 medium-confidence candidate (GET /cache/sports)
- Dead frontend: 1 item pruned (SearchableMultiSelect 228 lines)
- Layer separation: 16 violations (6 HIGH, 10 MEDIUM)
- God functions: 5 remain (370, 337, 310, 238, 202 lines)
- False positive lesson: useTheme() looked dead but was used internally by useThemedLogo()

title: Extract shared SelectedBadges component from picker components
description:
Extract the duplicated badge rendering logic (badges + RichTooltip overflow) from LeaguePicker, TeamPicker, and CheckboxListPicker into a shared <SelectedBadges> component.

## Current duplication
- LeaguePicker lines 206-237: badges with logos, X to remove, +N more tooltip
- TeamPicker lines 298-332: badges with X to remove, +N more tooltip
- CheckboxListPicker lines 148-175: badges with X to remove, +N more tooltip

## Shared component API
```tsx
interface SelectedBadgesProps {
  items: { key: string; label: string; icon?: string }[]
  maxBadges?: number  // default 10
  onRemove: (key: string) => void
}
```

## Implementation
1. Create frontend/src/components/ui/selected-badges.tsx
2. Refactor LeaguePicker, TeamPicker, CheckboxListPicker to use it
3. Verify build passes
4. ~60 lines of duplication eliminated

title: Evaluate picker component reuse — reduce duplication
description:
User asked: 'are we effectively reusing components like this in the UI instead of having duplicate code blocks?'

## Findings

### Badge pattern (10 + RichTooltip overflow) — STANDARDIZED ✅
All active picker components now use consistent maxBadges=10 with RichTooltip on overflow:
- LeaguePicker (default prop = 10) — 4 callers
- TeamPicker (hardcoded 10) — used in EventGroupForm
- CheckboxListPicker (default prop = 10) — used in TemplateAssignmentModal

### Potential shared sub-component: SelectedBadges
Badge rendering logic (badges + overflow tooltip) is duplicated in 3 components:
- LeaguePicker lines 206-237
- TeamPicker lines 298-332
- CheckboxListPicker lines 148-175

Could extract a shared <SelectedBadges> component with props: items, maxBadges, renderLabel, onRemove.
Estimated savings: ~60 lines duplication → 1 shared component.

### Dead code: SearchableMultiSelect
searchable-multiselect.tsx has ZERO callers. Tracked under 5hq.8 for pruning.

### Select All / Clear All duplication
Similar but not identical logic in LeaguePicker and CheckboxListPicker. Not worth abstracting — domain-specific differences (sport grouping, filtering) make a shared abstraction more complex than the duplication.

### Recommendation
LOW priority. The duplication is modest (badge overflow in 3 components). Extract SelectedBadges component if/when picker components get refactored for other reasons.
close_reason: Done: SelectedBadges shared component extracted and shipped; Select-All/Clear abstraction explicitly evaluated as not worth it. Findings documented.

title: Standardize badge cap to 10 across all picker components
Code Health Audit — next cycle
description:

title: Prune dead legacy migration code (~500 lines)
description:
_run_migrations() in database/connection.py is 797 lines. ~500 lines are unreachable legacy migration code that runs only for pre-checkpoint databases.

## Scope
- File: teamarr/database/connection.py, function _run_migrations(), lines 434-1230
- The function has a checkpoint system -- legacy migrations are skipped when checkpoint exists
- ~500 lines of legacy code are dead on modern databases
- Need to verify: which migration version is the checkpoint? What lines are truly unreachable?

## Approach
1. Read the checkpoint logic to identify the cutoff version
2. Identify which migration blocks are unreachable (post-checkpoint)
3. Remove dead blocks, keep the checkpoint system and active migrations
4. Run pytest + ruff + frontend build

## Risk
LOW -- checkpoint system guarantees these blocks never execute on modern databases. But trace carefully: some migration blocks may set state used by later blocks.

title: Trace and prune POST /matching/events endpoint
description:
The `POST /matching/events` endpoint and its backing `MatchingService` appear to be completely orphaned. No frontend or backend callers found.

## Scope of potential removal
- `teamarr/api/routes/matching.py` — entire file (route + helper)
- `teamarr/services/matching_service.py` — `MatchingService` class + `create_matching_service` factory
- `teamarr/api/models.py` — `EventMatchRequest` + `EventMatchResponse` (verify not shared)
- `teamarr/services/__init__.py` — re-exports of MatchingService, MatchResult, create_matching_service
- `teamarr/api/app.py:24,352` — import and router mount

## Required before pruning
- [ ] Line-by-line trace of `matching_service.py` to confirm nothing else uses it
- [ ] Verify `EventMatchRequest`/`EventMatchResponse` aren't shared with other routes
- [ ] Verify `MatchResult` dataclass isn't used elsewhere
- [ ] Check if removing the `matching` import from `services/__init__.py` breaks any other imports
- [ ] Run `pytest tests/ -v` before and after
- [ ] Run `ruff check teamarr/` after to catch any dangling references
- [ ] Run `cd frontend && npm run build` after

## Why careful tracing matters
This is a full module removal, not a single endpoint delete. If any piece of the chain is shared, the prune breaks things. At ~2% test coverage, we have no safety net.

title: Code Health Audit
description:
Repeatable code health audit for Teamarr. Run periodically to keep the codebase clean, well-structured, and free of dead code. Each child bead represents an audit pass — claim it, run the audit, fix issues found, close it.

## Audit Checklist

### 1. Dead Code Detection

**CRITICAL: The frontend uses BOTH structured API clients (`frontend/src/api/*.ts`) AND direct `fetch()` calls in pages/components. You MUST search for BOTH patterns when checking if an endpoint is alive.**

Detection method for API endpoints:
- [ ] For each route in `teamarr/api/routes/`, extract the URL path
- [ ] Search `frontend/src/` for the path in ALL forms: api client calls, direct `fetch()` calls, and string literals (e.g. `/dispatcharr/channel-groups` may appear as `fetch("/api/v1/dispatcharr/channel-groups")` in a page, not in an api client file)
- [ ] Search `teamarr/` for backend callers (excluding the route definition itself)
- [ ] Check for external consumers: Dispatcharr callbacks, webhook URLs, cron jobs, docker healthchecks
- [ ] **Only mark as dead if ALL of the above return zero hits**

Detection method for frontend code:
- [ ] Unused exports in `frontend/src/api/` — search all of `frontend/src/` for imports (not just hooks)
- [ ] Unused hooks in `frontend/src/hooks/` — search all of `frontend/src/` for imports
- [ ] Unused components — search all of `frontend/src/` for imports and JSX usage
- [ ] Note: some components may be used via dynamic imports or lazy loading — check `App.tsx` routes

Backend dead code:
- [ ] Functions/classes with no callers
- [ ] Unused imports (ruff handles most, but check re-exports)
- [ ] Stale migration code or backwards-compat shims

### 2. API Surface Audit
- [ ] Every route in teamarr/api/routes/ has a corresponding frontend caller OR is documented as internal/external-only
- [ ] Response models match what frontend actually consumes (no phantom fields)
- [ ] No duplicate endpoints doing the same thing via different paths
- [ ] Consistent error handling patterns across routes

### 3. Layer Separation
- [ ] Routes (api/) should only do request/response — no business logic
- [ ] Services (services/) own business logic — no direct DB queries
- [ ] Consumers (consumers/) own orchestration — no HTTP concerns
- [ ] Database (database/) owns queries — no business logic leaking in
- [ ] No circular imports between layers

### 4. Code Quality
- [ ] No god functions (500+ lines) — break into focused helpers
- [ ] No deeply nested logic (4+ levels) — flatten with early returns
- [ ] Type annotations on public APIs
- [ ] Consistent logging patterns ([MODULE] prefix, appropriate levels)
- [ ] No hardcoded magic numbers/strings — use constants

### 5. Frontend Hygiene
- [ ] Unused components (no imports)
- [ ] Unused API client functions
- [ ] Consistent hook patterns (useQuery/useMutation)
- [ ] No inline styles where Tailwind classes exist
- [ ] Dead feature flags or commented-out code

## Lessons Learned

### Q1 2026 — First Audit
- **False positive rate was high for dead endpoint detection.** Automated agents searched only `frontend/src/api/` for endpoint callers but missed direct `fetch()` calls in pages/components. Many endpoints flagged as "dead" were actually live (detection_keywords, aliases, dispatcharr, migration — all actively used).
- **Root cause:** The frontend has two calling patterns: (1) structured api client functions in `api/*.ts` and (2) direct `fetch("/api/v1/...")" calls in page components. Both must be searched.
- **Rule:** Never trust automated dead-code detection without manual verification. Always grep the ENTIRE frontend src directory for the URL path string, not just the api client files.
- **What worked well:** Layer separation audit (circular import check, HTTP-in-consumers check) and code quality audit (god functions, nesting depth, logging consistency) had reliable results.

## How to Run This Audit
1. Claim the current open child bead
2. Work through the checklist above
3. **For dead code: verify every candidate manually before marking for removal**
4. Fix issues as you find them (commit incrementally)
5. Document findings and lessons learned in the bead comments
6. Close the bead, create the next one for the next cycle
7. Update this epic description with any new lessons learned

## Frequency
Run quarterly, or after major feature pushes.

title: Document lifecycle service path architecture
description: Add a clear architectural comment/docstring to the lifecycle service documenting the parallel paths (creation vs sync vs cross-group) and their contract — what context each path requires and what settings each path manages. This prevents future disparity bugs.

title: Audit profile and channel group assignment parity
description: Creation path uses _dynamic_resolver for channel_group_id and channel_profile_ids with sport/league context. Sync path also resolves these. Verify both paths use identical resolution (dynamic mode, sport/league wildcards, etc.). Also audit stream_profile_id handling.

title: Audit logo resolution parity between creation and sync paths
description: Creation path resolves logos via template + upload. Sync path checks logo_id and re-uploads if needed. Verify they use the same resolution logic (template variables, fallback behavior, etc.).

title: Audit template resolution context parity between creation and sync paths
description: Compare what context variables are available in _create_channel's _generate_channel_name vs _sync_channel_settings' _generate_channel_name call. Verify all template variables (exception_keyword, card_segment, etc.) are passed identically. Check _resolve_template's build_for_event call in both paths.

title: Audit creation vs sync path disparity in lifecycle service
description:
The lifecycle service has two parallel code paths for channel name/settings: **creation** (_create_channel) and **sync** (_sync_channel_settings/_handle_existing_channel). These paths can diverge — e.g., segment context wasn't flowing through the sync path until ou3.4 fix. Audit all data flowing through both paths and identify disparities.

## Known paths
| Path | Entry point | Purpose |
|------|-------------|---------|
| Creation | _create_channel() | New channel: name, tvg_id, logo, group, profiles, stream |
| Sync | _sync_channel_settings() | Existing channel: re-resolve name, number, group, profiles, logo, tvg_id, stream_profile |
| Cross-group | _handle_cross_group_overlap() | Multi-league: add stream to channel in different group |

## Known disparities found during ou3 fix
1. **segment context** — _create_channel had segment, _sync_channel_settings didn't pass it to template resolution (fixed in e38fe74)
2. **segment_display auto-append** — was in _create_channel but not in EPG generator path (fixed: removed from both, now uses template variable)

## What to audit
- Template resolution context completeness (are all variables available in both paths?)
- Logo resolution (does sync path re-resolve logos the same way creation does?)
- Stream profile assignment (creation vs sync)  
- Channel profile assignment (creation vs sync)
- Channel group resolution (creation vs sync)
- Exception keyword handling (creation vs sync)

title: Per-stream error isolation in lifecycle batch processing
description:
One ValueError in `_generate_channel_name` (when template=None) kills the entire `process_matched_streams` batch. Events with valid templates never get processed.

## Root Cause
`process_matched_streams` (service.py:321-597) wraps the entire `for matched in matched_streams:` loop (lines 354-581) in a single try/except. One exception aborts all remaining streams.

## Fix
Wrap each iteration in its own try/except:
```python
for matched in matched_streams:
    try:
        # ... existing stream processing ...
    except Exception as stream_err:
        event_id = matched.get("event", {})
        if hasattr(event_id, "id"):
            event_id = event_id.id
        logger.error(
            "[LIFECYCLE] Error processing stream for event %s: %s",
            event_id, stream_err
        )
        result.errors.append({"event": str(event_id), "error": str(stream_err)})
        continue
```
Keep outer try/except for setup-level errors (lines 321-353: db connection, resolver init).

## Files
- `teamarr/consumers/lifecycle/service.py` (lines 321-597)

title: Remove channel name segment auto-append
description:
Remove the hardcoded segment_display auto-append from channel names. Users control segment display via `{card_segment_display}` template variable.

## Root Cause
`generate_for_matched_streams` (event_epg.py:421-423) auto-appends `- {segment_display}` to channel names. When users template already uses `{card_segment_display}`, the segment appears twice (e.g., "UFC Fight Night Prelims - Prelims").

## Fix
Delete lines 421-423 in `teamarr/consumers/event_epg.py`:
```python
# DELETE:
if segment_display:
    channel_name = f"{channel_name} - {segment_display}"
```

Users who want segment display in channel names use `{card_segment_display}` in their templates `event_channel_name` field.

## Files
- `teamarr/consumers/event_epg.py` (lines 421-423)

title: Per-event filler config for multi-template groups
description:
Filler (pregame/postgame) only loads from the default template. When no default exists, zero filler. When default exists, all events get wrong filler. Fix by loading per-event filler configs.

## Root Cause
`_generate_xmltv` (event_group_processor.py:2521-2531) only loads `filler_config` from `default_template_id`. `_generate_filler_for_streams` passes a single `filler_config` to all streams.

## Fix
**In `_generate_xmltv`:**
1. Store `_event_template_id` in each match dict during per-event template resolution (line ~2575)
2. After resolution, build `filler_cache: dict[int, EventFillerConfig | None]` for all unique template_ids
3. Load filler config for each template via `template_to_event_filler_config()`
4. Annotate each match with `_event_filler_config` from the cache
5. Generate filler if ANY template has filler enabled (not just default)

**In `_generate_filler_for_streams`:**  
1. Per stream, use `match.get("_event_filler_config") or filler_config`
2. Skip filler for streams where template has no filler enabled
3. Keep `filler_config` param as fallback/default

## Files
- `teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py` (lines 2490-2753)
- Uses `template_to_event_filler_config` from `teamarr/consumers/filler/event_filler.py:488`
- Uses `get_template` from `teamarr/database/templates.py:191`
notes:
## Bootstrap for next session

You're fixing a batch-killing bug in multi-template resolution. Start by reading this bead's description.

### The bug
In `teamarr/consumers/lifecycle/service.py`, `process_matched_streams()` (line 288) has a single try/except (lines 321-597) wrapping the entire matched_streams loop. When a group has sport/league-specific templates but NO default template (both NULL sports/leagues), any event that doesn't match a specific template causes:

`_resolve_event_template()` (line 245) → returns None → `_create_channel()` (line 945) → `_generate_channel_name()` (line 1142) → ValueError at line 1186 (no name_format) → **kills entire batch**

### The fix
Wrap each iteration of the `for matched in matched_streams:` loop (line 354) in its own try/except. Log the error per-stream, append to result.errors, continue. Keep the outer try/except for setup-level errors (db connection, resolver init at lines 321-353). The ValueError in _generate_channel_name stays — it correctly signals a missing template.

### Key files
- `teamarr/consumers/lifecycle/service.py` — the fix location
- `teamarr/database/groups.py:1606` — `get_template_for_event()` resolution logic
- `teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py:2408-2412, 2515-2519` — callers that pass empty strings for default lookup
- `teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py:2550-2583` — per-event EPG resolution (this path works correctly)

### Context
- Commit d3f29f8 (Feb 6) fixed a prior crash that masked this bug
- Commit cffc04f added diagnostic logging users are now seeing
- User logs showing `sport='', league=''` are from the DEFAULT template lookup (intentional empty strings), NOT the per-event lookup
- Run `bd show teamarrv2-ou3` for full investigation notes
- Run quality gates after fix: `ruff check teamarr/ && pytest tests/ -v && cd frontend && npm run build`
## Issue 132 analysis (2026-02-07)

### User 1 (issue 132 submitter)
- Templates: `[(4, [baseball,basketball,football], [nhl]), (5, None, [olywh]), (6, None, [olymh]), (7, [mma], None)]`
- NO default template → filler_config=None → 0 filler everywhere
- Template resolution WORKS: `event=600057328 sport=mma league=ufc -> template=7`
- Channel name: "UFC Fight Night: Bautista vs. Oliveira Prelims - Prelims" (duplicated segment)

### User 2 (new logs)
- Templates: `[(5, [mma], None), (4, None, None)]` — HAS default (template 4)
- Filler generated (27 filler) but ALL use template 4 config, not template 5 for UFC
- Template resolution works: `event=600057328 sport=mma league=ufc -> template=5 (default=4)`

### Key code paths
- `_generate_xmltv:2521-2531` — filler_config from default only
- `_generate_filler_for_streams:2700-2742` — single filler_config for all streams  
- `generate_for_matched_streams:422-423` — unconditional segment_display append
- `process_matched_streams:321-597` — single try/except for entire loop

title: Add tests for multi-template resolution with sport/league
description:
Add tests for multi-template resolution with per-event filler, segment naming, and error isolation.

## Test cases
1. Per-event filler: Group with 2 templates (default + MMA), verify MMA events get MMA filler
2. No default template: Group with only sport-specific templates, verify filler still generated
3. Channel name: Template with `{card_segment_display}` — no duplication after auto-append removal
4. Error isolation: One bad event doesnt kill the batch — other events still process

## Blocked by
- ou3.2 (per-event filler)
- ou3.4 (segment auto-append removal)
- ou3.5 (error isolation)

title: Trace where sport/league becomes empty in event pipeline
description: User logs show template resolution receives sport='' league='' for UFC events. Trace the full path from ESPN provider through cache, matching, enrichment, to template resolution to find where sport/league is lost.
notes: Root cause: 1) ValueError crash in _generate_channel_name when template=None kills entire batch. 2) Broad exception handling means one bad event stops all. 3) Default template lookup logs misleading 'No match' for empty-string queries.

title: Multi-template resolution fails when sport/league empty
description:
Multi-template resolution has three bugs affecting pregame/postgame and channel naming. GitHub issue #132.

**Bug 1: No filler when no default template**
Groups with ONLY sport/league-specific templates (no default with both NULL) get zero pregame/postgame.
`_generate_xmltv` (event_group_processor.py:2521-2531) only loads filler_config from default template. No default → no filler for any event.

**Bug 2: Filler uses wrong template for per-event overrides**  
Even when a default template exists, ALL events get that templates pregame/postgame. UFC events with their own MMA template still get the default pregame/postgame instead of MMA-specific pre/post fight content.

**Bug 3: Channel name segment duplication**
When template event_channel_name contains `{card_segment_display}`, the code at event_epg.py:422-423 appends segment_display again, resulting in "UFC Fight Night Prelims - Prelims".

**Bug 4 (existing): Lifecycle batch-killing ValueError**
No per-stream error isolation in process_matched_streams loop (service.py:354-585). One event without a template kills the entire batch.

Timeline: commit d3f29f8 (Feb 6) fixed a prior crash that masked these issues.
notes:
## Investigation trace

### User 1 log analysis
The `sport='', league=''` in their log is from the DEFAULT template lookup at event_group_processor.py:2412/2519 — these lines intentionally pass empty strings to find the template with both sports=NULL and leagues=NULL. When no default exists, it logs 'No match found' which is misleading but expected. The per-event resolution (line 2559) uses actual event sport/league and should work.

### User 2 log analysis  
Template resolution works correctly: sport='mma' league='ufc' -> template=6. This confirms per-event resolution logic is sound.

### Why 'MANY users' see this
Commit d3f29f8 (Feb 6) fixed a 4-tuple unpacking crash in _cleanup_team_filtered_channels that was killing ALL group processing. Before that fix, nothing worked so template issues were invisible. After the fix, groups process but the lifecycle ValueError surfaces.

### Secondary observations
- Filler (pregame/postgame) is only loaded from the default template (line 2527-2531), not per-event templates
- The 'No match found' log from default lookup confuses users into thinking resolution is broken
- Stale group.template_id (legacy field) is cleared on new add_group_template calls (d3f29f8) but not retroactively for existing groups

title: Fix template display mismatch between event group table and assignment modal
description: The event group table shows template_id (legacy single template) or 'Managed (N)' count badge. The modal fetches from group_templates table. When a group uses legacy template_id, the modal shows nothing. When using multi-templates, the table only shows a count. The two views are inconsistent and confusing.

title: v2.1.2 regression: group processing crash + template display mismatch
Bulk import writes literal 'league' instead of dynamic {league} channel group
description: When bulk-creating event groups with channel group set to {league} dynamic mode, the literal string 'league' is written instead of using the dynamic channel_group_mode='league'. This only happens during multi-select bulk import, not single group creation. Reported by user.

title: Add conditional title/subtitle UI to TemplateForm
description: Add title_final, title_not_final, subtitle_final, subtitle_not_final input fields to postgame and idle conditional sections in frontend/src/pages/TemplateForm.tsx. Also update ConditionalSettings interface in frontend/src/api/templates.ts

description: Update teamarr/consumers/filler/generator.py and event_filler.py to use title_final/subtitle_final when game is final, same pattern as description

title: Add title/subtitle fields to API ConditionalContent model
description: Add title_final, title_not_final, subtitle_final, subtitle_not_final to ConditionalContent in teamarr/api/models.py

title: Add title/subtitle fields to ConditionalFillerTemplate
description: Add title_final, title_not_final, subtitle_final, subtitle_not_final to ConditionalFillerTemplate dataclass in teamarr/core/filler_types.py

title: Conditional postgame/idle subtitle and title
description:
## GitHub Issue
Closes #102 - [Feature]: Enable postgame subtitle to be conditional

## Problem
Jellyfin/Emby show subtitle in guide view, description only when clicking an event. Users want postgame score in subtitle, but currently it shows during the game (spoiler) because subtitle is static while description is conditional.

## Solution
Add `title_final`, `title_not_final`, `subtitle_final`, `subtitle_not_final` to postgame_conditional and idle_conditional, following the exact same pattern as description_final/description_not_final.

## Migration
No explicit migration needed - JSON columns will have null for new fields, which falls back to static values (current behavior).

title: Re-check ESPN curling API on Feb 6 when Olympics start

title: Soccer mode: search box appears under wrong radio button
description:
When 'Follow Teams' is selected, the team search box visually appears below 'Select Leagues' radio button, which is confusing.

**User feedback:**
'Visually feels strange that the search box is under Select Leagues if you have Follow Teams selected'

**Current behavior:**
In SoccerModeSelector.tsx, the JSX structure is:
1. Radio button for 'Follow Teams' (lines 81-98)
2. Radio button for 'Select Leagues' (lines 100-118)
3. Team search content (lines 121-208) - appears BELOW both radio buttons
4. League picker content (lines 210-231)

**Expected behavior:**
The content for each mode should appear directly below its corresponding radio button, not below all radio buttons.

**Fix approach:**
Restructure the JSX so each radio option contains its associated content within the same visual block, creating a nested/indented appearance that clearly associates content with the selected option.

**File:**
- frontend/src/components/SoccerModeSelector.tsx

title: Soccer mode switch resets form state
description:
When creating a multi-league group, switching between 'Select Leagues' and 'Follow Teams' soccer modes kicks the user back to the beginning, requiring them to click Soccer again.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Create new multi-league group
2. Click on Soccer (expand it in the league picker)
3. Click 'Select Leagues' mode
4. Switch to 'Follow Teams' mode
5. User is kicked back to the beginning

**Root cause:**
In SoccerModeSelector.tsx lines 41-49, handleModeChange clears the opposite mode's data:
- Switching to 'teams' mode calls onLeaguesChange([]) - clearing selected soccer leagues
- This state change may cause parent form to re-render/collapse

**Files to investigate:**
- frontend/src/components/SoccerModeSelector.tsx
- frontend/src/pages/EventGroupForm.tsx

title: Multi-template assignment not used in EPG generation
description:
When templates are assigned via 'Manage Templates' (group_templates table), the EPG processor ignores them and only checks group.template_id. This causes:

1. Generation skipped - groups with only group_templates entries show 'no template assigned' and are skipped
2. Display bug - UI shows 'Unassigned' even when templates exist in group_templates

Root cause: event_group_processor.py only checks group.template_id, doesn't use get_template_for_event() which properly resolves from both sources.

Fix needed in:
- teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py (template check and loading)
- frontend/src/pages/EventGroups.tsx (template display column)

Reported by user running 2.1.0-dev+e397b7f with NCAA basketball + NHL multi-group.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 058dca2. Templates assigned via Manage Templates now properly detected and used during EPG generation.

title: Simplify SoccerModeSelector - remove redundant 'All Soccer Leagues' option
description: Consolidate SoccerModeSelector to only have two top-level options: 1) Follow Teams (auto league discovery based on team following), 2) Select Leagues (with 'Select All' as first option). Remove the separate 'All Soccer Leagues' radio since it duplicates the 'Select All' functionality.

title: Analyze user logs for last_result bug
description: Review /mnt/inbox/_teamarr_logs files to understand the data flow and identify why last game is being skipped
close_reason: Root cause: _get_past_games_from_schedule used date-only comparison but ESPN returns UTC dates. Fixed by using full datetime comparison (event_datetime >= now_utc).

title: Investigate incorrect last_result showing wrong game
description: Users report that for several NHL teams (Minnesota Wild, Montreal Canadiens, Nashville Predators, New Jersey Devils, New York Islanders), the previous game result is showing the game BEFORE the actual last game - skipping the most recent completed game. This persists even after clearing game data cache via UI.
close_reason: Fixed: ESPN date parsing used local date.today() vs UTC dates, causing late-night US games to be excluded. Now uses proper datetime comparison.

title: Add combat condition evaluators
description:

- is_submission: True if result was submission
- is_decision: True if fight went to decision
- is_finish: True if KO/TKO/Submission (not decision)

These allow conditional descriptions like:
- 'Fighter wins by {fight_result}!' when is_finish

title: Add results variables to existing combat.py

Existing combat.py already has: fighter1, fighter2, matchup, event_number, event_title, card_segment, card_segment_display, segment times, and bout list variables.

Add new RESULTS section with:
- fight_result: Human-readable result (e.g., 'KO', 'TKO', 'Decision (Unanimous)', 'Submission')
- fight_result_short: Abbreviated (e.g., 'KO', 'TKO', 'UD', 'SUB')
- finish_round: Round number fight ended (e.g., '3')
- finish_time: Time in round (e.g., '3:48')
- finish_info: Combined (e.g., 'R3 3:48')
- weight_class: Full name (e.g., 'Bantamweight')
- weight_class_short: Abbreviated (e.g., 'BW', 'FW', 'LW')
- fighter1_record: First fighter record (e.g., '8-1-0')
- fighter2_record: Second fighter record (e.g., '10-4-0')
- judge_scores: Formatted scorecard (e.g., '29-28, 29-28, 30-27')
- fight_summary: Full result summary (e.g., 'KO R2 4:31' or 'UD 29-28, 29-28, 30-27')

All variables use SuffixRules.BASE_ONLY (event EPG only, no .next/.last for MMA).

title: Update ESPN UFC parser to extract result data
description:
Update teamarr/providers/espn/ufc.py to extract combat-specific data from ESPN API:

1. Parse result method from 'details' array:
   - 'Unofficial Winner Decision' → determine unanimous/split/majority from linescores
   - 'Unofficial Winner Kotko' → TKO (or KO if first round instant)
   - 'Unofficial Winner Submission' → Submission

2. Extract finish round/time from status:
   - status.period = round number
   - status.displayClock = time in round

3. Parse weight class from competition.type.abbreviation

4. Extract fighter records from competitors[].records[].summary

5. Extract linescores (judge scores) from competitors[].linescores when present

Store all data in the new Event fields from ldz.1.

title: UI for promote-to-parent action
description:
Add "Promote to Parent" action in the Event Groups UI.

**Location options** (pick one or both):
1. Action menu in Event Groups list (alongside Edit, Delete)
2. Button in EventGroupForm when editing a child group

**Only visible for**:
- Child groups (has parent_group_id)
- Single-league groups

**Confirmation dialog**:
```
Promote "D" to Parent?

This will reorganize the hierarchy:
• "A" will become a child of "D"
• "B" will become a child of "D"  
• "C" will become a child of "D"
• "D" will become the parent group

Settings from "A" will be copied to "D".
Channels will be reassigned on next EPG generation.

[Cancel] [Promote]
```

**After success**:
- Toast: "D is now the parent group"
- Refresh group list to show new hierarchy

title: API endpoint for promote-to-parent operation
description:
Add POST /api/v1/groups/{group_id}/promote endpoint.

**Request**: No body needed (group_id in URL is the child to promote)

**Operation** (atomic transaction):
1. Get the child group and its current parent
2. Get all siblings (other children of same parent)
3. Copy parent's settings to child (template, channel config) if child has NULL
4. Set old parent's parent_group_id to the promoted child's ID
5. Set all siblings' parent_group_id to the promoted child's ID
6. Clear promoted child's parent_group_id (now a parent)

**Response**:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "promoted_group_id": 4,
  "old_parent_id": 1,
  "reassigned_groups": [1, 2, 3],
  "message": "D is now parent of A, B, C"
}
```

**Validation**:
- Must be a child group (has parent_group_id)
- Single-league group only
- Returns 400 if validation fails

**Database function**: `promote_to_parent(conn, group_id) -> dict`

title: Promote child to parent (swap hierarchy)
description:
Add a "Promote to Parent" action that swaps a child group to become the parent in a single operation.

**Current pain point**: Swapping parent-child relationships requires 6+ manual operations:
1. Detach each sibling
2. Detach self
3. Make old parent a child
4. Re-attach siblings

**Proposed solution**: Single "Promote to Parent" action on child groups that:
1. Detaches all siblings from old parent
2. Makes old parent a child of the promoted group
3. Re-attaches siblings to the new parent
4. Copies necessary settings from old parent to new parent

**Example:**
```
Before:                  After "Promote D":
A (parent)               D (parent)
├── B                    ├── A
├── C                    ├── B
└── D  ← promote         └── C
```

**UI location**: Action button in Event Groups list or in group edit form

**Requires confirmation dialog** explaining:
- Which groups will be affected
- Settings that will be copied
- Channel reassignment on next generation

title: UI warning when changing group parent
description:
Add contextual warnings in EventGroupForm when changing parent relationship.

**Scenarios needing warnings:**

1. **Child → Different Parent**:
   "Streams will be added to [New Parent]'s channels instead of [Old Parent]'s on next generation."

2. **Child → Standalone**:
   "This group will become independent. Settings will be copied from current parent."

3. **Standalone → Child**:
   "This group's streams will be added to [Parent]'s channels. Own channel settings will be ignored."

**Implementation**:
- Add warning Card/Alert below parent selector when value changes
- Use amber/warning styling consistent with other warnings in Settings
- Only show when selection differs from saved value

**File**: `frontend/src/pages/EventGroupForm.tsx`

title: Copy parent settings when detaching child to standalone
description:
When a child group is detached (parent cleared), it has NULL for inherited settings:
- template_id: NULL
- channel_start_number: NULL  
- channel_group_id: NULL

**Problem**: Detached group is broken until user manually configures.

**Solution options**:

A) **Auto-copy on detach** (recommended):
   When `clear_parent_group_id=True`, copy parent's settings to child:
   ```python
   if clear_parent_group_id:
       parent = get_group(conn, current_parent_id)
       if parent:
           updates.append("template_id = ?")
           values.append(parent.template_id)
           # etc for channel settings
   ```

B) **Require settings in request**:
   API rejects detach if no replacement settings provided.

C) **UI handles it**:
   Frontend prompts user to configure before detaching.

**Recommendation**: Option A - auto-copy is least surprising behavior.

title: Backend validation: prevent parent-with-children from becoming child
description:
Add validation in `update_group()` to prevent making a group with children into a child of another group.

**Problem**: If parent A has children B, C and user sets A's parent to D:
- B and C would become "grandchildren" (not supported)
- Or orphaned with invalid parent_group_id

**Solution**: In `teamarr/database/groups.py` `update_group()`:
```python
if parent_group_id is not None:
    # Check if this group has children
    children = conn.execute(
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM event_epg_groups WHERE parent_group_id = ?",
        (group_id,)
    ).fetchone()[0]
    if children > 0:
        raise ValueError("Cannot set parent on a group that has children")
```

**Also add to API layer** for proper error response.

title: UI for changing group parent in EventGroupForm
description:


title: Handle empty fighter IDs from ESPN
description:
**Problem**: ESPN scoreboard returns `id: None` for UFC fighters. Parser converts to empty string via `str(athlete.get("id", ""))`.

**Effect**: When `get_team()` is called with empty team_id, URL becomes `/teams/` causing 404 errors.

**Options**:
1. Generate synthetic IDs for fighters (e.g., hash of name)
2. Skip team lookups when ID is empty
3. Add guard in `get_team()` for empty IDs

**Location**: `teamarr/providers/espn/ufc.py` - `_parse_fighter_as_team()` method

title: UFC Pipeline Remaining Issues
description:
Tracking remaining UFC pipeline issues discovered after kg1 fixes.

## Issues Found (2026-01-31 Analysis)

### Issue 1: Lax Fighter Name Matching (6dx.2) ⚠️ HIGH PRIORITY
Strategy 2 uses dangerous substring matching instead of proper fuzzy matching:
```python
# Current (bad):
if len(last_name) >= 4 and last_name in stream_lower:
    return MATCHED  # False positives!
```

**Root cause**: Classifier already extracts fighters via separator detection (`classified.team1`, `classified.team2`), but event_matcher ignores them and does naive substring search.

**Fix**: Use fuzzy matching on extracted fighters (same as team_vs_team).

### Issue 2: Time-Based Segment Detection - No Distance Threshold (6dx.1)
Streams with garbage times match segments with 960+ minute distances.

**Fix**: Add MAX_SEGMENT_TIME_DISTANCE constant (~3 hours).

### Issue 3: Empty Fighter IDs → 404 Spam (6dx.3)
ESPN returns `id: None` for fighters. Parser converts to empty string, causing 404s.

**Fix**: Skip team lookups when ID is empty or generate synthetic IDs.

### Issue 4: get_team() Missing Combat Sports Guard (6dx.4)
`get_team()` lacks the LEAGUES_WITHOUT_TEAMS guard that `get_league_teams()` has.

**Fix**: Add early return in `get_team()` for combat sports leagues.

## Related
- Supersedes closed epic teamarrv2-kg1
- Blocks teamarrv2-kar (UFC Card Segments Phases 3-5)

title: Fix UFC stream segment distribution across channels
description:
**Problem**: Primary, backup, and third streams all go to one channel (e.g., only prelims) instead of being distributed across early_prelims, prelims, and main_card.

**Expected Behavior**:
- If stream has segment keyword (prelims, main card, early prelims) → use that segment
- If stream has no segment keyword but has time → disambiguate by time
- If stream has no segment keyword AND no time → fallback to main_card

**Investigation Needed**:
1. Are streams being correctly classified by segment?
2. Is the segment keyword detection working?
3. Is time-based disambiguation being applied when no keyword?
4. Are backup streams labeled differently than primary?

**Files**:
- teamarr/consumers/ufc_segments.py (expand_ufc_segments, disambiguate_prelims_by_time)
- teamarr/consumers/matching/classifier.py (detect_card_segment)

**Note**: This issue may be dependent on kg1.1 being fixed first - if streams shouldn't match at all (UFC Fight Pass 24/7), then segment assignment is moot.

title: UFC Matching & Segment Optimization
description:
Epic for fixing UFC-related matching, classification, and segment expansion issues.

## Issues Identified

### 1. Wrong UFC Event Matching
Streams match to wrong UFC event numbers (e.g., UFC 324 instead of UFC 325) due to:
- Substring matching on event numbers without date validation
- Potential stale/cached events in the event list
- ESPN returning multiple events in scoreboard response

### 2. Non-UFC Streams Matching to UFC Events  
Streams like 'UFC Fight Pass 24/7', 'UAE Warriors', 'BJJ Clubes', 'Cage Warriors', 'LUX' incorrectly match to UFC events because:
- Overly broad EVENT_CARD keywords (any stream with 'ufc' matches)
- Single-event fallback matches ANY stream with keywords to the only event
- No validation that stream represents a numbered UFC event

### 3. Stream Segment Distribution
Primary/backup/third streams all going to one prelims channel instead of being distributed across early_prelims/prelims/main_card because:
- All streams with same/no segment keywords grouped together
- No logic to distribute backup streams to different segments

### 4. ESPN 404 Spam
Repeated HTTP 404 errors for /sports/mma/ufc/teams/ endpoint (UFC doesn't have teams)

title: Cleanup: Remove deprecated categories and constants
description:
Final cleanup after migration is stable:

REMOVE FROM SCHEMA:
- Remove old category values from CHECK constraint (combat_sports renamed to event_type_keywords)
- Drop deprecated custom_regex_* flat columns (after confirming JSON migration)

REMOVE FROM CONSTANTS (constants.py):

- PLACEHOLDER_PATTERNS (if moved to custom_regex defaults)
- CARD_SEGMENT_PATTERNS (if moved to custom_regex defaults)  
- COMBAT_SPORTS_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS (if moved to custom_regex defaults)
- GAME_SEPARATORS (if moved to custom_regex defaults)
- EVENT_CARD_KEYWORDS dict (replaced by event_type system)

title: Consolidate MatchOutcome and MatchedStreamResult
description:
Fix lossy conversion between MatchOutcome and MatchedStreamResult.

BACKGROUND:
The two types are INTENTIONAL layered abstraction:
- MatchOutcome: Pure matching result ("did we find a match?")
- MatchedStreamResult: Business-level result ("should we include this stream?")

The conversion in _outcome_to_result() does real work:
- Applies inclusion rules (league filtering, final event check)
- Merges classification data (team1, team2, card_segment)
- Flattens enums to strings for human readability

PROBLEM:
The conversion is LOSSY - it drops important fields:
- failed_reason: not copied (caused AttributeError bug)
- filtered_reason: flattened to string, enum lost
- excluded_reason: not copied
- detail: not copied

SOLUTION:
Make the conversion lossless - copy all fields from MatchOutcome:
1. Add missing fields to MatchedStreamResult
2. Update _outcome_to_result() to copy them
3. Consumers can access full detail when needed

This preserves the intentional layered design while fixing the data loss.

title: Refactor classifier to use DetectionKeywordService
description:
Update teamarr/consumers/matching/classifier.py to use DetectionKeywordService instead of importing constants directly.

CHANGES:

Before:
```python
from teamarr.utilities.constants import (
    CARD_SEGMENT_PATTERNS,
    EVENT_CARD_KEYWORDS,
    GAME_SEPARATORS,
    ...
)

def is_event_card(text: str, ...) -> bool:
    for _league, keywords in EVENT_CARD_KEYWORDS.items():
        for keyword in keywords:
            if keyword.lower() in text_lower:
                return True
```

After:
```python
from teamarr.services.detection_keywords import DetectionKeywordService

def is_event_card(text: str, ...) -> bool:
    return DetectionKeywordService.is_combat_sport(text)
```

FUNCTIONS TO UPDATE:
- is_placeholder() → use service
- find_game_separator() → use service
- detect_league_hint() → use service
- detect_sport_hint() → use service
- is_event_card() → use service
- detect_card_segment() → use service
- is_ufc_excluded() → use service

This decouples classifier from constants, enabling DB-backed customization later.

title: Create DetectionKeywordService (loads from constants)
description:
Create teamarr/services/detection_keywords.py

SERVICE DESIGN:
- Loads detection patterns from constants.py (Phase 1)
- Later: reads from DB with constants as fallback (Phase 2)
- Caches compiled regex patterns for performance
- Provides typed accessors per category

CATEGORIES:
- combat_sports: list[str] - keywords that indicate EVENT_CARD
- league_hints: list[tuple[Pattern, str|list[str]]] - pattern → league code(s)
- sport_hints: list[tuple[Pattern, str]] - pattern → sport name
- placeholders: list[Pattern] - patterns to skip
- card_segments: list[tuple[Pattern, str]] - pattern → segment name
- exclusions: list[Pattern] - patterns to exclude (weigh-ins, etc.)
- separators: list[str] - game separators (vs, @, at)

API:
```python
class DetectionKeywordService:
    @classmethod
    def get_combat_keywords(cls) -> list[str]: ...
    
    @classmethod
    def get_league_hints(cls) -> list[tuple[Pattern, str|list[str]]]: ...
    
    @classmethod
    def is_combat_sport(cls, text: str) -> bool: ...
    
    @classmethod
    def detect_league(cls, text: str) -> str|list[str]|None: ...
    
    @classmethod
    def invalidate_cache(cls): ...
```

This creates the abstraction layer - classifier uses service, service uses constants.
Later we swap constants for DB without changing classifier.

title: Create detection_keywords database table
description:
Add table to schema.sql for user-defined detection keywords:

CREATE TABLE detection_keywords (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    category TEXT NOT NULL,  -- 'combat_sports', 'league_hints', 'sport_hints', 'placeholders', 'card_segments', 'exclusions'
    keyword TEXT NOT NULL,
    pattern TEXT,            -- Optional regex pattern (if is_regex=true)
    is_regex BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0,
    target_value TEXT,       -- For league_hints: the league code(s) to map to
    enabled BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    UNIQUE(category, keyword)
);

Include migration for existing databases.

title: Refactor EVENT_CARD_KEYWORDS to unified COMBAT_SPORTS_KEYWORDS
description:

EVENT_CARD_KEYWORDS = {
    'ufc': ['ufc', 'fight night', ...],
    'boxing': ['boxing', 'pbc', ...],
}

AFTER:
COMBAT_SPORTS_KEYWORDS = [
    # Organizations
    'ufc', 'bellator', 'pfl', 'one fc', 'pbc', 'top rank', 'matchroom',
    # Weight classes (universal)
    'heavyweight', 'middleweight', 'lightweight', 'welterweight', ...
    # Event terms
    'championship', 'title fight', 'title bout', 'undisputed',
    # Sanctioning bodies

title: Design scalable custom regex field architecture
description:
Design the Detection Library architecture:

DATABASE:
- detection_keywords table: id, category, pattern, is_regex, maps_to, is_builtin, enabled, description
- Single table with category column (simpler than 10+ separate tables)
- Categories: combat_sports, league_hints, sport_hints, placeholders, card_segments, exclusions, separators, provider_prefixes, city_translations, broadcast_networks

API:
- CRUD endpoints for detection keywords
- Filter by category
- Reset to defaults per category
- Bulk import/export

ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS (see plans/detection-library-architecture.md):
✓ Single table with category column (not separate tables)
✓ Team Aliases stays separate (different data model)
✓ Built-ins can be disabled, not deleted
✓ User patterns have precedence
✓ Detection tab in Settings (not new page)
✓ Service with caching, invalidate on DB change

Deliver: Architecture decision document ✓ plans/detection-library-architecture.md

title: Custom regex for non team-vs-team events
description:
Extensible detection system for non-team sports (combat, tennis, golf, racing) with user-configurable keyword libraries and separate custom regex flows per event type.

ARCHITECTURE (Top-Down):
1. Detect event type first (TEAM_VS_TEAM, EVENT_CARD, FIELD_EVENT)
2. Route to specific custom regex/extraction flow per type
3. Separate fields: custom_regex_teams, custom_regex_fighters, custom_regex_competitors

PHASES:

Phase 1: Detection Logic (no DB yet)
- Unify COMBAT_SPORTS_KEYWORDS (refactor constants.py)
- Create DetectionKeywordService (loads from constants)
- Refactor classifier to use service
- Add separate custom regex fields per event type

Phase 2: Persistence
- Create detection_keywords DB table
- API endpoints (CRUD, reset, import/export)
- Service reads DB + constants (user overrides built-in)

Phase 3: User Interface
- Detection Library tab in Settings
- Consolidated view with Team Aliases

Phase 4: New Sports (blocked by league enablement)
- Enable golf/motorsports leagues
- Research stream formats
- Implement detection patterns

DETECTION LIBRARY SCOPE:
- Combat Sports Keywords (classify as event card)
- League Hint Patterns (detect league from stream name)
- Sport Hint Patterns (detect sport from stream name)
- Placeholder Patterns (skip filler streams)
- Card Segment Patterns (detect prelims/main card)
- Exclusion Patterns (skip weigh-ins, press conferences)
- Game Separators (vs, @, at patterns)
- Team Aliases (existing - consider consolidated UI)

LABELS: roadmap

title: Verify EFL matching works end-to-end
description: Test that Paramount+ EFL streams match correctly after fix

title: Fix EFL matching issue
description: Apply fix based on investigation findings

title: Investigate why EFL streams fail matching despite league hint
description:
Paramount+ streams with 'English Football League:' prefix are failing to match.

TEST STREAMS:
- Paramount+ 06: English Football League: Grimsby Town vs. Milton Keynes Dons
- Paramount+ 03: English Football League: Sheffield Wednesday vs. Wrexham
- etc.

INVESTIGATION FINDINGS:
1. ✅ League hint detection WORKS: returns ['eng.2', 'eng.3', 'eng.4', 'eng.fa']
2. ✅ Classification: TEAM_VS_TEAM (correct)
3. ✅ Team extraction: Grimsby Town, Milton Keynes Dons (correct)
4. ✅ Events exist in cache: eng.4 has Grimsby Town vs Milton Keynes Dons
5. ✅ Fuzzy matching: 100% score (would pass threshold)
6. ✅ Date comparison: 2026-01-31 matches

ROOT CAUSE FOUND:
- Stream classifier extracts sport_hint='Football' from 'English Football League'
- ESPN provider stores sport as 'soccer'
- Code in team_matcher.py line 640-642 compares sport_hint with event.sport
- Comparison 'soccer' != 'football' fails and skips the event

FIX NEEDED:
- Add sport name aliasing to treat 'football' and 'soccer' as equivalent

title: Scheduled channel reset for Jellyfin logo cache issues
description:
Add option to schedule periodic channel reset (purge all Teamarr channels from Dispatcharr) on a separate cron schedule. This helps users whose media server (Jellyfin) caches channel logos and needs channels deleted to refresh them.

**Implementation:**
- Add `channel_reset_enabled` and `channel_reset_cron` settings
- Add UI card in Advanced Settings with subtitle explaining use case

- Fix missing `is_in_progress()` check in `execute_reset_channels()`
