Upstream issue: https://github.com/Pharaoh-Labs/teamarr/issues/124

title: Build stream timezone dropdown UI component
description:
Create a user-friendly timezone dropdown for the Event Group form.

**Requirements**:
1. Dropdown with common timezones for sports broadcasting
2. Format: 'ET (New York)' / 'PT (Los Angeles)' / 'GMT (London)' etc.
3. One representative city per timezone (recognizable, major city)
4. Include guidance text explaining when to use this setting

**Timezones to include** (subset of TZ_ABBREVIATION_MAP for UI):
- ET (New York) - America/New_York
- CT (Chicago) - America/Chicago
- MT (Denver) - America/Denver
- PT (Los Angeles) - America/Los_Angeles
- GMT/UTC (London) - Europe/London
- CET (Paris) - Europe/Paris
- AEST (Sydney) - Australia/Sydney
- JST (Tokyo) - Asia/Tokyo

**Guidance text**:
'Select the timezone used by your IPTV provider for stream date/times. Useful when midnight crossover between timezones causes mismatches and streams don't include timezone indicators.'
close_reason: Implemented StreamTimezoneSelector component with dropdown showing major timezones. Added to EventGroupForm, bulk import modal, and bulk edit modal.

title: Capture timezone indicators from stream names
description:
Update datetime extraction to capture timezone indicators when present.

**Changes needed**:
1. Update TIME_PATTERNS in normalizer.py to optionally capture TZ suffix (ET, PT, CT, MT, EST, PST, CST, MST, EDT, PDT, CDT, MDT, GMT, UTC, CET, etc.)
2. Add `extracted_tz: str | None` field to NormalizedStream dataclass
3. Stop stripping TZ codes in classifier.py - capture them instead
4. Update custom time regex UI to show TZ capture group option

**TZ indicator mapping** (for later use):
- ET/EST/EDT → America/New_York
- PT/PST/PDT → America/Los_Angeles
- CT/CST/CDT → America/Chicago
- MT/MST/MDT → America/Denver
- GMT/UTC → UTC
- CET/CEST → Europe/Paris

This captured TZ takes priority over the group's stream_timezone setting.
close_reason: Added TZ capture to normalizer.py: TZ_ABBREVIATION_MAP with 50+ abbreviations mapped to IANA names. Updated TIME_PATTERNS to capture optional TZ suffix. Updated extract_and_mask_datetime() to return 4-tuple including TZ. Added extracted_tz field to NormalizedStream dataclass.

title: Comment on PR #118 with timezone solution
description:
After implementation is complete and pushed to dev, comment on PR #118 explaining the solution to Sp0rtsFr3ak.

**Comment should explain**:
1. We've added full timezone awareness to stream datetime parsing
2. Groups now have a 'Stream Timezone' setting for providers that don't include TZ indicators
3. How to configure it for their UK timezone streams use case
4. The priority order: explicit TZ in stream > group setting > local fallback

Link to the relevant commits in dev branch.
close_reason: Posted comment on PR #118 with timezone solution details

title: Apply configured timezone during date extraction
description:
Modify the stream date extraction logic to use the group's configured timezone.

**Implementation**:
1. When extracting datetime from stream name, check if group has stream_timezone set
2. If set, interpret the parsed datetime as being in that timezone
3. Convert to UTC or local as needed for event matching
4. If no group timezone set and no TZ indicator in stream, fall back to local timezone (current behavior)

**Priority order for timezone determination**:
1. Explicit TZ indicator in stream name (ET, PT, GMT, etc.) - always wins
2. Group's stream_timezone setting
3. System local timezone (fallback)

This ensures backwards compatibility while enabling proper TZ handling.
close_reason: Applied TZ-aware date matching. Updated MatchContext to include stream_tz. Modified date/time comparison logic to use stream_tz when comparing extracted dates to event dates. Updated StreamMatcher and EventGroupProcessor to pass stream_timezone through the call chain.

title: Add group-level timezone setting
description:
Add a per-group setting to specify the timezone for stream datetime parsing.

**Schema change**: Add 'stream_timezone' column to event_epg_groups table (nullable, defaults to null meaning 'use local timezone')

**UI**: Add timezone selector to EventGroupForm with guidance text:
'Stream Timezone: Select the timezone used by your IPTV provider for dates/times in stream names. This helps match streams correctly when providers use a different timezone than your local system. Useful when midnight crossover between timezones causes date mismatches and the provider does not include timezone indicators (ET, PT, GMT, etc.) in stream names.'

**Values**: Standard timezone list (America/New_York, Europe/London, etc.) or common abbreviations
close_reason: Added stream_timezone column to event_epg_groups table - schema v47. Updated groups.py (dataclass, from_row, create, update), API routes, and tests.

title: Audit timezone processing in date matching
description:
Determine if/how timezone information is used when matching stream dates to events.

Questions to answer:
1. When a TZ indicator IS found in a stream name, is it actually processed?
2. How are parsed datetimes compared to event datetimes?
3. Are event datetimes stored with timezone info?
4. Is there any timezone conversion happening currently?
5. What assumptions are made about timezones (local, UTC, etc.)?

This determines whether we need to add TZ processing or fix existing processing.
close_reason: Audit complete: Events stored as UTC (ESPN parses ISO with Z suffix). user_tz converts for display/compare. Stream dates are naive - compared directly without TZ conversion. This is the root cause of mismatches.

title: Research datetime parsing in stream name parser
description:
Investigate how the stream name parser captures datetime strings.

Questions to answer:
1. Where is datetime extraction implemented? (likely in matching/ or a parser module)
2. What regex/patterns are used to capture dates and times?
3. Does the parser capture timezone indicators (ET, PT, GMT, CET, etc.)?
4. What datetime formats are currently supported?
5. How are captured datetimes stored/returned?

Document findings for the next task.
close_reason: Research complete: TZ indicators detected but stripped, not captured. No TZ field in NormalizedStream. Naive dates compared to user_tz events causing mismatch.

title: Stream Timezone Awareness
description:
Full timezone awareness for stream datetime parsing and matching.

**Problem**: When IPTV providers include dates/times in stream names but no timezone indicator, streams may fail to match events due to midnight crossover between the user's local timezone and the event's actual timezone.

**Example**: 'Denver Nuggets vs LA Clippers @ Jan 30 9:00 PM' - if parsed in UK timezone, this becomes Jan 31 4:00 AM, causing a date mismatch with the Jan 30 event.

**Solution**: 
1. Audit current datetime parsing for TZ capture/processing
2. Add per-group timezone setting for streams without TZ indicators
3. Apply configured TZ during date extraction for matching

Related: PR #118 comment from Sp0rtsFr3ak about European timezone date adjustment
close_reason: Stream Timezone Awareness feature complete in dev

title: Research and create implementation beads
description:
Comprehensive research phase to create detailed implementation beads.

## Research Tasks
1. Analyze real tennis stream naming patterns from common providers
2. Document ESPN tennis API fully (all fields, edge cases)
3. Design tennis-specific matcher extending current architecture
4. Define template variables for tennis (player names, court, round, ranking)
5. Determine league configs needed (ATP, WTA, grand slams vs tour events)

## Deliverables
- Stream pattern analysis document
- Tennis matcher design spec
- Template variable list
- Database schema changes
- Ordered implementation beads with dependencies

## Key Questions to Answer
- How to extract court numbers from stream names (regex patterns)
- How to handle player name variations (Djokovic vs N. Djokovic vs Novak)
- Time window tolerance for disambiguation
- Whether to treat each match as separate event or tournament as event
- How doubles matches work (team names vs player pairs)

title: Tennis Support
description:
Add tennis support with court-based and player-based stream matching. ESPN provides per-match data with court assignments, player names, rounds, and exact times. Key challenge: stream name disambiguation using court numbers, player names, and time windows.

## ESPN API Structure
- Tournaments contain groupings (Women's Singles, Men's Singles, etc.)
- Each match has: court, round, players, start time, rankings
- Courts: numbered (Court 5) and named (Rod Laver Arena)

## Matching Challenges
- Court-based streams: 'Australian Open Court 5'
- Player-based streams: 'Rybakina vs Sabalenka'
- Time disambiguation: multiple concurrent matches
- Named vs numbered courts

## Leagues
- ATP (Men's tour)
- WTA (Women's tour)
- Grand Slams: Australian Open, French Open, Wimbledon, US Open

Relates to closed GitHub issue #24.

Build the Follow Teams mode UI for soccer.

**UX Flow:**
1. User searches/selects teams (reuse existing TeamPicker patterns)
2. System shows discovered competitions for those teams
3. User sees preview: 'Manchester United plays in: EPL, FA Cup, EFL Cup, Europa League'
4. Leagues are auto-selected based on team's active competitions

**Technical:**
- Fetches team competitions via API (4pe.4)
- Displays grouped by team with competition badges
- Auto-updates if team enters new competition (promotion, cup qualification)

**Edge cases:**
- Team in multiple competitions (show all)

title: Add soccer_mode field to event_epg_groups
description:
Add soccer_mode column to event_epg_groups table.

**Values:**
- 'all' - Subscribe to all soccer leagues (auto-include new ones)
- 'teams' - Follow selected teams across their competitions
- 'manual' - Explicit league selection (current behavior)

**Migration logic:**
1. Count total available soccer leagues in DB
2. For each multi-league group containing soccer:
   - If selected_soccer_leagues == total_soccer_leagues → 'all'
   - If selected_soccer_leagues < total_soccer_leagues → 'manual' (preserve their trimmed selection)
   - If no soccer leagues selected → NULL (not applicable)

This preserves work users have already done trimming their soccer leagues.

description:
BLOCKED: Requires authoritative hierarchy data from ESPN API.

2. Use simple league_code prefix grouping without storing in DB
3. Skip hierarchy - just show flat list with search

Manual tree view (4pe.9) may not need this if we use option 2 or 3.

title: Update event group matching for soccer modes
description:
Update event group matching to respect soccer_mode field.

**Mode behavior:**
- `soccer_mode = 'all'`: Dynamically include ALL enabled soccer leagues
- `soccer_mode = 'manual'`: Use explicit leagues array (current behavior)
- `soccer_mode = NULL`: Non-soccer, use leagues array (current behavior)
- `soccer_mode = 'teams'`: DEFERRED - requires soccer_followed_teams field

**Implementation:**
1. Add helper method to resolve effective leagues based on soccer_mode
2. Call it wherever group.leagues is used for event fetching/matching
3. For 'all' mode, query leagues table for all enabled soccer leagues

**Files to modify:**
- teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py
- teamarr/database/groups.py (add helper query)
close_reason: Implemented soccer_mode league resolution in event_group_processor.py - 'all' mode dynamically fetches all enabled soccer leagues, 'manual'/NULL use explicit leagues. Added get_enabled_soccer_leagues helper and soccer_mode to EventEPGGroup dataclass.

title: Soccer League/Team Selection Modes
description:
Implement 3 selection modes for soccer event groups to address #89.

**Modes:**
1. **All Soccer** - Subscribe to all leagues, auto-include new ones
2. **Follow Teams** - Pick teams, auto-discover their competitions (EPL + UCL + cups)
3. **Manual** - Explicit league selection with hierarchy view

**Why:**
- Current UX subscribes to everything by default
- No tree structure like other sports
- Performance impact from unnecessary leagues
- Users want granular control at import time

**Key challenges:**
- Cross-competition discovery (team plays in multiple leagues)
- Promotion/relegation (teams change leagues)
- Soccer league hierarchy (confederation → country → league)

**Reusable components (from research):**
- `LeaguePicker` - Already has soccer special handling, extend with region grouping
- `SearchableMultiSelect` - Used in TemplateAssignmentModal, good for manual mode
- Existing `useSports`, `useLeagues` hooks
- `GET /cache/leagues` endpoint

**Migration:**
- Users with ALL soccer leagues → `soccer_mode = 'all'`
- Users with trimmed soccer leagues → `soccer_mode = 'manual'` (preserve selection)
- This respects work users have already done customizing their groups

**Schema additions needed:**
- `soccer_mode` column on event_epg_groups
- Optional: `region`/`confederation` fields on leagues table for hierarchy

Closes: GitHub #89
close_reason: Implemented soccer selection modes. 'all' mode auto-subscribes to all soccer leagues; 'manual' mode uses LeaguePicker with soccer filter. 'teams' mode (4pe.8) deferred to future release - requires soccer_followed_teams schema.

title: International Sports Research
description:
Research epic for GitHub #122. NO CODE CHANGES - deliverable is documentation of findings.

Goal: Understand what it takes to implement highest-impact international competitions (FIFA World Cup, Euros, FIBA, IIHF, Olympic team sports, handball, rugby).

Findings to document:
- ESPN/TSDB API coverage for each competition
- League IDs and endpoint patterns
- Tournament format handling needs (groups → knockouts)
- National team naming conventions
- Scheduling patterns (annual vs multi-year cycles)
- Estimated implementation effort per competition

Related GitHub Issues:
- #122 - International Sports Support (primary)
- #88 - Olympic Events (overlaps with Olympic team sports research)
- #100 - Rugby League (overlaps with rugby research)
- #104 - European/international hockey (overlaps with IIHF research)
close_reason: All 8 research tasks complete. Findings: plans/research-international-sports.md. 30+ new leagues identified across soccer, basketball, rugby, Olympics. Phase 1 (ESPN Tier 1) can start immediately. Phase 2-3 (TSDB Tier 2) blocked by s9n (BYOK refactor).

title: Add group_templates table to schema.sql
description:
Create group_templates join table:

```sql
CREATE TABLE group_templates (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    group_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    template_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    sport TEXT,              -- NULL = any sport
    leagues JSON,            -- NULL = any league, or ["nfl", "nba"]
    priority INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- unique per group, higher = checked first
    
    FOREIGN KEY (group_id) REFERENCES event_epg_groups(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    FOREIGN KEY (template_id) REFERENCES templates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    UNIQUE(group_id, priority)  -- ensures no ties
);
CREATE INDEX idx_group_templates_group ON group_templates(group_id);
```

Add migration in connection.py to create table.

title: Implement get_template_for_event() resolution
description:
Add resolution function to teamarr/database/groups.py:

```python
def get_template_for_event(conn, group_id: int, sport: str, league: str) -> int | None:
    """Find best matching template_id for event within group.
    
    Checks templates in priority order (highest first).
    First match wins - no tie-breaking needed due to unique priority.
    
    Match criteria:
    - sport: NULL matches any, otherwise must equal event.sport
    - leagues: NULL matches any, otherwise event.league must be in JSON array
    """
```

Integrate into event_group_processor.py:
- Replace single `group.template_id` lookup with `get_template_for_event()`
- Call per-event in the matched_streams loop
- Fall back to None if no templates configured

title: Group Multi-Template Assignment
description:
Allow multiple templates per group with sport/league specificity.

Schema: group_templates(group_id, template_id, sports JSON, leagues JSON)
- sports: NULL = any sport, or JSON array like ["mma", "boxing"]
- leagues: NULL = any league, or JSON array like ["ufc", "bellator"]

Resolution order (specificity):
1. leagues match (event.league in leagues array) - most specific
2. sports match (event.sport in sports array)
3. default (both NULL) - fallback

No priority field needed - specificity IS the priority.

UI: Multi-league groups get a modal for template assignment. Single-league groups keep simple dropdown.

Closes: GitHub #102 (Conditional postgame subtitle)

title: Update documentation for playoff filter bypass
description:
**Files to modify:**
- `docs/guide/settings/teams.md` - Add playoff bypass section
- `docs/guide/teams/team-filters.md` - Document new option

**Documentation content:**

### Playoff Game Bypass

When you have a team filter configured, you can optionally include all playoff games regardless of which teams are playing.

**Setting:** "Include all playoff games regardless of team filter"

**Behavior:**
- **Disabled (default):** Team filter applies to all games including playoffs
- **Enabled:** Playoff/postseason games bypass the team filter

**Example:**
- Your team filter is set to only include NY Rangers and Boston Bruins
- During regular season: Only Rangers and Bruins games appear
- During playoffs with bypass enabled: ALL playoff games appear (Stanley Cup Finals, etc.)

**Supported leagues:**
| Provider | Season Type Detection |
|----------|----------------------|
| ESPN | ✓ Full support |
| HockeyTech | [TBD based on investigation] |
| TheSportsDB | [TBD based on investigation] |

**Note:** If a provider doesn't supply season type data, games from that provider cannot be detected as playoffs and will still be subject to the team filter.

setTeamFilter({ ...teamFilter, bypass_filter_for_playoffs: checked }) } disabled={!teamFilter?.enabled} />

When enabled, playoff and postseason games will bypass the team filter, allowing you to watch championship games for any team.

``` **UX considerations:** - Disable toggle if team filter is disabled (nothing to bypass) - Clear description explaining the behavior - Position near the team filter enable toggle

title: Modify _filter_by_teams to bypass for playoff games
description:
**Files to modify:**
- `teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py`

**Function:** `_filter_by_teams()` at line ~1762

**Current logic:**
```python
def _filter_by_teams(self, matched_streams, group, conn):
    # Get filter settings
    include_teams, exclude_teams, mode = self._get_effective_team_filter(group, conn)
    
    # For each stream, check if team matches filter
    for stream in matched_streams:
        event = stream.event
        # Check team against filter
        ...
```

**Modified logic:**
```python
def _filter_by_teams(self, matched_streams, group, conn):
    from teamarr.database.settings import get_team_filter_settings
    
    # Get filter settings
    include_teams, exclude_teams, mode = self._get_effective_team_filter(group, conn)
    team_filter_settings = get_team_filter_settings(conn)
    bypass_playoffs = team_filter_settings.bypass_filter_for_playoffs
    
    filtered = []
    excluded_count = 0
    bypassed_count = 0
    
    for stream in matched_streams:
        event = stream.event
        
        # Bypass team filter for playoff games if enabled
        if bypass_playoffs and event.is_playoff:
            filtered.append(stream)
            bypassed_count += 1
            logger.debug(
                "[EVENT_EPG] Playoff bypass: %s (team filter not applied)",
                event.name
            )
            continue
        
        # Normal team filter logic
        ...
    
    if bypassed_count > 0:
        logger.info(
            "[EVENT_EPG] Team filter: %d playoff games bypassed, %d excluded",
            bypassed_count, excluded_count
        )
    
    return filtered, excluded_count
```

**Key changes:**
1. Load team filter settings to check bypass flag
2. Check `event.is_playoff` before applying filter
3. Log bypassed games for debugging
4. Count and report bypassed games separately

title: Add is_playoff helper to Event model or utils
description:
**Files to modify:**
- `teamarr/core/types.py` OR create `teamarr/core/utils.py`

**Option A: Add property to Event dataclass**
```python
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Event:
    ...
    season_type: str | None = None
    
    @property
    def is_playoff(self) -> bool:
        """Check if this is a playoff/postseason game."""
        if not self.season_type:
            return False
        st = self.season_type.lower()
        return "post" in st or "playoff" in st
```

Note: Dataclasses with `frozen=True` can have properties.

**Option B: Standalone utility function**
```python
def is_playoff_event(event: Event) -> bool:
    """Check if event is a playoff/postseason game."""
    if not event or not event.season_type:
        return False
    st = event.season_type.lower()
    return "post" in st or "playoff" in st
```

**Recommendation:** Option A (property) is cleaner for usage:
```python
if event.is_playoff and settings.bypass_filter_for_playoffs:
    # Bypass team filter
```

**Reuse existing logic from:**
`teamarr/templates/variables/playoffs.py:37-41` - same detection logic

title: Parse season_type from ESPN API responses
description:
**Files to modify:**
- `teamarr/providers/espn/provider.py`

**ESPN API Response Structure:**
```json
{
  "id": "401547123",
  "season": {
    "year": 2024,
    "type": 3,
    "slug": "post-season"
  },
  ...
}
```

Season type values:
- `1` = Preseason
- `2` = Regular Season
- `3` = Postseason/Playoffs

**Changes to `_parse_event()` (line ~381):**

```python
def _parse_event(self, data: dict, league: str) -> Event | None:
    ...
    # Extract season type
    season = data.get("season", {})
    season_type_num = season.get("type")
    season_type = self._map_season_type(season_type_num)
    
    return Event(
        ...
        season_type=season_type,  # Add this field
    )

def _map_season_type(self, type_num: int | None) -> str | None:
    """Map ESPN season type number to string."""
    if type_num is None:
        return None
    mapping = {
        1: "Preseason",
        2: "Regular Season",
        3: "Postseason",
    }
    return mapping.get(type_num)
```

**Testing:**
- Check scoreboard API response for playoff games
- Verify `is_playoff` template variable now returns "true" for playoff games

title: Ignore Team Filters for Playoff Games
description:
## Problem

Users set team filters to only show their favorite teams' games. During playoffs/championships (Stanley Cup, Super Bowl, World Series, etc.), they want to watch ALL playoff games regardless of which teams are playing, but their team filter blocks non-filtered team games.

**User request:** [Issue #70](https://github.com/Pharaoh-Labs/teamarr/issues/70) by @sethwv

> "I like watching Stanley Cup / Superbowl regardless of teams."

## Current Architecture

### Team Filtering
- **Location:** `teamarr/consumers/event_group_processor.py:1762-1840`
- **Function:** `_filter_by_teams()` checks if event teams match include/exclude lists
- **No playoff awareness** - filters apply equally to all games

### Season Type Data
- **Event model:** `teamarr/core/types.py:66` has `season_type: str | None`
- **Template variables:** `teamarr/templates/variables/playoffs.py` has `is_playoff` logic
- **Problem:** ESPN provider (`provider.py:427-443`) doesn't populate `season_type`

### ESPN API
ESPN provides `season.type` in responses:
- `1` = Preseason
- `2` = Regular Season  
- `3` = Postseason/Playoffs

Currently **not being parsed** into Event objects.

## Proposed Solution

1. **Parse season_type from ESPN API** (and other providers where available)
2. **Add setting:** "Include all playoff games regardless of team filter"
3. **Modify filter logic:** Bypass team filter when event is playoff AND setting enabled
4. **Add UI toggle** in Team Filter settings

## Implementation Plan

### Phase 1: Provider Investigation & Data Extraction
1. Add season_type parsing to ESPN provider
2. Investigate HockeyTech provider for season type data
3. Investigate TSDB provider for season type data
4. Add helper function to determine if event is playoff

### Phase 2: Settings & Database
5. Add `bypass_filter_for_playoffs` setting to team filter settings
6. Database migration for new setting
7. Update settings API and types

### Phase 3: Filter Logic
8. Modify `_filter_by_teams()` to check playoff status
9. Add logging for playoff bypass events

### Phase 4: Frontend UI
10. Add toggle to Team Filter settings in UI
11. Update documentation

## Success Criteria
- Playoff games bypass team filter when setting enabled
- Regular season games still respect team filter
- Works across all providers with season type data
- Clear UI indication of the setting

title: Virtualize Failed/Matched Streams Modals
description:
## Problem

Users with 1000+ failed matches can only see the first 500 in the Failed Matches modal. Same issue exists for Matched Streams modal.

**User request:** [Issue #119](https://github.com/Pharaoh-Labs/teamarr/issues/119)

## Current State

- Both modals fetch up to 500 items and render all in a single table
- No virtual scrolling = slow rendering with many items
- Test Patterns modal already uses `@tanstack/react-virtual` for 10,000+ streams

## Solution: Virtualization (not Pagination)

Use the same virtualization pattern as Test Patterns modal for consistency:
- Single API call fetches all results (remove 500 limit or increase significantly)
- Virtual scrolling renders only visible rows
- Client-side filtering is instant
- Same UX pattern across all modals

**Advantages over pagination:**
- Consistent with existing Test Patterns modal pattern
- No API changes needed (offset/limit/total)
- Instant client-side filtering
- Simpler implementation

## Implementation Plan

1. Create reusable VirtualizedTable component (extract pattern from StreamList.tsx)
2. Apply to Failed Matches modal
3. Apply to Matched Streams modal  
4. Add filter dropdowns (optional enhancement)

## Success Criteria
- Users can scroll through all failed/matched items smoothly
- Rendering stays fast regardless of item count
- Same scrolling UX as Test Patterns modal

title: Logo Management Audit & Refactor
description:
## Overview

Audit and refactor how Teamarr manages channel logos. Current implementation may have non-working/inefficient code for tracking which channels have logos and clearing them during lifecycle management.

## Background

Discord feedback:
> There's an api endpoint to run logo cleanup /api/channels/logos/cleanup/, so a 3rd party app or a dispatcharr plugin could be developed to schedule it. But you can't schedule it within dispatcharr itself

## Current State (Needs Audit)

- Homegrown solution for tracking logos per channel
- Attempts to clear logos during lifecycle management
- May be non-working or inefficient
- Need to identify all logo-related code paths

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Remove Homegrown Logo Tracking
Delete the custom logo tracking code and rely on Dispatcharr's native cleanup API.

### 2. Add Dispatcharr Settings Option
Add a toggle in Dispatcharr integration settings:
- **"Clean up unused logos after generation"** (default: off)
- When enabled: Call `/api/channels/logos/cleanup/` at end of full generation
- Show as a streamed step in the generation toast

### 3. Clear User Expectations
Make it explicit in the UI:
- This cleans ALL unused logos in Dispatcharr, not just ones Teamarr created
- If disabled, Teamarr-uploaded logos persist until manually managed in Dispatcharr

## Implementation Steps

1. **Audit** - Find all logo management code, document what exists
2. **Evaluate** - Determine what's working, what's not, what's redundant
3. **Add setting** - New toggle in Dispatcharr integration settings
4. **Implement hook** - Call cleanup API at end of generation (if enabled)
5. **Stream progress** - Show cleanup step in generation toast
6. **Remove old code** - Delete homegrown logo tracking
7. **Update docs** - Document the new behavior

## UX Considerations

- Cleanup should be opt-in (default off) since it affects ALL unused logos
- Clear warning in settings about scope of cleanup
- Progress indication during cleanup (streamed toast step)

Files: teamarr/providers/espn/client.py

title: ESPN Client Resilience & Configurability
description:
## Overview

Redesign ESPN client for resilience against DNS throttling while maintaining high performance for normal users. ESPN has generous API limits that are practically impossible to hit - the real issue is users with rate-limited DNS (PiHole, AdGuard, router limits) experiencing failures due to connection pool churn.

## Problem Analysis

**Current State:**
- `max_connections=100`, `max_keepalive_connections=10` → connection churn
- Linear backoff (1s, 2s, 3s) → insufficient for DNS recovery
- No jitter → thundering herd on retries
- No configurability → users can't tune for their environment
- No 429 handling → if we ever hit ESPN limits, no recovery
- 100 parallel workers × connection churn = high DNS pressure

**Root Cause:**
Connection pool keeps only 10 connections alive despite allowing 100. Connections get dropped and recreated, each requiring DNS lookup. Users with DNS rate limiting (PiHole default: 1000 queries/min) get throttled.

## Design Principles

1. **Default to high performance** - ESPN is reliable, don't penalize normal users
2. **Maximize connection reuse** - Reduce DNS pressure by keeping connections alive
3. **Smart retry for transient failures** - Exponential backoff with jitter
4. **Reactive 429 handling** - If we ever hit limits, back off gracefully
5. **Env var escape hatches** - Let constrained users tune without code changes
6. **Don't over-engineer** - No proactive rate limiting, no TSDB-style complexity

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Connection Pool Fix (Primary)
```python
# Current: churn-heavy
limits=httpx.Limits(max_connections=100, max_keepalive_connections=10)

# Proposed: maximize reuse
limits=httpx.Limits(
    max_connections=int(os.environ.get("ESPN_MAX_CONNECTIONS", 100)),
    max_keepalive_connections=int(os.environ.get("ESPN_MAX_CONNECTIONS", 100)),
)
```

### 2. Retry Strategy (ESPN-specific)
```python
# ESPN-tuned exponential backoff with jitter
# - Short initial delay (ESPN is fast)
# - Jitter to prevent thundering herd
# - Reasonable cap (not TSDB's 120s - overkill for ESPN)

RETRY_BASE = 0.5  # Start at 500ms (ESPN responds fast)
RETRY_MAX = 10.0  # Cap at 10s (vs TSDB's 120s)
RETRY_COUNT = 3   # Keep current

def _calculate_delay(attempt: int) -> float:
    """Exponential backoff with jitter."""
    base_delay = RETRY_BASE * (2 ** attempt)  # 0.5, 1, 2, 4...
    capped = min(base_delay, RETRY_MAX)
    jitter = random.uniform(0, capped * 0.3)  # ±30% jitter
    return capped + jitter
```

### 3. Reactive 429 Handling
```python
# If ESPN ever rate-limits us (rare), back off gracefully
# NOT proactive like TSDB - just reactive defense

RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_BASE = 5.0   # Start at 5s if we hit 429
RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_MAX = 60.0   # Cap at 60s (vs TSDB's 120s)
RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = 3      # Give up after 3 rate-limit retries

if response.status_code == 429:
    # Respect Retry-After header if present
    retry_after = response.headers.get("Retry-After")
    if retry_after:
        wait = min(float(retry_after), RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_MAX)
    else:
        wait = min(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_BASE * (2 ** attempt), RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_MAX)
    logger.warning("[ESPN] Rate limited (429). Waiting %.1fs before retry.", wait)
    time.sleep(wait)
```

### 4. Environment Variables
| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `ESPN_MAX_CONNECTIONS` | 100 | Max concurrent connections (also sets keepalive) |
| `ESPN_MAX_WORKERS` | 100 | Max parallel workers for team/event processing |
| `ESPN_TIMEOUT` | 10 | Request timeout in seconds |
| `ESPN_RETRY_COUNT` | 3 | Number of retry attempts |

### 5. Connection Error Classification
Distinguish between:
- **429 Rate Limit** - Longer backoff, respect Retry-After header
- **DNS/Connection errors** - Standard exponential backoff with jitter
- **HTTP 5xx errors** - Standard retry (ESPN server issue, rare)
- **Timeouts** - Standard retry

## Impact Assessment

**Performance (normal users):**
- No change to defaults
- Connection reuse improvement may actually speed things up

**Reliability (constrained users):**
- Jitter prevents synchronized retry storms
- Exponential backoff gives DNS time to recover
- Env vars let users tune: `ESPN_MAX_WORKERS=20 ESPN_MAX_CONNECTIONS=20`

**Resilience:**
- 429 handling provides safety net if ESPN ever throttles us
- Retry-After header respected for graceful recovery

**Code complexity:**
- Moderate - ~50-60 lines changed in client.py
- ~5 lines each in team_processor.py, event_group_processor.py, cache/refresh.py

## Non-Goals

- No proactive rate limiting (ESPN doesn't need it)
- No circuit breaker (overkill for ESPN's reliability)
- No statistics tracking (TSDB needs it for free tier, ESPN doesn't)
- No request queuing or throttling

title: Test Patterns modal polish
Rewrite regex test patterns modal
description:
## Overview

Rewrite the regex test patterns modal from PR #118 to properly mirror the EventGroupForm fields and provide real-time stream-level regex preview with interactive pattern generation.

## Problem

PR #118 includes a 1160-line modal that tests regex patterns against streams. While the concept is sound, the implementation has significant issues (redundant regex compilation, no virtualization, 1160-line monolith, hardcoded league codes, missing React best practices) and — more importantly — doesn't mirror the actual form fields. It invents its own pattern UI instead of reflecting skip_builtin_filter, include/exclude, teams, date, time, and league extraction as they actually work.

## Desired Behavior

1. Modal loads ALL streams from the current group — raw, unfiltered, including placeholders
2. User interacts with the SAME regex fields available in EventGroupForm:
   - skip_builtin_filter (checkbox) — dynamically shows which streams would be filtered
   - stream_include_regex + enabled — highlights included streams
   - stream_exclude_regex + enabled — highlights/strikes excluded streams
   - custom_regex_teams + enabled — highlights team1/team2 extraction
   - custom_regex_date + enabled — highlights date extraction
   - custom_regex_time + enabled — highlights time extraction
   - custom_regex_league + enabled — highlights league extraction
3. All highlighting is real-time as the user types
4. Interactive suggestion: user clicks/highlights text in a stream name, chooses what it represents (team1, team2, date, time, league), and a regex is generated automatically using proper Python named groups
5. Apply button sends all patterns back to the form
6. Works on groups with 10,000+ streams (virtualized list)

## Architecture

### File Structure
```
frontend/src/components/TestPatternsModal/
  index.tsx                    — Modal shell, state management, Apply logic
  StreamList.tsx               — Virtualized stream list (@tanstack/react-virtual)
  StreamItem.tsx               — Single stream with multi-layer color highlighting
  PatternPanel.tsx             — Regex input fields mirroring form (6 patterns + skip_builtin)
  InteractiveSelector.tsx      — Click-to-select text overlay for pattern generation

frontend/src/lib/
  regex-utils.ts               — Pure functions: validate, test, python-to-js, js-to-python
  pattern-generator.ts         — Generate regex from user text selections (escape, generalize)
```

### Color Coding
| Field | Color | Visual |
|-------|-------|--------|
| Built-in filtered | Gray | Dimmed + strike-through |
| Exclude regex | Red | Strike-through |
| Include regex | Green border/bg | Subtle highlight on matching streams |
| team1 | Blue | Highlighted text span |
| team2 | Cyan | Highlighted text span |
| date | Yellow | Highlighted text span |
| time | Orange | Highlighted text span |
| league | Purple | Highlighted text span |

### Stream Loading
Need a lightweight API endpoint that returns raw stream names for a group WITHOUT running the full matching pipeline. The existing preview endpoint (/groups/{id}/preview) runs full matching which is expensive and not what we want — we need ALL streams, unprocessed.

Options:
- New endpoint: GET /groups/{id}/streams/raw — returns stream_id + stream_name only
- Or fetch from the M3U/Dispatcharr source directly

### Interactive Pattern Generation
When user selects text in a stream:
1. A popover appears: 'What is this?' → team1 / team2 / date / time / league
2. Selection is analyzed across all streams to find the common pattern
3. Regex is generated with proper named group: (?P<team1>...), (?P<date>...), etc.
4. Generated regex appears in the corresponding field
5. Live highlighting updates immediately

### Python <-> JS Regex Conversion
- Display/edit in Python syntax (what gets saved)
- Convert to JS for client-side testing: (?P<name>...) → (?<name>...)
- Convert back on save: (?<name>...) → (?P<name>...)
- Handle: named groups, backreferences. Skip exotic features (conditionals, atomic groups)

## Dependencies
- Add: @tanstack/react-virtual (virtualized list for 10,000+ streams)
- Existing: @tanstack/react-query (data fetching), lucide-react (icons), class-variance-authority (styling)

## Implementation Beads (to be created)

1. Add raw streams API endpoint
2. Create regex-utils.ts and pattern-generator.ts utility modules
3. Create StreamItem and StreamList components with virtualization
4. Create PatternPanel mirroring form fields
5. Create InteractiveSelector for click-to-generate
6. Create main TestPatternsModal and integrate with EventGroupForm
7. Add @tanstack/react-virtual dependency

## Verification
- npm run build (frontend compiles)
- Modal opens from EventGroupForm
- All streams load (including placeholders)
- Each pattern field highlights correctly in real time
- Interactive selection generates valid regex
- Apply button populates form fields
- Works on a group with 1000+ streams without freezing
- Python named groups round-trip correctly

## PR #118 Feature 3 Response
Reject the PR's implementation. We will rewrite to our standards with:
- Proper file decomposition (7 files vs 1 monolith)
- Mirrors actual form fields instead of inventing its own
- Virtualized stream list for large groups
- Interactive pattern generation (click-to-select)
- Correct React patterns (stable keys, memoization, abort controllers)
- No hardcoded league codes

title: Draft PR #118 review response
description:
## PR #118 Response Drafts

Drafts for responding to each of the three features in PR #118. To be finalized after all three features are reviewed.

---

### Feature 1: Startup Cache Refresh Setting — REJECT

Thanks for looking into this — the underlying observation is spot on. Hammering ESPN/HockeyTech on every restart when the cache was refreshed 10 minutes ago is wasteful, and we appreciate the thoughtful implementation.

After discussing it, we're going a slightly different direction. Rather than exposing a user-facing setting, we want cache freshness to be something users never have to think about. The mental model should be: 'it just works.' Our concern with a configurable threshold is that most users won't know what the cache is or why it matters, and a setting they don't understand is a setting that gets misconfigured.

Here's what we've landed on:

- **Startup**: Always refresh unconditionally (no change). Startups are infrequent enough that ~15 seconds of provider calls is acceptable, and it guarantees a fresh baseline every time the container comes up.
- **Scheduled**: Changed from 7-day to 1-day staleness check. The cron scheduler already runs on the user's configured interval (hourly by default for EPG generation). On each tick it now checks whether the cache is older than 1 day — if so, it refreshes silently in the background. This covers long-running instances that haven't restarted.
- **Manual**: The refresh button in the UI stays as-is — available for troubleshooting or when a user knows they need fresh data (e.g., a new league was just added to a provider).
- **Development**: We added a `SKIP_CACHE_REFRESH=true` environment variable that skips the startup refresh. Documented in the Docker Compose example. This is strictly a developer convenience — not exposed as a user setting.

The net effect is that cache is always <1 day old with zero user configuration. The three refresh paths (startup, scheduled, manual) already share the same CacheService code, so there's no redundancy to clean up — just the staleness threshold change.

We won't be merging the startup cache setting, migration, or API endpoint from this feature, but we appreciate the thinking behind it.

---

### Feature 2: Dark Mode Checkbox/Switch — REJECT (superseded by root fix)

Thank you for catching the dark mode visibility issue — it's a real bug and we can confirm checkboxes and switches were nearly invisible.

After investigating, we traced this to a deeper root cause: all color definitions in our @theme block use hex format (#xxxxxx), and Tailwind v4 cannot apply opacity modifiers (/50, /10, etc.) to hex values. This silently breaks 161 alpha modifier usages across 40+ component files — not just the checkbox and switch.

Rather than patching individual components with CSS attribute selectors, we're converting all @theme color definitions from hex to oklch() format. This is a ~30-line change in index.css that fixes every broken alpha modifier in the entire frontend, including the checkbox/switch visibility. The existing Tailwind classes on the components were correct all along — they just needed a color format that supports alpha channels.

Additionally, we did a full standardization pass on all checkbox usage across the codebase — consistent \<label\> wrapper pattern, unified onCheckedChange handler, cursor-pointer everywhere, solid border (no alpha) for unchecked state visibility.

Your diagnosis of the symptom was accurate and led us to a much better fix. We won't merge the CSS/component changes from this feature since the oklch conversion supersedes them entirely.

---

### Feature 3: Regex Test Patterns Modal — REJECT (rewriting)

This is an excellent feature idea and clearly addresses a real pain point — writing regex for IPTV stream names is one of the hardest parts of configuring Teamarr, and the 'Test Patterns' placeholder has been there too long. The suggestion engine and live highlighting concepts are exactly right.

We're going to rewrite this to integrate more deeply with the existing form:

1. **Mirror the actual form fields** — the modal will have the exact same regex fields as EventGroupForm (skip_builtin, include/exclude, teams, date, time, league extraction) rather than a separate pattern UI. This way users learn the real fields while testing.

2. **Interactive pattern generation** — beyond suggestions, users will be able to click/highlight text in a stream name and tell the system 'this is team1', 'this is the date', etc. The regex generates automatically with proper Python named groups.

3. **Proper decomposition** — the 1160-line monolith will be split into focused modules: regex utilities, pattern generator, stream list (virtualized for large groups), pattern panel, interactive selector, and the modal shell.

4. **Performance** — virtualized stream list via @tanstack/react-virtual for groups with 10,000+ streams, memoized regex compilation, proper React keys and abort controllers.

Several of your implementation ideas — the Python-to-JS regex conversion, the color-coded highlighting, the live testing approach — will carry forward into the rewrite. Thank you for the contribution and the inspiration for this feature.

title: Convert @theme hex colors to oklch for Tailwind v4 alpha support
description:
## Problem

All 161 Tailwind alpha modifier usages (e.g. bg-primary/50, border-muted-foreground/50) across 40+ frontend files are silently broken because @theme in index.css defines colors as hex (#xxxxxx). Tailwind v4 cannot apply /N opacity modifiers to hex values. This is the root cause of the checkbox/switch dark mode invisibility reported in PR #118.

## Research Completed

- Audited all alpha modifier usage: 161 occurrences, 47 unique patterns, 40+ files
- Most common: bg-muted/50 (34x), bg-muted/30 (23x), bg-secondary/50 (16x), border-primary/50 (12x), bg-primary/10 (12x)
- Found 1 existing rgba() workaround in index.css:266 confirming prior awareness
- Confirmed @theme defines 14 dark colors + 12 light overrides, all hex
- No tailwind.config.ts — project uses CSS-first @theme approach (Tailwind v4.1.18)

## Implementation Steps

1. Convert all @theme color definitions from hex to oklch() format (14 dark + 12 light = ~26 values)
2. Convert .light override colors from hex to oklch() (12 values)
3. Remove the manual rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.1) workaround at index.css:266 — replace with var(--color-primary) reference or Tailwind class
4. Verify checkbox.tsx and switch.tsx existing Tailwind classes are correct as-is (they were always correct, just the color format prevented alpha from working)
5. Run frontend build to confirm no regressions
6. Spot-check a few key components visually if possible

## Files Modified

- frontend/src/index.css — @theme block and .light overrides (sole change)
- frontend/src/index.css:266 — remove rgba() workaround

## PR #118 Feature 2 Response

Reject the CSS attribute selector approach. The checkbox/switch visibility fix is a symptom of the hex color format issue. Converting to oklch fixes all 161 broken spots including checkbox/switch, with zero component changes needed.

## Verification

- npm run build (frontend compiles)
- All existing Tailwind alpha classes now resolve correctly
- Checkbox and switch visible in dark mode
- No visual regressions in light mode

title: Checkpoint FK state corrupted by _migrate_channel_group_mode_to_patterns call
Add seconds support to TIME_PATTERNS

title: Normalize em/en dashes before datetime extraction

title: Clean up empty parentheses after datetime mask removal

title: Fix city translation overwriting team names
description: CITY_TRANSLATIONS converts 'Napoli' to 'naples', 'Roma' to 'rome'. These are team names (SSC Napoli, AS Roma). Remove team-conflicting entries or add exclusion logic. File: teamarr/utilities/constants.py + teamarr/consumers/matching/normalizer.py

title: Recognize provider prefix patterns in pipe handler
description: Pipe handler in _clean_team_name() doesn't recognize 'US (Paramount 010)' as noise. It checks league/sport hints which fail. Add pattern to detect regional provider prefixes like 'XX (Provider NNN)'. File: teamarr/consumers/matching/classifier.py

title: Change league hint patterns from ^ to \b anchoring
description: 31 of 56 LEAGUE_HINT_PATTERNS use ^ (start anchor), failing for mid-string league names like 'US (Paramount 010) | UEFA Champions League: ...'. Sport hints already use \b everywhere. Change all ^ to \b. Add missing 'UEFA Champions League' pattern. File: teamarr/utilities/constants.py

title: Stream matching extraction improvements
Refactor: hoist DetectionLibrary AliasSection/KeywordSection out of parent
description: Follow-up to txh4. AliasSection/KeywordSection are inline-defined components inside DetectionLibrary, so every parent re-render remounts them (resetting state, re-running KeywordSection's useDetectionKeywords query each render). persistKey masked the collapse symptom, but the remount/refetch churn remains. Hoist them to module-scope components with explicit props (they currently close over ~15 pieces of parent state + handlers). Eliminates remounts + wasted refetches. Medium effort; verify no behavior change.

title: Docs: Homepage Custom API widget config + setup
description: Document how to add Teamarr to a gethomepage/homepage dashboard: a copy-paste services.yaml Custom API widget snippet (url -> /stats/homepage, mappings -> label/field per KPI), auth note (API base URL), and a screenshot/example. New page under docs/guide/ (or a section), linked from installation/integrations.

title: Backend: /stats/homepage flat KPI endpoint
description: Add a read-only endpoint (e.g. GET /stats/homepage) returning the flat KPI payload from the anchor, composed from existing stats sources (/stats, /stats/dashboard, /stats/live, match-rate). No new computation where existing services cover it — assemble + flatten. Stable field names (Homepage widget config references them). Layer-clean: route delegates to services. Route docstring + OpenAPI.

title: Define the KPI set + widget JSON shape (anchor)
description: Decide the at-a-glance metrics to surface on a Homepage widget and the flat JSON shape Homepage's Custom API widget maps to fields. Candidates: managed channels (active), last generation (status + relative time), match %, programmes generated (today/total), next scheduled run, Dispatcharr connection state. Homepage Custom API maps fields by JSON path, so keep it flat/predictable. Output: field list + sample JSON the endpoint will return. Blocks the rest.

title: Homepage dashboard widget — surface Teamarr stats via API
description: Make Teamarr's key stats/metrics surfaceable on a gethomepage/homepage dashboard (the user runs gethomepage/homepage:latest on :3000). Homepage's Custom API widget maps a REST JSON response to displayed fields, so the deliverable is a stable, flat KPI endpoint + a documented widget config. Teamarr already exposes /stats, /stats/dashboard, /stats/live — but those are shaped for the Teamarr UI, not for a single-glance external widget. Goal: a purpose-built endpoint returning the handful of at-a-glance KPIs (managed channels, last generation status/time, match %, programmes generated, next scheduled run, Dispatcharr connection) in a flat shape the Custom API widget can render, plus copy-paste Homepage YAML. Stretch: a native gethomepage widget contributed upstream.
notes: STARTING POINT: a primitive/non-functional homepage widget existed in a previous version — recoverable from git commit 231ca2f 'Add Homepage dashboard integration to README' (since removed from current README). Pull that config as a reference for the Custom API widget YAML (hjr2.3).

title: Template var name collision: 'matchup' (combat MMA variant shadowed by identity)
description: Two variables register name='matchup': combat.py:101 (MMA-specific 'Fighter1 vs Fighter2', guards sport=='mma') and identity.py:151 (generic '{away} @ {home}'). registry.register() silently overwrites (self._variables[name]=...), and __init__.py imports combat (line 9) BEFORE identity (line 12), so identity's generic matchup wins and combat's MMA variant is DEAD. Impact LOW (identity's works for MMA too, just '@' vs 'vs' + order), but it's a silent shadow + dead code. Surfaced by the yk4j.8 scope audit. Fix options: (a) make identity matchup sport-aware (use 'vs'/fighter order for mma), or (b) register combat's under a distinct name (note: renamed var -> docs table per CLAUDE.md). Also consider making registry.register() warn/raise on duplicate names to catch future collisions.

title: Feasibility write-up + go/no-go recommendation
description: Synthesize the mapping, architecture, Gracenote, and spike findings into a recommendation: is an NL MCP worth building, at what scope (which intents first), with what risks (safety of agent-driven mutations, auth, maintenance). If go: produce the implementation epic.

title: Spike: prototype one end-to-end NL flow
description: Throwaway proof-of-concept wiring ONE intent (e.g. 'subscribe to NBA') from an MCP tool call through to a real Teamarr API mutation, to surface unknowns (auth, error handling, idempotency, confirmation UX). Time-boxed; informs the recommendation. Do not productionize.

title: Gracenote-formatting feasibility for EPG output
description: Define what 'as close as possible to Gracenote formatting' means for our XMLTV output and assess the gap. Inventory what we already have (the {gracenote_category} variable + how it's populated), compare against Gracenote/XMLTV conventions (categories, episode-num, icons, credits, etc.), and list what'd be needed to approximate it. Feeds both this epic and the template work (yk4j).

title: MCP architecture options (server, auth, deployment)
description: Evaluate how an MCP server would be built and run: Python MCP SDK / FastMCP wrapping the local REST API; how it authenticates to Teamarr (same-process vs HTTP+key, ties to kd5a API-key work); whether it ships in-container alongside app.py or as a sidecar; tool granularity (one tool per intent vs thin REST passthrough + LLM planning). Recommend an approach.

title: Map NL intents -> existing API surface (coverage & gaps)
description: For the three example intents (subscribe to a league, reorder/prioritize a team's games to the top, produce Gracenote-style EPG) plus the obvious adjacent ones, map each to the concrete existing endpoint(s) in teamarr/api/routes/. Identify what's already callable vs what gaps an MCP would need (e.g. is there a clean 'set team sort priority' endpoint? sort_priorities.py). Produce an intent->endpoint table + gap list.

title: Feasibility: natural-language MCP for Teamarr
description: Investigate (feasibility/spike, not commit-to-build) an MCP server exposing Teamarr to natural-language agents, so a user can say things like 'subscribe to NBA', 'bring Pistons games to the top', and 'make the EPG as close as possible to Gracenote formatting' and have it executed. The REST surface to wrap already exists: subscription.py, leagues.py, teams.py, sort_priorities.py (stream ordering), templates.py, epg.py, groups.py, channels.py. The example intents map cleanly: subscribe->subscription/leagues; reorder->sort_priorities; Gracenote EPG->templates+epg (note: a {gracenote_category} template variable ALREADY exists). Output of this epic is a go/no-go recommendation with scope, architecture, and risks — NOT a shipped server.

title: Dashboard status strip: live progress during active generation

During an active generation run, the dashboard info/status strip stats (Last Generated, Managed Channels, Match Rate) should reflect that work is in-flight — e.g. a spinning/indeterminate progress indicator or live-updating values instead of showing the last static run's numbers.

Today the strip shows the previous run's snapshot even while a generation is mid-flight, which reads as stale. Want a visual 'this is updating now' cue on those three tiles while GenerationContext reports an active run.

Scope/feasibility note: needs the strip to subscribe to generation state (GenerationContext already tracks active runs) and swap each stat into a loading/animated state; if real-time intermediate values aren't cheaply available, an indeterminate spinner per tile is an acceptable first cut. Requested by user during v2.7.0 IA overhaul.

title: Contribute custom league upstream (one-click submission)
description:
Close the loop: let users push a working custom league back to the main project so battle-tested leagues become built-ins (version-controlled, documented, tier-classified).

WHY: custom leagues live only in the user's DB (is_custom=1). The ones that actually work are exactly the leagues we'd want to ship. A contribution path turns the user base into a league-discovery pipeline and retires the manual request loop entirely.

DESIGN (low-effort, no backend auth/integration):
- A 'Contribute upstream' action on a working custom league (only enabled once it has passed test-fetch / has cached teams — carry that proof).
- Generate the canonical INSERT OR REPLACE INTO leagues (...) VALUES (...) line, exactly matching schema.sql column order, ready to paste.
- AND build a prefilled GitHub new-league issue URL (github.com/Pharaoh-Labs/teamarr/issues/new?template=...&title=...&body=...) with league details + the SQL + the test-fetch sample-event proof (count + a couple of event names) auto-filled into the existing new-league issue template. No GitHub token needed — pure URL prefill.
- Maintainer side: the issue arrives with validated data + ready SQL, so adoption is a copy-paste into schema.sql (+ tier classification + docs).

OUT OF SCOPE: direct PR creation, auto-accepting writes to our repo, any server-to-server GitHub integration. Keep it URL-prefill only.

DEPENDS ON: eqz.3 (test-fetch proof) and eqz.5 (UI surface).

title: Docs + schema for custom leagues
description:
Per CLAUDE.md Documentation Updates table (new feature + new column + provider behavior).

Deliverables:
- docs/reference/providers/tsdb.md (or supported-leagues.md): how to add a custom TSDB league via UI, premium-key requirement, finding idLeague/strLeague, the test-fetch step.
- Note the is_custom column and that custom leagues live in the DB only (not schema.sql / not version-controlled).
- README features section + appropriate docs/guide page.
- Clarify league-count claims wording so custom leagues aren't conflated with built-in counts.

title: Auto team-cache refresh on custom-league create
description:
Adding a league does not auto-populate teams (cached_team_count=0, last_cache_refresh=NULL until a refresh runs).

Deliverables:
- On successful custom-league create, trigger a scoped team-cache refresh for just that league (reuse consumers/cache/refresh.py path) so teams populate without waiting for the weekly schedule or a full manual refresh.
- Update cached_team_count / last_cache_refresh on the new row.
- Handle/report failures gracefully (league still created; surface 'teams not yet cached' state in UI).
close_reason: Auto team-cache refresh on custom-league create. Verification caught the core bug: create committed the row but never reloaded the in-memory league mapping, so the TSDB provider couldn't resolve the new code and cached 0 teams while reporting success. Fix: reload mapping after create+commit, before refresh; plus supports_league guard so an unresolvable league reports success=false not a silent zero. Verified live: custom league -> 16 Superettan teams cached. Filed eqz.9 for delete-orphan asymmetry.

title: Logging: per-group EPG summaries rotate out of teamarr.log during DEBUG gens
description:
Observed while debugging EPG matching (epic crs): during a full generation at DEBUG level the main log rotates at 10MB (RotatingFileHandler, 10MB x5 in teamarr/utilities/logging.py), so early-processed groups' [EPG-RESOLVE]/[EPG-MATCH] group=/[XTREAM-EPG] summary lines get pushed into teamarr.log.1 before the run finishes — had to grep the rotated file to see all groups in one run. Per-group resolve/index/fallback summaries are the key observability signal and should survive a whole run.

Options (pick during impl):
1. Bump main-log rotation size (e.g. 25-50MB) and/or backup count.
2. Emit a compact per-run EPG-matching summary block at the end of generation (one line per group: streams, resolved channel/direct/name, xtream-filled, indexed, matched) so the whole picture is in one place regardless of rotation.
3. Optional dedicated rotating log for EPG-matching INFO lines.
Lightweight; nice-to-have for diagnosing multi-group EPG runs.

title: NCAA team selection by conference (ESPN-only)

Scope: surface ESPN conference grouping in the team picker for NCAA leagues; let users select/add a whole conference at once. Closes GH #91.
notes: AUDIT 2026-06-14: VALID/keep (P3). ESPN provider DOES parse conference/division onto teams (espn/provider.py:_parse_groups), but there is no select-teams-by-conference flow in subscriptions/UI. Feature unbuilt; data foundation exists.

title: Implement channel name suffix for separate mode
description:
When global_consolidation_mode = 'separate', add an optional suffix to channel names to distinguish streams from different sources.

Options to expose in Settings:
- Append event group name (e.g. 'Lakers vs Celtics — NBA Group A')
- Append M3U account/provider name (e.g. 'Lakers vs Celtics — ESPN+')
- Append both
- Off (current behavior)

Implementation:
1. New setting: channel_separate_mode_suffix (none/group/provider/both)
2. In channel naming logic, detect non-consolidate mode and append suffix
3. UI toggle in Settings > Channel Management

GH: #108

title: Append provider/group name to channel name in non-consolidate mode
description:
When channel consolidation mode is set to 'separate' (multiple streams for the same event get individual channels), optionally append the event group name or M3U provider name to the channel name to make streams distinguishable.

Example: 'Lakers vs Celtics' becomes 'Lakers vs Celtics — ESPN+' and 'Lakers vs Celtics — Paramount+'.

This addresses the core usability problem of identical channel names for the same event from different sources, without requiring a full global sort reordering implementation.

GH: #108
notes: AUDIT 2026-06-14: VALID/keep. Distinct from feed-separation (4uy, appends HOME/AWAY) and UFC segments. In 'separate' mode each stream gets its own channel but with the SAME name — no provider/group disambiguation suffix exists in naming logic (only segment/keyword/feed_team suffixes). GH #108.

title: Implement Priority Teams channel ordering

2. API: GET /channel-sort-priorities/teams + POST/DELETE endpoints
3. Frontend: TeamPicker section in SortPriorityManager in Settings, below the sport/league priority list
4. Sort: in event_group_processor, query priority teams and use as top sort key: (not is_priority_team, sport, league, time)

GH: #144

New channel_priority_teams table + priority_teams.py DB layer; /sort-priorities/teams CRUD (ordered before /{sport} routes to avoid shadowing); get_all_channels_sorted prepends a priority tier matched by (sport, home/away team_name). Frontend reuses TeamPicker inside SortPriorityManager (saved list = picker selection, diff add/remove). 8 tests incl. sort behavior + sport-scoping. Docs updated.

title: PR #204: Distinguish auth failures from no-data in Supabase REST client

title: Add LEAGUES_WITHOUT_TEAMS guard to get_team()
description:
**Problem**: `get_league_teams()` has guard for combat sports but `get_team()` does not.

**Effect**: Individual team lookups for UFC/boxing still hit ESPN API and get 404s.

**Fix**: Add early return in `get_team()` similar to `get_league_teams()`:
```python
if league in self.LEAGUES_WITHOUT_TEAMS:
    return None
```

**Location**: `teamarr/providers/espn/provider.py` - `get_team()` method (line ~270)

title: Suppress ESPN 404 spam for UFC teams endpoint
description:
**Problem**: Logs show repeated HTTP 404 warnings for https://site.api.espn.com/apis/site/v2/sports/mma/ufc/teams/ - ESPN doesn't have a teams endpoint for UFC since MMA fighters aren't teams.

**Root Cause**:
- Cache refresh (cache/refresh.py:267) calls get_league_teams() for ALL leagues including UFC
- ESPN provider doesn't check if league is UFC before calling teams endpoint
- LEAGUES_WITHOUT_SUMMARY exists but no equivalent for teams

**Fix Needed**:
1. Add LEAGUES_WITHOUT_TEAMS = {'ufc'} to ESPN provider
2. Skip teams API call for UFC in get_league_teams()
3. Or skip at cache refresh level for leagues with event_type='event_card'

**Files**:
- teamarr/providers/espn/provider.py (get_league_teams, add skip for UFC)
- teamarr/consumers/cache/refresh.py (alternative: skip teams for event_card leagues)

title: Stretch: native gethomepage widget (upstream contribution)

title: PR #204: Persist Supabase credential cache to disk
description: SupabaseLeagueClient uses an in-memory TTLCache for extracted credentials and logo maps, so every process restart re-scrapes the league website even though the cache TTL is 7 days. Not a correctness issue, but the docstring promises 7-day caching across the process boundary. Persist to disk (or to the existing project cache layer) so cold starts are fast and we don't hammer league sites on container restart.
notes: AUDIT 2026-06-14: VALID/keep (P4 backlog OK). client uses in-memory TTLCache only (self._cache = TTLCache(), client.py:85); no disk persistence. Cred cache is lost on restart as described.

Optional right-side actions slot (rendered as SIBLINGS of the toggle button, never nested — avoids invalid nested-button HTML, cf 5hq.16). Optional persisted collapse via persistKey (usePersistentCollapse, localStorage key teamarr.collapse.<key>; key now optional). Do NOT use it for dropdown/picker chevrons (Select, filter-select, LeaguePicker, TeamPicker, VariablePicker, checkbox-list-picker, hierarchical-sortable) — those mean 'open a menu'. Dispatcharr GET /api/epg/programs/search/ (epic 183 EPG matching) live-validated 2026-06-01. DRF-paginated count/next/previous/results; params start_before,end_after,tvg_id,title(substring),page_size,page. Program object embeds channels[] and streams[] each with id/name/tvg_id/m3u_account -> join key tvg_id (raw /api/channels/streams/ has EMPTY tvg_id; only EPG-mapped streams carry one, surfaced via program payload). Clean ISO8601-Z back-to-back start/end usable as attach/detach windows. epg_source field -> exclude our own '_Teamarr'. Busy linear channels 30-66 programs/day; multiplicity gate (LINEAR>1 vs DEDICATED=1) works. No-EPG=HTTP200 count0. CRITICAL: naive at/vs title regex ~100% false positives (news/movies); matcher must route titles through existing classify_stream->TeamMatcher. Findings: plans/spike-183.1-epg-programs-findings.md, repro: scripts/spike_epg_programs*.py

CBL Supabase score-merge (client.py build_score_map/_merge_scores, fix hh7 commit 5cc0e34): join completed games to schedule entries on game_number, NOT parsed city names. games.schedule_game_number (int) == schedule_game_overrides.game_number (int). Live probe: every CURRENT-season completed game has it (100%); 390 legacy rows have season_year=None and no number (real code filters season_year=eq.year so they never appear). Name key (date, home_firstword) was fragile: 'Chatham-Kent Barnstormers' firstword 'chatham-kent' != schedule override 'chatham' -> silent score miss; also collided on doubleheaders. Kept (date,home,away) name key as fallback only. To inspect live schema: init ProviderRegistry from get_db, SupabaseLeagueClient(mapping_source=registry.get_league_mapping_source()), get_league_config('cbl'), _supabase_get(url,key,'games?select=*&limit=3').

CRITICAL workflow constraint (maintainer, 2026-06-12): origin/dev is an early-adopter RELEASE CHANNEL — real users pull dev every time they see an update. Do NOT push half-finished/WIP/broken work to dev. This OVERRIDES CLAUDE.md's default 'push to dev after every change' for multi-step features. For large multi-bead epics (e.g. vhbo IA overhaul), work on a FEATURE BRANCH and only merge to dev when the change is coherent and 'somewhat usable' end-to-end. Small self-contained fixes/features that leave dev usable can still go straight to dev. When unsure whether something is usable enough, ask before pushing to dev.

Epic 183 EPG matching is BROKEN by a tvg_id namespace mismatch (bead teamarrv2-6z1, P0). Dispatcharr /api/epg/programs/search/?tvg_id= filters on program.tvg_id = the EPG-SOURCE numeric id (e.g. '68344'). But a program payload carries THREE tvg_id namespaces: program.tvg_id (numeric '68344'), channels[].tvg_id (gracenote-ish 'CSPANHD'), streams[].tvg_id (the M3U stream id 'CSPAN.us'). Teamarr candidate M3U streams carry the 'CSPAN.us' namespace. EPGProgramIndex.build feeds that to search_programs(tvg_id=...), which filters program.tvg_id -> always 0. Proven live on Dispatcharr 0.24.0: search(tvg_id='CSPAN.us')=0 vs search(tvg_id='68344')=203. Channels expose epg_data_id (numeric, matches program.tvg_id namespace) but candidate linear streams aren't on channels at match time. Also fixed a separate P1 crash (teamarrv2-npz): _build_epg_index used naive datetime into to_utc().

Match stats split into two metrics (bead teamarrv2-nnj, dev 3f44fc6): EPG/TEAM_ONLY matching fans ONE source stream out to MANY matched results, so counting matched RESULTS over eligible STREAMS pushed match rate >100%. BatchMatchResult.matched_count = result/VOLUME count (can exceed stream count). matched_stream_count / unmatched_stream_count = distinct-stream COVERAGE (true 0-100%), via {r.stream_id for r in results if r.matched}. event_group_processor wires coverage into streams_matched/matched_count and volume into match_result_count (new event_epg_groups column) + run extra_metrics['match_results']. Frontend EventGroups.tsx bar = coverage %, tooltip = matches produced. GOTCHA: any new matched/total rate must use stream counts not result counts.
